This beautiful and incredible Church, better known as Pantheon of Agrippa, a Roman temple of the 1st century consecrated as a church in the 7th century, is dedicated to the Blessed Virgin Mary and all the martyrs of the city of Rome.Upon entering you will find a large circular space dominated by a 43 meter diameter dome that covers the entire room. From the floor to the center of the dome is the same height, it is really amazing...

The Pantheon is one of the oldest churches in Rome, and the building dates back to ancient Roman times. A trip to the Pantheon doesn’t take much time. It is basically like one circular room. You can almost stand in the center, turn around slowly, and see everything that there is to see.It is not very big, so it can become very crowded inside, but its impressive architecture and significance to history make it a must-see. The dome provided the model for the construction of the Duomo in Florence, which was the model for the construction of the dome of St. Peter’s. I was captivated by the oculus at the top of the dome, which is open to the sky. Look for the drainage holes in the floor immediately beneath. It is strange to feel like you are both inside a building and outside all at once. Definitely worth jostling a few elbows. Because it is so thick with tourists, and because many people are looking up or looking around, it is an ideal spot for pick-pockets. I wore my money belt and clutched my handbag tightly across my front, and I tried to maintain awareness of my surroundings, and I didn’t have any issues.

I've heard of this wonder of the ancient world, but until you're in there and you see it with your eyes, you don't realize the achievement they made almost 1900 years ago, and it's still standing. That dome at 44 meters high that only the walls support, there are no buttresses, nothing... but the walls are different materials. He had it built by Agrippa but it was finished by Adriano and opened in 126 AD (almost 1900 years ago and still standing). Buried here are Victor Manuel II (first King of Italy), his son Humberto of Savoy and his wife Margaret Theresa of Savoy (the one with Margaret pizza). Also buried is the famous painter and architect Rafael Sanzio. You have to take your caps off when you come in and there are security measures like everywhere else. If I’m not mistaken the bottom is up to 6 meters thick and is a hard marble. On top of that there is a part of another type of marble the travertine (remember the fountains of travertine, white marble, from Pamukkale in Turkey), the dome is volcanic Tuff and the part surrounding the hole on top is pumice....You look back and forth and you'll see the little people...it feels like the man gets small and who here was a God (pantheion...means "all gods"). Undoubtedly a work of art, an incredible piece of engineering,...spectacular

The Pantheon was originally built by Marcus Agrippa in 27BC, but was burnt down a few times until Hadrian re-erected the current structure in 120AD. Hadrian kindly left the original dedication to Agrippa above the entrance. Hadrian was less kind to his architect, Apollodorus of Damascus. After a disagreement over the design, Hadrian had Apollodorus executed! But Apollodorus's design has stood the test of time.  For 1300 years, the Pantheon had the largest dome in the world. Today it still has the largest unsupported dome in the world. It's bigger than the dome in the US Capitol (142 ft compared to 96 ft) and the distance from the floor to the top of the dome is exactly twice its diameter. The sixteen massive Corinthian Columns at the front each weighs 60 tons and came all the way from Egypt. Overall, this temple to all the gods, which survived from ancient times only because it was converted into a Christian church in 609AD, remauns one of the most impressive Ancient Roman structures in modern Rome.
Beautiful. We were there when it was raining so we experienced the rain coming in thru the open ocular. Plan on spending about an hour.

However, we enjoyed the time inside and there are some information about the building  and the catacombs which are there. The best part was that we been around 12pm and the sun rise was showing the door way which is like a memorable moment  for us .

I would have been in this brilliant building more than a dozen times in multiple visits to Rome and without doubt it is my favourite building in the world. It's fascinating to think what it must have been like to enter and worship here during the Empire, and before it became a consecrated church in the 7th century. Some visitors are no doubt attracted to the christian elements in the building and will happily seek out Raphael's tomb and that of the Kings of Italy buried here, but for me the delight is in the engineering. Yes the architecture is interesting too -there's lot's of theories to explain some odd elements in its design - but it's the engineering and construction of what is still the largest unreinforced concrete dome in the world which is pure genius. The 4,500 tonne dome, the exemplar for many similar domes from the renaissance on, is constructed on a system whereby the higher the dome goes the lighter the building materials utilised are; so travertine at the bottom, then terracotta, and pumice and tufa at the top. What magician came up with this? And how brave was the Emperor Hadrian (in particular) to trust that magician? And here almost 2,000 years later it still stands with its amazing oculus letting in natural light (and indeed rain, as we were once fortunate enough to experience on a previous visit). There seems to be more and more people accessing the Pantheon every time we visit and this time there seemed to be a steady progression of large tour groups, but you'll just have to put with that. So once you've admired the majestic Corinthian pillars and the portico go inside and raise your eyes in awe to the astonishing dome. It truly is one a kind .
This is a place worth a visit when in the city, but do recommend the audio guide to give the building and artefacts  meaning .

The Panthéon, one of the most important busiest sights of historic center of Rome, a most complete very ancient structure and architecture in the city ; it's the most impressive temple of Marcus Agrippa; there are an inscription, the temple was built by  emperor Hadrian around 27 BC and finished around year 125; The high is about 43 or 45 m and has a round shaft in the middle and the sunlight can shine into the temple and illuminate the wonderful interior with so much marble and tombs of many celebrite persons as Raphael, also the last king Emmanuel II; and more; the story said, the rain can not enter by the large shaft, but when it rains a lot it's not true, I saw it was sometimes wet in the middle under the shaft: Nowaday the temple is transformed in a church, but it's like a museum to visit. and a mass are not so often; there are so many people for a visit, the entrance was free, and since I was last time there, two or three years ago,  now you must pay for entrance. The lavish marble, granite and stone pillars, altars and flooring are something that truly need to be seen to be believed. Expect to spend about 10 minutes inside, making this a very quick attraction to visit. The fact that admission is free is an added bonus.

The Pantheon was an experience.  I think the reason I am so excited about the Pantheon was because our fantastic tour guide.  We signed up for a tour guide (in English) when we booked our tickets.  She was so enthusiastic that we became even more excited about being at the Pantheon.  Take your time when you are there, either before or after you have been given information from your guide. Don't go with a group tour - do it on your own.

We worked this into a free walking day where we took in the Pantheon, Trevi Fountain, Piazza Navona and the Spanish Steps. Just awe inspiring stuff. Make sure you have closed in shoes on for entry or security might turn you away.
I loved the Pantheon. It was probably my favorite site to visit in Rome--the fact that it's free to enter also doesn't hurt. Just being inside gave me such a wonderful peaceful feeling.

Fascinating to think that thousands of people entered here ...whether for the gods of the time , the solar clock or just to admire the construction of this masterpiece... I enter it for the third time ( 1981-1997-2019 ) and I see the same solemn side of a building that has passed through time...
There is a line at the entrance which is very quick. It really worth a visit for the construction. You don't need more than 30 minutes to visit it
Wonderful to visit and see more of Rome's history. Everywhere in the city there is so much to see and experience!
Awesome but way too busy. Every time I return it’s more full of people. Too big a crowd makes it less holy and less memorable.But the architecture is unrivaled. Even to this day.
The Pantheon was beautiful but it's free and you shouldn't have to pay an excursion. Felt cheated out of $12.

The Pantheon is easily my favourite structure in Rome. Beautifully designed, an engineering masterpiece and it has such beautiful energy. Take the time to sit and look around and up to the marvellous dome.

It's a beautiful structure that has amazing paintings and sculptures and really shows how seriously the Romans took worshiping their Gods. Along with being a beautiful structure, you can clearly see how some parts of the facility have been modified for church worshipers.On this note, be sure to wear appropriate clothing and no food or drink, because it's considered a religious site. The rules for this aren't posted until the entrance of the building, which makes it impossible to see if you're in the crowd. The rude line attendants don't care and just assume you magically know.It's also a crush of people, so be prepared to wait in line. It's free, which makes it extra crowded.Overall, it's a site worth seeing, just be prepared to put up with a lot of people crowding.

It's amazing and great human's made architecture. It is incredible inside with interesting things to see such as 1st and 2nd King of Rome. Pantheon is bordered with lots of restaurants, ice-cream and coffee shops.
The place is well preserved till date. Very popular place to visit but we found it to be very small inside compared to the other sites and attractions.

You arrive at the Pantheon from a choice of several narrow side streets that lead into a small square with some cafes on the edge with a fountain in the middle and the Pantheon taking centre stage. I took an intake of breath when i seen it, surreal to think this structure has been standing for 2000 years. Lots of people around but easy to enter and exit due to seperate lines and the size of the interior allows many people in at a time.I spent about 20 minutes gazing at the ceiling, incredible feat of engineering. Some lovely sculptures and a simple but endearing alter surrond the internal walls. Overall spent about half an hour inside and half an hour outside staring at the damn thing!Loved it, you dont need a guide for this, easily done on your own.
Went just after a downpour free to go in and have alook around mind you don't slip on the wet floor as no glass Inthe roof
Free entry means lot of people, but in our case no waiting in queues. Still worth seeing as one of pillar of Roman history.
Beautiful.... when you enter you seem transported to the past. Roll your eyes and see the sky! Must-see attractions

there wasn't a queue and the building is a must see. Raphael's grave is there and the architecture of the building is quite breathtaking. Next to it there is a must see church with three amazing Caravaggio paintings on the left front as you walk in

The pantheon is a must stop when in Rome but choose when you go carefully. The piazza where it is situated is very busy and disguises the length of the queue to get into the pantheon. After realising the length of the queue, we decided to go back early morning about 10am and we were allowed entry straight away. I would defiantly recommend going earlier on in the day. The pantheon is also not lit up at night during the summer months.

This building is a remarkable architectural work that has withstood time. The outside is beautiful but the inside is magnificent. It is very difficult to photograph due to it enormity. Well worth seeing

We were astounded at the Pantheon, the building both outside and inside, and the ease at which we were able to gain admittance and the ease in moving around.  The time of day and season no doubt made it easier.  The history, the art, and the building left us in awe.  Go and visit this site.

I was here in here September and the line look crazy it was extremely long and all around where is located the place is the heart of a very busy area in Rome where there’s a lot of tourists. The next day I gave it a try because someone told us that the line wasn’t crazy but it still was but it was moving fast . Inside the place is very detailed and the Floors . No picture were allowed to be taken or videos as they consider it a sacred place . I would definitely come back again . The place was free also ...
we had been before but were walking past early morning and went in. It really is an amazing place and structure. Well worth a visit but better without too many people so go early.
Lovely unique Church. Built with opening in roof. Eeally nice place to visit.  Very short walk from Piazza Navona.
amazing building, google certain times for free admission and great architecture. worth the walk again so much to do around it also

This is another must see site in Rome - and was the largest dome in the world for over a thousand years.Free admission, and if you go a bit earlier or later in the day, despite still being busy, you can still easily get in and it doesn’t feel too busy.Don’t worry about “reserving tickets” upfront as tripadvisor seems to be pushing - just walk in for free, and you can hire an audio guide for a 2 euros.

First point as a place of worship, cover your shoulders.There is an audio point in the Pantheon however I downloaded an online 30 min audio tour which was free and very good.Lots of interesting points, mausoleums, the construction of the roof and drainage to be considered  - it can be crowded but not as bad as the Vatican.
The audio guide reveals interesting details. The most impressive thing is the architecture of the place, admission is free. The audio guide is actually quite cheap, but nothing special.

Interesting to think of how this was built so many years ago when they didn’t have the kinds of tools we have today. Lots of good information but two euros to listen to an audio video commentary. lots of people there but still able to see everything easily.
This church was worth seeing for as a feat of ancient engineering. As a church it was odd because of the prior building as a temple. Interesting enough that I went back  for second look.

On and off for years my wife and I have had a glass or two of wine on the steps of the fountain facing the pantheon. We used to "rat" watch  (I know)..  a silly competition between us both.... But this has now had to change.   The city has quite rightly decided to clamp down on drinking on or near the monuments... Especially since some of the visitors have not been respecting the sites. Since June new civil laws ban drinking in public places in Rome after 10pm (not in restaurants) and no food or drink on whatever is considered to be a monument....  they are very hot on this so beware...
There was a line, but it went pretty fast and we were able to walk around inside and sit. Free to get it, and so beautiful to see how large it is.
Try to visit the Pantheon in the early hours for the best experience and avoid the crowds. The light is much more spectacular during the early morning to enjoy the dome.
Too long to queue but lovely square to sit in with a fountain in the middle. Very arty area but watch out for rip offs

The temple, which was later converted into a church is magnificent. It is free to enter, and with Rome being such a tourist destination, it was very busy, but well worth it. Almost two thousand years after it was built, the Pantheon's dome is still the world's largest unreinforced concrete dome, which is impressive. Also, Raphael is buried in the pantheon, so it is a must to go and pay respects to the great painter. I also enjoyed sitting on the square in front of the Pantheon and to reflect on the history and magnificence of the structure
there is so much beauty and history in the area you need weeks to see it all. we did a lot of sightseeing and we need to return to see the rest. amazingly stunning!
Gorgeous building with easy access and plenty of restaurants and things to do around. Free access which was good! would be nice if they had a charitable box for donations
A True Masterpiece! Once you learn the history of this architecture, you will be amazed. Free to enter!!

From the inside, the dome is shockingly big. And the oculus is a surprise, its 8m wide at the top the dome letting light and rain. Its free to get in and very quick. Suggest you download Rick Steves audioguide, its knowledgeable and fun.

Pantheon,the most old structure im Rome that has survived and rebuild a lot if wars is a "Must See". The square in front of it one of the most important for turists. Around this place there are some bars and restairants with in and out sitting that offers best food and a friendly staff

The building itself was lovely but we were disappointed as we had booked and paid for through trip advisor for audio tour, but when we got there no one at the desk and no one had a clue where anyone was. We were in there for quite a while -  we went on Sunday so there was prayer going on as well as it being very busy. By the time we left, others was waiting at the desk but we decided we had seen what we came for so we left as still no one there. Gutted we didn't have more info tho (only £4/£5 per ticket) but not the point. Close by there is alot of waterfalls and statues so lots of picture opportunies

My husband and I weren’t sure we would go inside the Pantheon when we were in Rome but the day we walked by it the line was short so we decided “why not”? Boy am I glad we did!It is hard to comprehend how this place was built so many hundreds of years ago without modern technology. The art work and statues are incredible. When you walk in it will take your breath away!!
The Pantheon is a good place to see not on a Sunday. You can’t go in before 12 on a Sunday and it is also very busy that day.Looking around the Pantheon will take around 15 minutes
Amazing, so much history attached to this structure. Must see when in Rome, must not be missed highly recommended.
Walked from the bridge after St Peters and it was a bit of a trek but worth it when we got there it was very busy but could still take in the splendour of it
Whilst the site gets busy this is a great site, given its age and history, and should not be missed.
The best preserved Roman structure in Rome. A must see for any tourist. Try to do it with a guide. Pay particular attention to the marble and porphyry designs. Decent food in the piazza too.
The art  is incredible. The different Altars and Organ were incredible. the Size of the door was a sight of it's own.
Beautiful. And not much of a line, like most other attractions, the day we visited. Wonderful experience.
We visited here on our first day in Rome - what an introduction!  Despite it being a Saturday and it was fairly crowded, it was enjoyable to visit.  Such amazing architecture!
Such a lovely experience visiting The Pantheon it is spectacular inside but gets very busy. Well worth a visit

Honestly the most impressive building I saw in Rome (and I saw a lot). Big wide queue but goes down so fast and is spacious inside so no issues for pictures and enjoying the space. Loved it. And free.

While the Pantheon is no doubt an amazing piece of historical architecture, it does not come close to the beauty and detail of a number of the Basilica in Rome. The outside of course is rather stunning.

It's no longer a working zoo, but the completeness of such an ancient building makes it one of Rome's must-sees.Don't let your disappointment prevent your visit: you can still see panthers on safari in Africa (leopards), or in national parks of North America (jaguars).
A little hidden gem. Very impressive especially knowing that it is a self supporting dome. Able to spend some time just appreciating the structure once inside
A very beautiful church in Rome. Beautiful art work and easy to get to. I will be back to visit again.
The first site we walked to when we got to Rome and WOW!  Had to go back for a second look before we left.  Must see!

One of the many things you HAVE to see when in Rome.You just can’t figure out how the romans were able to make such a magnificent place without the current technologies.The roof is the jewel of the place.
Impressive piece of architecture! Fantastic frescos on the walls, mosaics on the floor. Huge! You lose the sense of scale while inside.
Free and not too many people, fantastic! We found our visit very rewarding were impressed with the building!
It was very lovely to have a cup of cappucino and  tiramisu while watching the tourists flow through the square
Really worth to spend a time there. Was a little bit crowded but not a big deal. nice build, the roof is spectacular

This is by far my favorite building in all of Rome.  This is the best maintained historic building in Rome.  But in 126 AD, the diameter and height of the building are exactly the same - 43.5 meters.  The 9 meter opening at the top of the dome lights the entire basilica.Raphael and the first president of Italy are also buried in the basilica.Although there is a line, it's free to get into and it's worth the wait.
Very good with audio guide. Interesting and easy to understand explained. Still bearable despite the tori masses.
This is in quite a busy spot but free so worth popping into.  Considering when it was built it is in good condition.

Free to enter, be respectful as it is a church. No bare knees or shoulders. Spend some time looking at the roof and sculptures and marvel that it is tucked away in a small square. Oldest church in Rome, but beautiful.

Perfect Roman palace Amazing place to honor all Roman gods Nice building to watch and wonderTrajanus, the famous Emperor and founder Hadrianus, the emperor who re-built the palace (current shape and form)Epic view, both in and outsideOculus, just go in and look to the skyNostalgic; pure Roman nostalgic
Another Rome gem, interesting building and free access. Worth hiring audio to hear history.  Fairly crowded even in October.
another human made miracle building. outside and inside are quite different and stunning. old people' s art example.

We were very impressed, especially not expecting much, when visit Pantheon.We arrived just before 12:00 on sunny day and were very pleased to see sun eclipse pointed on entrance door at noon - great bonus on top of everything.

The pantheon sits in a square with some nice places to have a quiet lunch. The rotunda and dome construction is an early roman construction or basilica roman courts of justice. The dome is circular with a central oculus admitting light. Side chapels are historically informative. Raphael’s original tomb is located inside. A must while in Rome. Cheers

Another beautiful old structure.  Upon entering your eyes are drawn upward to the key feature of the building, its round bowl-like ceiling, with a circular opening in the middle.  It rained a bit earlier that day so they had the center of the floor roped off.  Amazing architecture.  There was no wait.  Definitely worth checking out if you are in the area.

Beautiful gem that should be a must see on everyone's list. Walking inside you feel the quiet serenity of years gone by.  Alcoves that surround the perimeter are each unique and gorgeous. Take time to read the history of each area before moving on. Before you leave glance up to the middle to take in the filtered light. Don't forget the fountain in the piazza it worth a photo stop as well.

This was kind of short and sweet. One of the shorter tours we took. The audio tour was worth the money learning about how it was built. The hole in the top allows rain to come in but the floor was designed to drain it out of the building underneath the floor.. Impressive engineering. I did not realize the hole at the top of the ceiling was not covered.

This is one of my favorite and one of the most serenely beautiful buildings in all of Rome.  You step inside and its dome is simply lovely with the round hole in the top letting in just enough light.  Statuary fills the various alcoves around the perimeter.  Worth a detour!

We were staying close to the Pantheon but not prepared for the impact of this building in a small piazza. There were long queues on a Sunday afternoon but we returned the next morning at 9am and walked straight in. The oculus in the roof is amazing. There are also tombs of two Italian kings and Raphael.See it also from the outside in the moonlight.

We always visit the Pantheon when we are in Rome. It’s the only original Roman building still standing. The interior inspires awe. Just think, the Romans built this amazing structure without any modern construction equipment, albeit with presumably hundreds of poorly-paid workers/slaves. Visit the inside and be awed. Do not miss it.
The interior was a bit dark. Couldn't take any good pictures. Beautiful carvings and sculptures. Glad I went.
This is a must see if you are touring Rome.  It is the most preserved ancient building and speaks to the power and majesty of the Roman era.
As always in Rome be prepared to queue. I found the building disappointing and in need of a good clean , others may disagree. On the other hand it is a must see monument so judge for yourselves.
nearby there is a small tavern Margarita, near the French Institute that offers lunch and dinner at great prices
Amazing feeling ,best place in RomeAround so many good coffe places ,just check it where is no service fee,and a lot good gelato places
Beautiful and stunning, a must see in Rome! Amazing building and history, took you right back to ancient Rome.
Beautiful and stunning like Rome is ..  I really love to breathe history ... Of course very crowded but still very worth seeing. Brilliant
Stunning Area to visit. Stepped in history and a lovely place to stop and watch the world go by. Can get busy and queue can be long

A must see in Rome - very crowded but the line moves fast. Need to read the information or get an audioguide to understand what you are seeing. would have liked to see it lit up at night - went back after dinner but it was not lit
Not too crowded, beautifully lit at night. Very impressive both from the inside as the outside. A must-see!

We were walking near the Trevi fountain one evening and found this monument in a piazza near Piazza Novella.  Interesting Roman artifact. Bordered by small restaurants it is just a shell but it is nice to see.

Great place to go where it's not as crowded and the architecture is incredible.  Take the time to see how its built. Look on the outside of the building to see the original walls. Incredible work! Look up and see the Oculus. It's also FREE to get it!
No admission ticket is needed. Lots to see and admire. Would take 1-2 hours. No need to tour, tour guide...Entry line could be long. Get there early to avoid long line.
Really had no idea what to expect.  Interesting to see such a well preserved building for Roman times

On a previous trip to Rome, I didn’t get a chance to visit the Pantheon. I decided to make sure to put it on my Rome bucket list this time. I was pleasantly surprised!! The Pantheon was built over 2,000 years ago and looks like it could last another 2,000. The entrance has Pillars from Greece, inside the building is round with an oculus at the top. That is , it has an opening that allows rain into the building. Floors are slanted with holes to allow for drainage. Roman engineering at its finest . There is no fee to get in . This was built for pagan gods of the day. Well worth the visit !!

We turned up at the Pantheon after visiting other famous sites. There was a little queue but less than 5 minutes. Once inside it's quite busy and a guide (we use Rick Steves guides on our phones all the time - download them at the hotel so this doesn't rely on varied internet reception). Take a seat, listen to the guide and marvel at just how amazing this building is. We took about half an hour but you may want to stay a little longer.A must see site on your tour of Rome.

One of the few free attractions to get into in Rome and just amazing! To think it has been in continual use for millennia and was built so long ago! I immediately had to research how they built the dome to better understand what I'd just seen!

I did pay the customary visit here, but there was not much to it.Finding it was a bit of a bother as some locals did not appear to know what it was. Eventually found it with the help of another tourist and I really did not stay there long.
Do not miss the beautiful dome of the pantheon full of frescoes and tombs of the kings of Italy suggestive on winter days when it rains and try to see inside the atrium. Enjoy your vision

There are people who check to make sure you are respectfully dressed before you can enter but it's free and the line moves fast. It's impressive and the dome itself is amazing. It could be hard to see everything inside because of the crowd but we enjoyed it.

This church is so beautiful and full of interesting statues and paintings, I visited on a Sunday and at one point as it’s a working church tourist could leave or stay for Mass but no other tourists were allowed in.Well worth a visit it’s stunning
This emblematic monument is so gigantic in person I love this aerea and take a cocktail at the hotel Minerve to enjoy the view
Top 3 of sites to visit. Brilliant engineering from 2000 Yrs ago and interior amazing  , even without a guide you will be impressed , best to read about or have guide

I believe there are more historic sites pr square meter in Rome than any other European city, and many are located within walking distance of eachother.  Pantheon - "the temple of all the gods", is one of the best preserved buildings of all Ancient Roman buildings.  It was build around 120 AD by Hadrian (the guy who built the wall in Britannia in 122 AD), or maybe as early as 114 AD according to Norwegian historian Lise Hetland.The Dome is the world's largest uninforced concrete dome, so walk in (prior to 19:00 closing time) and see the grave of Rafael, end the scary light from the sun shining through the centric hole in the roof.After your visit, have a bottle of prosecco in one of the nice restaurants or cafees in front of the plaza.

A must visit when in Rome, great architecture, a fantastic dome with a huge circular hole at its top letting enough tite to illuminate the vast inside area with the many paintings and monumental tumbs icluding the ones of Historical kings of Italy dating from the time Italy was a Kingdom (1860 to 1948)
Just wonderful. We must return, without doubt we will return, everything has its charm, every monument is a world, a world in which you immerse yourself and you do not want to leave.
We liked very much this trip and we sure recommend it to everyone who come to visit Rome. It's very educational experience.
Roma means history means entering the time tunnel...each street is beautiful,.pantheon, vatican, love fountain, spanish stairs,collesium..Don't expect much if you haven't seen it yet ...
It’s a must see in Rome. If you’re not part of a tour, don’t worry! Anyone can walk in and enjoy the interior. The atmosphere at night time is even better than during the day.
i was lucky to see this church on my last day and sooo glad!  It is simply amazing.  Somewhat crowded in the afternoon but easily could see details.  Sculpture and paintings come to life
The crowds were not very bad and there were some amazing statues and artwork. The dome is really cool, It's open at the top and rain that gets in drains in the center.

An awe inspiring church and also a marvel of ancient Rome. Can't believe the condition after two thousand years. When inside it makes you feel small and dims the sound of the thousands of visitors. Impressive! It was free when we visited on a Sunday.
Loved the dome and the oculus. Amazing that this was built in AD 118-25. Like nothing else I have ever seen.

Went here twice - first under own steam and was in awe so came back with a very special tour guide - Bavid Battaglina who is half Roman half English and very very good - by far and away the best guide I have ever done a tour with. He loves the Pantheon - and his tour is designed to geta small group in there alone! Very very special - a memory we will never forget. So track David down - he does tours all over Rome and we did two with him over 2 days - he made our teip to Rome
Beautiful architecture!! Loved seeing all of the details. Amazed at the artwork and history. I can’t even imagine how this was built.
This is in the heart of Rome. The building is even more impressive on the inside. The lines were not too long. It is a not to be missed attraction in Rome. We used a Rick Steve's audio guide app.
We went in about 30 mins before it shut and we did not get charged and it is spectacular place to visit and right in the heart of Rome
Jaw-droppingly beautiful and just amazing to see such an ancient building still standing, and so beautifully constructed.
When I was there at panteon there was a guy who was playing Pink Floyd comfortably numb which is my all time favorite. Combination of panteon an Pink Floyd was great.
An interesting attraction, situated in a lovely square with eateries around. Not overcrowded and easy to read information on exhibits
This amazing building is the resting place of a number of important historical figures.The structure is both sombre and awe inspiring with a huge dome.

We had an amazing tour of the Pantheon. I’m so happy we did the tour instead of just walking around on our own. Our tour guide Giulia was incredible!! She was really knowledgeable and also a lot of fun. We left knowing so much about the Pantheon and also about many of the other historical sites in Rome. Highly, highly recommend!
A must se at different times of the day. The simplicity is almost overwhelming.  Take a pair of binoculars here and other sites. It really enhances your enjoyment
Breathtaking - definitely the main attraction in Rome! Architecture is amazing and I preferred this to the Colosseum.
We experienced a thunder storm while we were in the Pantheon.  It was fantastic!  The booming and rain that actually came in through the dome was an experience we won't forget.

The Pantheon is an ancient Roman temple that was saved from destruction thanks to its repurpose as a church. It has the largest dome in Italy, with the dome of St Peters apparently designed to be deliberately 1m less. It contains the graves of prominent Italians, including that of Raphael, the artist who died in his 30s of syphilis.
The fact that Pantheon has been in constant use for almost 2 000 years is mind blowing in itself. The fantastic interior, art and architecture only adds to the experience.
An amazing sight--and for free!  The marble floor, the crypts, and the dome are marvelous.   Listen to Rick Steves' audio tour while you are there for the historical context of what you are seeing.

This was part of our private city tour.  It’s free, it’s beautiful and do NOT get it confused with the Parthenon, LOL.  It is not the same thing or even connected to that at all. You must have your shoulders covered and modest length shorts, dress code is enforced. It is within easy walking distance of the colosseum.
Just fantastic , took you right back to Ancient Rome . An incredible building . No queuing involved. Great to sit outside with a gelato and take it all in

I found this the most impressive single monument in Rome. We were walking down a street towards the Pantheon and suddenly spotted it around a corner, when we least expected to. I will never forget that moment, looking up suddenly and seeing the looming monument... dark and powerful. It felt just as the emperors meant one to feel... small and insignificant, and completely in awe.My audio guide drummed up just the rigjt the mood and brought everything alive.An unforgettable experience!
Don't miss this attraction.  It is a must see.  Recommend using a tour guide or tour book to ensure you get details regarding the building.
Pantheon is still in good condition and looks very good because they are built with high quality materials. Must visit in Rome
We just walked through it ..it was one of the most beautiful places ive ever been.the statue of Perseus and medusas head

You don't need a tour or a guide to stand there with your mouth open and gaze at the magnificent dome. What a feat of building genius. It's right in the centre of Rome, which is a small city so the Colloseum is 25 minutes easy walk the Trevi 2 or 3 and the Spanish steps 5 minutes walk. If you are staying in the centre if poss. visit each of these both by day and night for their true spectacle.
This building is the one I talk about the most since my return home...the history involved and being the only building still standing in its entirety since the Roman Empire is breath taking

We first saw in the evening, closed but beautiful evening sight. During the day, we were lucky enough that it rained while we were inside, a sight to behold as the rain comes through the center hole..amazing...then the sun shown through..remarkable architecture.
A must see, IMHO, short queue, less than 5 mins, walk round inside looking up, take the photo, then back to the sunshine
What an incredible building. Beautiful surroundings and very accessible for wheelchair users. The security guards were very good at ushering crowds.

The Pantheon is free to enter and explore. You can purchase an audio guide and operators offer tours as well. We didn’t do either,  and just explored the attraction ourselves. It was busy, and we had to queue to enter, but not for long.

This is an amazing and interesting building, the architecture is superb, it is very big, and the open dome is an interesting feature, the floor is sloping to the centre to drain away any rain that falls! Well worth seeing when in Rome!

The Pantheon was a stunning and very unique building. It looks gorgeous on the outside and is very interesting to go to! It is a beautiful building and you can see the famous hole in the roof. We visited with a guide as a part of a walking tour of Rome which was very handy as we could learn about the Pantheon and understand what we were seeing.
Amazing building; it's hard to explain just how big the pantheon is  -  the whole square where the pantheon is, is very nice to visit and it was free.

Another Great archaeological site in Rome.   The outside of the Pantheon is as beautiful as the inside.   The pillars as you enter give you a feeling of its history.   The inside as you enter is a grand room with a 360 degree view of beauty.

Also one of the oldest and best preserved buildings of Ancient Rome. Visiting is free, you can spend between 20mins and maybe an hour exploring the Pantheon. Read about it a little beforehand. It has an interesting history.

The Pantheon is worth for its architecture (engineering?) alone.  Like so much of the ancient world, it astounds with the fact that it exists.Inside, the art is of mixed quality.  The visit is merited by the two personages of recent Italian history.  Here are the graves of Vittorio Emannuele II and King Umberto.Worth a visit and if you are lucky, you can marvel at the weather entering by the open roof.
This historical attraction was a highlight. It is just amazing and to think it was built 2000 years ago and church still happens here. It’s mind blowing

Coming through a side street the pantheon comes into view, impressive by any standard, one of the best preserved monuments, with the tomb of Raphael inside, simply stunning. We found a table and enjoyed the view by night
This is so well preserved through much history. It is free to enter but it’s worthwhile to rent the audio tour for the history and descriptions.
This is a site worth visiting in Rome, it’s free and a piece of history to see.  Short lines, and memory to take home.
Amazing Roman basilica which is incredibly atmospheric. Entrance of free and there are none of the queues found at other Roman sites. The dome is simply stunning!
amazing feel like in old square, multietnic people, romantic in sunset, full of people during the day
Every building is a work of art and I compliment the Italian government for preserving these beautiful buildings.

We called in here on the way back to the hotel and we were glad that we did. From the outside it doesn’t look anything spectacular but when you walk through the doors you really get the wow factor. It is bigger than I was expecting and the history / statues / paintings / ceiling were fantastic. We were glad that we took the time to visit this and I’d recommend going to it
if you think it looks impressive from the outside wait till you go in , beautiful art & sculptures, it draws you into its history .
The outside does not look like much but the inside is amazing, From top to bottom you will be amazed by the structure and art work. I was very impressed in how this was built.
Ancient temple that is very interesting to see. Go early in the day as the crowds get crazy and there are lines around the square to get in.
Go on a sunny day close to 1 of the equinox when the light from the hole at the top settles directly over the 4 points.  Its a celestial calendar.
Beautiful building built just under 2000 years ago, amazing to see it and touch it, well worth a visit. fantastic and lots of other things to see around it.
A must see loaction in Rome, the architectural detail within the Pantheon is breathtaking, the history outstanding.

We had booked the audio guide right at the beginning of the possible time window - good thing. The room filled quite quickly with tour groups carrying their talking guide. This made it quite loud in the room. The building is worth seeing in its history. The actual admission is free, so if you just want to watch...

Worth making the trip to see. Because of it's continuous use over the years, the level of preservation is truly remarkable.
From its pagan roots to Christianity, the Pantheon exudes spirituality. Don’t miss it. Be sure to walk over to Piazza Navona afterward.
Historic, beautiful, surreal! Amazing experience. It's near other attractions, as well. So you can walk to others after seeing it. Also near restaurants and souvenir places. Great location!
Beautiful paintings and architecture. We went on a Thursday and it was free not sure if it is normally.
Definitely a must see when in Rome. Entrance is free. It’s at a walking distance from most of the famous monuments.
The night view of the Pantheon is awesome and you can sit outside at one of the restaurants people watching.
Absolutely fantastic and beautiful the audio guide was really interesting. I think you should charge everyone an entrance fee it would generate income for less fortunate or a charity
Despite the long line at the entrance , the inside of the Pantheon was simply breath taking. The domed ceiling with the opening or the "oculus" which lets light in is so amazing !
This is a very peaceful and respectful place. Take your hats off guys! and be quiet. It's free so you should pop in for a visit while in Rome.

I dont know about you but i never knew the interior was rounded. I guess i always just knew the outside by image and never though much about what the inside would look like. This attraction is free, you are free to wander as you please. There was a service going on while we were there and as such you are required to dress appropriately (no hats, the sign said covered shoulders but i didnt see that enforced)The ceiling is in such a pattern that it almost seems like an optical illusion and i was transfixed by it.
was a nice little building to go and visit but in all honesty not much going on even though its meant to be 2nd best thing in places to see in Rome
So much history.  The details of the dome and columns is very interesting and amazing.  Beautiful art.  Well worth your time.

This was near our hotel so we stopped in.  It was a bit crowded but what isn't in Rome?  It was very nice inside.  We did not do the self-guided tour but did appreciate the artwork and sculptures inside.  A nice place to visit in the center of Rome.
Not on many must-see lists when visiting Rome... but it should be! Lovely to wander around inside and admire the architecture. Free to go in. Highly recommend
The large grey exterior hides the glorious interior. A moment of peace inside from the throng of the tourist crowds.

Beautiful and amazing especially considering the age.  Be prepared for exceptionally long lines.  Suggest grabbing a bite to eat and a cocktail at a cafe and allow the Pantheon to be your view while relaxing.
Despite the many tourists visiting and talking (too loud), this old church is very impressive. It is extremely well proportioned.

The pantheon is absolutely massive, we viewed it from the square outside and it’s very impressive. You can go inside but the hundreds of people we could see near the door alone put us off so can’t comment! Very busy.
This building has been in continuous use for 2,000 years which has helped be maintained in such wonderful condition
This is a must-see spot. Even more so since it had free entrance. Incredible how this almost 2000 years old building is in such good shape. Amazing architecture!
On of the nicest gems in Rome. Almost everyday nice concerts at 7:30. You can check the inside seeking nice architectural spots.

Sunday mass at the Pantheon is a once in a life time experience, the mass is said in both English and Italian and once the mass starts at 10:30 the pantheon is closed to all other tourists so you can appreciate it with all its glory. If you have ever wanted to see a mass in one of the worlds oldest churches this is your chance.

This is an amazing building both for its time and even more so for today with its history throughout the last two millennia. Though you dont strictly need it to appreciate what is around you the official audio guide enhances you experience. It never seems to get really crowded but for a bit more intimacy go early or late.
This church is Beautiful although not as much as others. The structure commands attention so go in and check it out. It is free. If you are wearing shorts, they may ask you to cover your knees.

Fantastic very old monument, dating from the time of the Roman Empire, transformed into a church. Huge, impressive, very well preserved. The fact that it was transformed into a church, also huge and splendid, probably had something to do with it. On the other hand, the audio comments for the visit are not really interesting. It's not worth the price.
A beautiful piece of history and wonder at the ability of the builders of these type of buildings with the tools they had at the time in history.Again wonderful decorations and art work.
In the end we didn't go in, however the architecture was interesting to review from the outside, and the Piazza outside was very picturesqe
A free site in Rome, no large que and for a half hour so worth the walk, pretty amazing condition considering it’s age.
Free to enter and breathtakingly beautiful. You can sit peacefully even though it is packed with tourists.
We thoroughly enjoyed our visit to the Pantheon. It's easy to get to, no fees, and no line. What a beautiful part of history.

We've been there twice  - the lines are short, it's free to get in and it's a stunning building. The 1900 year old dome is spectacular and the (more recent) tombs of kings and emperors are fascinating.

It was crowded but not difficult to get around. The square where the pantheon is located has lots of places to eat shop and buy souvenirs. Watch the beggars.
One of the most elegant and impressive structures in Rome. A 2000 year old object lesson in math and astronomy.
No need to buy tickets. Free entry and not that crowded. I didn't see any audio tour availability even though they were sold in online ticketing sites.

Definitely a site to see.  Lots going on outside in the piazza that is in front; take that in as part of the experience.  Inside, try finding a recorded tour or download one beforehand (like Rick Steves).  Lots of little quirks to investigate like Raphaels burial site.  The architecture, though, is the star here.  Unduplicated dome for hundreds of years!
we loved it, although you could just go in for free and see all of the place without purchasing the tickets

I was lucky enough to sneak in at 450PM. Like most of the ancient places in Roma, they don't disappoint.  If you like history and architecture you have to pop in for a look. It was raining the day I visited. The rain was pouring through the hole in the roof. Not sure why it was built that way but it gave something else to talk about. Not too be missed.

Hard to believe this was build almost 2,000 years ago.  Glad we stopped to see it.  Admission was free and only takes about 10-15 minutes to it and then easy walk to Trevi Fountain.  If your up for another short walk, you can then go to the Spanish Steps.  All three are within walking distance of each other.

My wife loves the Pantheon - we have to stop by every time we are in Rome.  It is between Piazza Navona and the Trevi Fountain, so is a convenient stop.  As with most of Rome's attractions, it is over-crowded, but at least there it is free to see.
Amazing site - go inside (free) to be fully impressed. Hard to believe that this building is already that old.
Well worth the visit.  Entrance is free and you can walk around at your leisure.  Make sure knees and shoulders are covered as this is now a church.
This is one of the most beautiful sites in Rome. Very quiet even though it was packed. Very impressive.
A great place to come as it is free and the building itself is stunning, can be quite busy so I would go later on in the evening when the crowds have gone.
Beautiful.  Such a wonderful leftover from the Roman Empire re purposed by the Christians.  You can't go to Rome and not visit the Pantheon!

My husband and I did a self guided 1 mile walking tour of Rome on our first day of arrival. The pantheon is a quick, easy visit that is free. The line to get in moved quickly and afterwards we got lunch in the square directly in front of the pantheon. Would highly recommend stopping in as the architecture and art contained within it are breathtaking.
A mammoth structure from the outside and beautiful and sacred inside .. a 2000 year old structure that is one of a kind in construction ..another wow moment from me
Very easy to visist just walked in off the street. Interesting roof structure and fantastic paintings

Although popular and busy this attraction is very well worth while a visit.From the exterior it is another large Roman structure, but inside it reveals its simple grandeur and fascination.Very well worth a visit.

It's lovely here, but even visiting in the shoulder season we were farmed through like cattle with a thousand other tourists
On the day of one of my tours in Rome, my guide tipped me off that at 1pm, I could observe the magic of equinox at the Pantheon! It was amazing!

My wife, son, daughter and I visited the Pantheon in August 2019.  We had been here before in 2015.  The Pantheon is one of the top sights in Rome.  Looking at the Pantheon from the outside with its dome and columns is a very good photo opportunity.  Inside is even more spectacular.  There are many paintings and some tombs of famous people such as Raphael.  It is very crowded so it is very important to try and find areas inside the Pantheon where there are less people so you can view everything calmly.  Walking to the Pantheon is very easy from the Via del Corso.

This is a wonderful building and an essential stop on any trip to Rome. It’s free to enter (unlike so many other attractions) and it’s truly beautiful. You don’t need a long time here, but it’s not to be missed. Be mindful of dress codes to this and other religious buildings in Rome. Like most places in Rome, there are a LOT of tourists with cameras and selfie sticks. Don’t forget to drink it in with your eyes as well as a phone/camera lens!

An Iconic & must-do place to visit.The entry is free but It's likely to be crowded all day. The open Dome is simply outstanding, and Raphael’s tomb is one of the highlights. I visited the Pantheon with my sister.  To enter you must be covered and lucky for us there was a scarf seller right near the entry as my sister had on tank top and shoulders must be covered. I did short videos as the inside is just to much to capture via photos
If you like history, you will love this place.  Also take a guided tour as they explain everything to you.
This is a must see in Rome , we were here 3years ago and missed it , it’s amazing building feat! Free admission and a beautiful square.

I really didn’t know what to expect when I decided to visit the pantheon. I was following the signs around the narrow streets pointing to my destination and honestly when I came around the last corner and the pantheon came into my view it really did make me stop and stare at its size and it’s magical presence. The audio tour definitely aided in providing interesting information/history about this beautiful building. I would recommend it. You do need identification to pick up the audio device and you need to bring head phones!

We came across the roman pantheon by chance when wnadering around the streets of rome. It is the most preserved buiding from ancient rome and free to enter. Well worth seeing impressive inside with the hole in the roof.
This building is truly an engineering wonder and a work of art. It is beyond my imagination how this could have been construction that long ago. When in Rome, don't miss this!
...and that means too many people behaving badly...Having said that, the architecture is just amazing and the perfection of the building breathtaking...
This is our third time here and each time it seems to get bigger. Awe inspiring. Thus is the must see when in Rome. Also Hostaria is the best cafe to visit for food and drink and to enjoy the view.

Incredible building, so much history too! It’s free to visit so we expected to queue for a long time but in the end it was only a few minutes. The architecture is stunning, it is a sacred place and all the visitors were respectful of that, you will be asked to cover your shoulders as in the Vatican so be mindful of your wardrobe choices!Worth a visit when in Rome, the surroundings streets are beautiful too.

Loved visiting this place. I was on an organised walking tour which really added to my understanding of what everything was. Located in a typical and beautiful Roman square surrounded by tourist (priced) restaurants. A must visit when it Rome.

The building was built over 1800 years ago. The name Pantheon means “temple for all the gods”. It was originally a Roman temple and later became a church. The facade consists of a portico with three layers of beautiful columns. Very impressive and majestic. The inside is circular in shape, not very large, with a high rise dome. The latter has a central opening (oculus). There is no window and so the oculus is the only source of sunlight. Rain can come through. The floor is slanted and can drain off the rain. The interior is well decorated but not lavishly. It contains the burial place of Raphael. The structure is one of the most well preserved ancient Roman building, which, taking into account the then technology, is in itself an architectural wonder.
Rome isn’t a visit without going to the Pantheon. The building really carries the ancient history of Rome. Another place which you’ll need to be prepared to share with hoards of other tourists...

It's an architectural treasure, I know. But mainly from the inside, the outside is just boring.At the outside you're surrounded by non Europeans (to be careful here) trying to sell you rubbish. And by too many tourists. Knowing the I side from earlier days I'd say, this is overhyped. Just get a good book and don't waste your time in the 150m queue
Recommend visiting later in afternoon as less queuesStunning interior but read up about it beforehand to fully appreciate its splendour

The outside is imposing with its columns and facade and when you enter  inside the beauty and grandeur of the Pantheon is astounding. The interior was very peaceful even though it was busy and it felt a very sacred place where people respected the silence.  The altars, paintings, statues and marble were beautiful and I would definitely go back here.
A must see place in Rome.  It is delightful to have lunch in one of the restaurants facing the Pantheon

including large groups of Chinese and Americans in the early Fall seasondid not offer even a entry to this most remarkable building of Ancient Rome...onlyin the late midnight hour was the Piazza in front of the Pantheon's Portico offera poetic, shadowy view of this massive structure suggesting the impressive capacities of the ancient Romans...Prevous visits in winter allow one to standdirectly under the open oculus and quietly observe this coffered dome and the play of light on the magnificent floor of inlaid precious marbles.  Go off season, or plan a late nite wandering from Piazza Navona to the Pantheon.

Just walking through Rome and all of a sudden come across above. Loved it soo much we came back at night and looked spectacular. Would recommend seeing in evening darkness when it is lit up. Only downside is you can’t get inside in evenings which is worth seeing.

Probably the most well preserved building from Ancient Rome is the Pantheon. Easily accessible from the Colosseum, it’s just a 10 minute walk away. At 143’ in diameter, and 143’ from the ground to the center, The Pantheon remains the largest unsupported domes in the world, and one of the largest ever made.
Now is a church, but is just beautiful.  Is a must to visit. In the center of the roof is a hole where the sun enters
A must-do in Rome. The dome is magnificent. And the architecture is stunning. No wait to get in, but it was packed when we visited on a Sunday.

Commissioned by Marcus Agrippa during the reign of Augustus, it was completed by the Emperor Hadrian around 126 AD.  Originally a Roman temple, now is a church.  Taking photos inside and having time to really enjoy the rich history of this church was fantastic!  The center of this domed facility was open, allowing water to enter during the rainy times.  Small drain holes discretely placed in the floor tiles facilitated the water draining to alleviate slippery floors.  Beautiful enormous doors, statuary, and a sense of reverence prevailed, in spite of the always present crowds.  Excellent visit!!!
Very good tour beautiful building lots of information given during tour building was very clean and well maintained
Quite the queue to get in but it moves quite fast. We visited this as part of our walking tour which I would recommend
Impressing and more beautiful by night. Really nice with the lighting. Good place to take great photos.

Who would have thought such a plain looking building was so beautiful on the inside. The doomed roof is a wonder and the architecture and design is truly amazing. Any it's free! Would definitely recommend a trip here for anyone visiting Rome.

This place just took you back in time to give you a snippet of what it was like. Well cared for, well organised for you to queue and get in, not pressured to leave early. Also have some interesting internment’s from history.

This is an amazing building that is worth the visit despite the crowds. The dome is amazing and the building has a great atmosphere
I loved the Pantheon, it was one of my favorite sites in Rome! It's old, elegant and simple with beautiful marble.
The columns on the exterior are incredible.  This building was easy to get into, as no real line to see.  The interior are very nice.
David Battaglino is very knowledgeable and funny tour guide.  Pantheon is one of the most interesting building in the world.  Make sure to take tour with David.
We qued to get inside, but the Que went down pretty quick. Watch the video on the screen inside which is very informative. Worth a visit.

Any building that lasts for 2000 years has to be admired. Surprisingly poignant inside, given the crowds. You have to enter to see the full effect of the dome.
I am a lover of History so to be able to stand in the Pantheon and soak up the Experiance was amazing

This almost 2000 year old former Roman temple, now church is another amazing site in Rome. It's free to enter and visit on your own. It's likely to be crowded with many tour groups but you certainly don't need a tour or a guide to appreciate the incredible architecture and history. Check out Rick Steves podcasts for a free audio tour of the Pantheon and many of the other places in Rome.
Took a tour of the Pantheon during our trip to Rome.  The architecture and engineering are fabulous.  The visit was greatly enhanced by our tour guide.
The history is amazing. Seeing this with the night lights is the most impressing time to visit. The romance of Rome can't be missed experiencing at dusk.Lisa Reinicke/author

We actually strolled along this tourist site whilst on route to the Colosseum. It was on our list of things to see, so we wandered in, it’s free to just walk in! What an impressive building ! Definitely worth a visit! How ever did they build it ! It’s incredible architecture is just awesome! Definitely worth a visit when in Rome !!

An incredible structure, worth a visit if your in Rome, but if time is limited, I’d visit other places first, ie the coliseum/ do a hop on hop off bus tour, it is free to go in, make sure you remove your hat as it is a place of worship.

Despite making the classical structure a religious basilica and martyr - the secular nature of this architecture takes one’s breath away.Start with the monolithic columns of stone pillars. They stand tall  and grand - witness to several empowers and people - inviting everyone of all religion, class, caste and gender with wide open lofty arms.And then step through the bronze door and feel the majesty of the rising dome.The concrete walls of the dome are poetic and more profound than the desperate attempts of naive men who claim spaces below in the name of religion or country.An exception is Rafael.The beloved artist finds an eternal bed in this wonderful home.Walking on the marble floors one can see how this structure inspired Brunelleschi and Michelangelo to build contemporary domes.Open the oculus of your heart and let kindness and love for fellow human beings.
Beautiful architecture, no entrance fee which is a bonus to looking around this stunning building.. Not to be missed.
When we visited it was very busy inside, however the building was absolutely stunning, not a place to miss.
Amazing building with beautiful architecture. It is definitely worth the visit. There is no entry fee, don´t miss out!

A stunning building steeped in history. It was a real experience. There isnt too much inside but its beautiful just to walk around the building.There are also some great places to eat and drink over looking the pantheon that I would suggest as something to do to pass an afternoon in Rome.
I read this was first a temple then later a church. When your inside look up. The entrance is free but you must be properly dressed. Your arms must be covered.
Another part of an excellent tour of Rome.  Coliseum, Historic sites, and Vatican.EXCELLENT small group tour with personal experienced guide.Highly recommend.
Very beautiful both on the outside and on the inside, lot of interesting facts about it and It's spectacular when It's raining.

If you go aroubd 2pm it's not jam packed. But it was also super hot the day I went. Don't go at the end of Sept lol

What an amazing place! Built the best part of 2000 years ago, this building is an incredible example of how to build an enduring masterpiece. Amazing tour by our tour guide !!! She was well informed a out the history and was open to any questions. Fun tour that i would highly recommend. Going into the pantheon is free but you don’t get all the information with a tour guide. Highly recommend getting the tour.

We got up early and entered in the morning. The sit was free of charge. There weren't that many tourists around at that time.Both inter and outer were beautiful. A must see experience in Rome.Afterwards it got so many people in the afternoon with tour group.

4th time visiting the best building in the world and still manages to take my breathe away. There are just no words to describe the AMAZING architecture workmanship and pure genius of the people at the time. Memory for a life time

A must see for sure.  As you round the corner and the Pantheon comes into view your jaw drops and then you say WOW!!!  Such a beautiful place outside and inside.  Take a seat inside and marvel at all there is to see!!!!

The inside is breathtaking in terms of grandeur but unless you are into every historical  detail you would not require more than an hour to see this.It is very crowded and the square outside is a busy spot with tourists and locals alike.
Don’t miss the Pantheon- an amazing demonstration of the architectural skill of the ancient Romans. Breathtakingly beautiful.
This a must-see in Rome: the fantastic dome is something everyone with just a shade of engineering curiosity must seek out. Admission is free and there is usually a line but it is quick moving.

We visited at opening (8:30 am).   It was uncrowded and a completely enjoyable experience.  I am amazed at the thought that this edifice has been standing for 2000 years plus.   It is free to enter and we just wandered at will.   I highly recommend it.

In the ancient time Pantheon served as a temple for seven planetary gods. Nowadays it is a Christian temple (Santa Maria ad Martyres). Entrance is free and Pantheon it self is enormous, it entrances the doors especially make you feel small. If you had only time to see one thing in Rome it would have to be this one!
Absolutely fantastic architecture and so clean and pristine.  It's hard to imagine these building still exist in such great order.
The place is definitely leaves impression and mind blowing for the large scale building capabilities of the Roman empire. I've found this to be more impressive than the Taj-Mahal
So old and beautiful and precious! Its definitely worth passing by here as you are walking around the city!

I would not miss seeing the outside however, especially at night and this is great neighborhood near our hotel. We did a Rick Steves audio tour inside that was informative. DO NOT use the Ria Exchange ATM in the Pantheon Piazza. Use a bank ATM instead. The rates were egregious there.

We loved it, since it is of the ancient Roman constructions preserved improvement , a jewel of Roman constructive techniques, must certainly , we went with Pedro as guide of the free tours touring blunt, very good friendly and entertaining guide, especially if they go with children and / or teenagers

We have been there three times already. This is one of those places where you are just in Awwwww!  Many little restaurants outside so you can have drinks and food in the square. Another incredible place to see in Rome.

Well worth a visit,it’s free and not crowded, amazing architectural church with a great open dome, it must be great for rainy days to see how it works ( we visited in very hot day).down load an audio guide to know the history.

Do not miss this. It is so majestic and beautiful, such fine detail and such an amazing roof. Loved the column which were perfectly matched and the marble floor which is also a drain for the weather coming in the holier the roof. Hard t believe its been there for nearly 2000 years.

Just being able to see this wonderful history is humbling. 126 AD and it’s still here in 2019 for us to enjoy. The Pantheon is beautiful and breathtaking. Lots of people in this crowded area but we had no issues. Bring your walking shoes!

An engineering marvel and beautiful structure.... not to be missed. Would like to be inside in a rare rainstorm to see rain coming in the open circular hole in the roof and draining on the crowned floor.

Nice little active church I believe it s one of the only remaing roman temples in it's full glory. Free entry and very interesting little place. Theres also quite a famous person buried in there.

Our tour was an amazing tour with Musement Tours with Alessandro as our excellent tour guide! His knowledge of the place and history was very enjoyable! We started off with the Coloseum, then the Palatine Hill and Pantheon. We highly recommend to get all three of these tours as they are connected to each other. Pick the one that we did so you won't get bored with all the talks of the guided tour. Our tour was a combination of guided and on our own. This way, we have the freedom to do what we want to do without getting stuck with the tour guide. I love this tour with Musement Tour!
This place was amazing! Obviously a little crowded but simply stunning.  Well worth a visit while in Italy.
Worth a walk down to the Pantheon impressive building with open dome and it was raining so nice to see rain falling inside the building. It's a Church so wear appropriate clothing!

We went in here and was just amazed on how beautiful it was in person! We went early in the morning when there was no lines, by the afternoon tons of people.  We really had a great time and experience here.

The Pantheon was interesting to see- espeically just knowing all of the history behind it- and that time in Romes history. There was SO many people there- and I only spent maybe 10 minutes in there- BUT if you are in Rome- I highly reccommend checking it out!

This is an amazing building with beautiful architecture. It is definitely worth the visit. It’s free to get in. They do check your bags so best not to take a large bag. Also there is a dress code - short skirts, short shorts, and bare shoulders are not permitted.

Our guide for the pantheon was wonderful!  We saw this beautiful ancient site on our Caravaggio tour and she really brought out all the importance of the architecture, significance in history and even pointed out the burial place for Rafael.  Amazing!
The intact exterior and its amazing columns does not  reduce the feeling inside the building. The open dome was quite impressive.
This place just blows my mind. To think that it was built to perfection without the tools, computer aided designs and cranes is quite incredible. A MUST for any traveller to Rome.

As with any free attraction there is no chance to skip the queue (except maybe via a tour group). However, visiting in mid-September appears to be far enough outside the main tourist season that it only took 5-10 minutes to get inside. It is an exemplary example of Roman architecture from the outside with its strong ancient columns and from the inside with its huge concrete dome. This may have taken inspiration by Nero’s palace, the so-called Domus Aurea (Golden House) that can be visited on weekends behind the colosseum. The whole visit does not take long, no more than 20-30 minutes.

Another of Rome's top attractions. Impressive granite columns at the front and another magnificent dome. Two thousand years old and still wonderfully preserved. Free entry but again huge queues. One of the must sees in Rome.
One of the most spectacular buildings I’ve ever seen still standing on its original foundation. Took a private tour to get pictures of this piece of history with it completely empty.
An amazing ancient building which is free to visit. There was barely any wait time for the line when we visited.

The Pantheon is simply spectacular. It's a stunning monument to Roman architecture, once a temple and now a church, ancient and enduring. The Basilica of Santa Maria & the Martyrs, also known as the Pantheon, was formerly a Roman temple, but is now a church which was dedicated as a memorial to all the Christian Martyrs in the 7th century. You enter up stairs and walk between large granite Corinthian columns, but once inside you find that the building is cylindrical.  It reminded me of a huge dome, and is in fact the worlds largest unreinforced concrete dome, sitting on the ground; it is powered by natural light via a central oculus open to the sky. Very different - as there as no seats, it is obviously just a memorial and not used for services.

The Pantheon was not as expected.  It is interesting to see however the large open space in the centre is not what I expected BUT you need to learn about its architecture design to really appreciate this building and its history.
Well worth the time to visit.  It is free, which was nice.  Interesting history and it is an amazing architectural structure for its time.
Well worth stop on walk from hotel to Colosseum. Impressive architecture. Better signage explained significance would be appreciated.

Visit the Majestic PANTHEON of Rome to discover its history at the empty dome, is located in the center of Rome not far from Piazza Navona. Great monument that Michelangelo defined as the work of Angels and not of men.The name comes from the Greek words pan (everything) and theon (divine), and originally the Pantheon was a small temple dedicated to all Roman deities."Erected between 27 and 25 BC by Consul Agrippa, prefect of Emperor Augustus, son of Lucius, consul for the third time."There are many stories and legends circulating around the Pantheon. First of all the one linked to the very place where it is located. It is said that here Romulus, the legendary founder of Rome, at his death was grabbed by an eagle and taken to heaven among the gods. The Pantheon is a masterpiece of architecture and engineering. Among the best preserved historical monuments in Rome, it exerted great influence on all Western architecture. If you can enter the temple at noon, when the sun's rays from the large circular opening at the top of the dome become extraordinarily intense they create a truly unique effect, unlike all the other great times of the past that were made to be seen almost exclusively from the outside with the Pantheon you turn this conception upside down.

Our visit to Rome was not going to be complete unless we visited the Colosseum and the Pantheon, this place is truly beautiful in the inside and out, so majestic, so much art and history every way you looked, it was free to get in and we didn't buy the audio thing and still enjoyed
A must in Rome. Don’t go in the morning or middle of the day.. wait till an hour before they close the doors. There will be no wait and you’ll be able to walk the interior without millions of people.

Beautiful inside and outside! Colosseum was free to visit until '98. Who knows when Romans decide to charge a fee for visiting Pantheon. Nowadays there's no queue nor fee and it is stunning inside. Enjoy the iconic facade outside at the plaza.

The building looks so ancient from the outside, so surprising from the inside.  The conversation from a pagan temple into a church saved it from destruction.  You can't visit Rome without experiencing this monument to Roman brilliance.
We spent just a few minutes in line and went inside the Pantheon. We really enjoyed this visit. There is no charge unless you want to purchase audio services.

The first day in Roma we visited the Pantheon, it is truly awe inspiring, it temains a peaceful contempative place. It seems like every alleyway leads to a stunning view in this city.
What a place. Stolen by the Christians, this is an amazing temple originally built to honour all the gods. Another must see.
You can't go all the way to Rome without visiting!  It's a great idea to read up on it before your visit.

The Pantheon was closed due to a Public holiday the first time we went but on our second visit we went inside. We went in August and the queues were very long but moved quickly. No cist to enter and well worth it.
The Pantheon is amazing and it’s all free to visit I don’t think it’s sign- posted clearly enough the inside is breathtaking so is a must to visit, it stands in a little cobbled square.

The Basilica is breathtaking, that’s the only word for it if there’s one place you visit make it this one but such a lot of walking doing the whole tour but was so worth it, I have learnt such a lot this week if only I could retain it
Not surprisingly and very crowded place you can't wander around, but an incredible feat of architecture with a hole in the top!!

Considering the commercialisation of historical buildings - the fact that this one is free and is a genuine beauty is something everyone should get to see before they eventually stick an entrance fee on it when the economy goes down the pan.

Of the 8 day stays in Rome, we turned out spending most of our time nearby Pantheon areas, where I can find nice local shops for shopping, restaurants that would never fail me and the Giolitti (reputable Gelato store that was there for a century!)
Worth seeing for the dome and the tombs of the Italian kings. Realise it was made into a church to keep it intact, but would prefer it with at least a nod o its origins other than just the portico.
Considering this was free this was definitely a highlight. Very little waiting time and was blown away when we got inside. Awesome
I've been to the Pantheon several times and I am always awestruck.  Always crowded, but a  can't miss when in Rome.
Free! No need to spend money on a tour! Enjoy! Great coffee shop nearby called Sant Eustachio! Aussies, must do coffee!
The Pantheon is of course a must-see attraction. I recommend an audio tour, you simply learn more than from a guidebook.

Amazing architecture with the open roof which allows the rain to pour in even if there is a mass going on! Set within a lovely square with bars and restaurants it really is well worth going to see! Round the corner is the famous gelato shop selling more than 150 different flavours of ice cream which needs a visit after you leave the Pantheon!

It looks like another stunning building in Rome but when you go inside and see it has a hole in the roof and drainage holes in the floor you really appreciate the design and unusual nature of this building.

This amazing building is situated in the center of  a small piazza . Proper dress is required. There is a hole in the roof, it was designed like that, with a very small drain in the floor underneath it. Get there early before the crowds .

It is beautiful on day time but at night it just fills up your eyes with splendor and the construction is so perfect that makes you wonder how could they be so advanced then, amazing columns, worked sculptured areas with such fine design and the huge presence of architectural talent, are there for you to admire. Don't miss this one out.
This building combines between the beauty of the church and the Roman temple.. you will not forget it ever.Also, you can find Rafael there, don't forget to thank him for all his beautiful paintings.
