Great building to visit!  Loved the details inside the Pantheon and our guide made it part of his free walking tour.  Gaston was very knowledgeable!
I recommend just sitting down in the pews and observe the beautiful architecture without your phone.  There is so much to see when you are not looking through the lens of a camera.
Must go and see this beautiful Place. Has the tomb of two king and the Queen of Italy. A open roof that male you thinking how is possible that Is real a hole in the cupola! Must see!!!
Arrive early to avoid crowds. The view and the architecture of the dome is spectacular especially the size of it.

Horrible experience all together in Rome. The military was overlooking all monuments - to make the entry painfully slow. I assume the city should think more about a better solution. Look at that line to wait! We skipped to see the Pantheon

I didn't know what to expect heading to the Pantheon, but was very surprised with what we saw. Incredible architecture and to still be standing almost 2000 years later with the original floor through all of Rome's history of being sacked and raided is phenomenal.
We arrived early and was able to walk around unhindered, the dome is spectacular. So much history leaves you in awe.

The architecture is amazing. The interior is a lot colder than outside so make sure to bring a coat with you if you want to be more comfortable during the visit. I would suggest you take a hat as well but they dont allow that since this is  still considered a church.

In the heart of Rome one of the wonders of the world... the Pantheon offers a magical perspective where knowing its history makes you travel with imagination. If you are visiting Rome for the first time this is a must.

Awe inspiring building, really with a visit for the sheer size. Virtually no queue to get inside. Raphael's tomb, with it's slightly ratty taxidermy birds hung overhead was interesting! So many tours going on, you get a good gist of the history too.

There are so many historical places to visit in Rome but this one was an unexpected surprise. The artwork and ceiling in particular are spectacular. Using an audio guide enhances this experience and places what you are viewing into context. If you are just looking for a visual 'wow' than just wander in your own time.
It is one of the most beautiful building of Imperial Rome and it is an masterpiece of engineering and architecture. Italian kings are buried here
Very impressive story what has since the emperors and when you are at the front door in the afternoon when the sun comes in is impressive
I would recommend going to tour the Parthenon in the morning, to beat the crowds and the Summer soaring temperatures

This building gives you the real sense of what the Romans were all about - engineering!  Not only is it beautiful (although it is SO strange that they've turned it into a church, which seems so counter-intuitive), but learning about the open dome on top, and it's significance about time and the seasons is astonishing.
This was a really good place to visit if you’re going to go here you might as well go I really enjoyed it
The Pantheon was amazing, the shops in the area were quaint an the food and coffee were to die for.  I highly recommend this stop.  The history inside of the Pantheon is nothing but amazing

If you only have a few days in Rome, this is a must see. As with many of the great historical buildings and monuments, this is located in the middle of a quaint little square and just a marvel to see in person. Definitely see the Vatican, Colosseum, Trevi Fountain and a few of the other major sites first; but don't forget to see this one.

I was really impressed with the level of maintenance and the history behind the Pantheon. The fact that it is so well kept considering the location is astounding. It is really a recommended area to visit.

This is a great building to visit in Rome. It’sFree to enter which is great considering it’s age. Gets busy inside but no real queue to get inside. Amazing building, free entry, small queue to enter, what’s not to like!

In a word "Magnificent" ! First visited it in 1982 when it was much quieter in tourist numbers, but I still love it as much now as I did then ! It was my husband's first Italian visit and I chose this as the first place to introduce him to Rome, and as it's now his favourite also I think my job is done ! The Pantheon sums up the Romans completely ; innovative, creative, enduring and just a little bit mad ! The fact that it's still standing there intact in that beautiful piazza after two thousand years is a marvel, and we all must thank the genius engineers, brick builders and other tradesmen who designed and built this amazing structure !

It’s a cool place to see and won’t take long.  As the name describes it’s a pantheon (where people are buried) and for Italians it’s considered a sacred place. Therefore, for women bring a shawl to cover your shoulders if wearing a tank top. Also, wearing short shorts is not allowed. Rafaello’s tomb is here (the famous artist) so is the tomb of the first kings of Italy among other ancient important figures.
The Pantheon was just incredible. So beautifully preserved. I highly recommend taking a guided tour of all these historical structures. There is so much interesting history to learn.
Wow. This was absolutely breathtaking! Such an impressive building! Was very peaceful in amongst the bustle of Rome. Worth a visit.
You can not miss this wonderful place. Walk around then go and sit in the square and have an aperol spritz:)

Very much worth a trip to see this marvel. Do some research ahead of time to appreciate the significance of this structure. This may be the most mysterious structure in the world outside of the Sphinx and pyramids in Giza. The uncut machined 5 foot diameter Egyptian marble columns out front speak for themselves. Very close walk from Trevi or Piazza  Navona and free entry!! with no pressure to leave. Good food right in the square at Napoletano restaurant and Salami Salumeria. Both fantastic culinary treats.
Just go there on a sunny day. its magic, as the light falls through the open circle in the roof and enlightens the place!

The Pantheon wasn't originally on our list of sights to visit in Rome, however it was recommended by a tour guide so we went early one morning as i had seen long queues the day before.  We were able to go right in, there was no entry charge (which surprised me). It is a beautiful building and im so glad we visited.  We didn't get a guided tour, the notices on the walls were enough for what we wanted.  It probably takes 1/2 - 3/4 hr to get around and its time well spent
Absolutely stunning very busy b4 u go in you must remove all hats not sure why very busy be prepared to que

This building the Pantheon, in Rome, has preveiled thru over 2,000 years of history. The architecture is amazing with its open dome roof which still to this day is the only ever  known unreinforced dome in history. There are small drainage holes in the marbel floor to take away any rain away out underneath that may fall inside. There are all colours of marble from all over the country and beyond, even from Egypt. There are two gigantic doors at the entrance 7 metres high and nearly a metre thick. There are past Kings and artists buried around the inside of the wall. This place is absolutely amazing. If you are ever in Rome make sure you visit the Pantheon.

This was an enormous building in wonderful condition. It was formerly a temple to all the gods- now a Catholic Church. Simply gorgeous inside with stunning sculptures and art. The floors are original from the temple. You must see this!
history enacted ..  This was busy place but very worth searching out.   A building of some historic and religious significance the touristy nature of the place somewhat detracts.
Bit disappointed was packed with people no order. Was not one of the best places to visit. Nice inside not one of the best.

Once you get your picture outside, head in and view the inside Dome. It is a church so you might get turned away if you are not covered up enough however they didn't seem to be that strict and do let alot of bare legs and shoulders march in. I would suggest you visit here if on your way to or from another larger attraction. It's a fly by visit.

Panteon is definitely worth seeing. It is beautiful - altars, decorations, etc. everything is great. The original thing is the only "window" is just a hole in the middle of the cupola.Visiting Panteon is free but during holidays, on weekends (and especially in the afternoon), queues  are so long that it may be daunting. It is better to come in the morning or during the rest of the days of week.

This is a beautiful building, a part of our history that should absolutely be visited.  Try to visit when it's sunny so the sunlight can filter in through the oculus and light up the building a bit.  Enjoy wandering slowly through the building and just soaking in the history.

It's a must-see of course. Easy walk from Piazza Navona. Sunny day helps so you can see the light play through skylight to the walls. We're big fans of Rafael so it was a real treat to see his tomb. Enjoyed just standing out by the fountain and watching the people go by.
A place full of history, in a nice area.One of the oldest buildings in rome.The bronze from the building is used im the skt. Peters church.
This was not a planned tour. We used Rick Steve’ Audio tour and were very impressed with this jewel we stumbled on. The architecture is amazing and the story more so. Would highly recommend.
Just another beautiful example of great architecture.  Go.  Sit.  Take a moment to enjoy.  It is beautiful and it is free.

The cylindrical temple building was constructed over the 113 to 126 AD period. Very impressive large granite columns at the front, give it an opulent appearance. Since 609 AD, it has been used as a Roman Catholic church dedicated to St. Mary and the martyrs. The interior of the massive coffered dome is utterly magnificent, and at its centre, allows light to enter through a central oculus.
Takes you back as soon as you walk in! Ensure to wear appropriate clothing as my partner got turned away for showing too much of her shoulders! Free facility which really has to be seen when in Rome.
Very interesting, so much history here, the dome at the top is worth going for let alone the rest of the building, crowds weren't to bad when we went at the end of May - free entry.
Amazing piece of history, well worth seeing. We did the guided tour. Even though it was 40 degrees some people forgot to respect this  holy building. Very busy place.

The Pantheon was one of the sights at the end of our list of places to go, but now we have been it should have been right up there toward the top.We had a lovely evening there, with a great meal sat right by the Pantheon thinking about how ancient this place truly is.

A must see but remember to take a scarf to cover up as they don’t like you showing a lot of flesh.Beautiful building that should not be missed. Can cool down on the benches and enjoy the stunning interior
The wait wasn't too long, and I can honestly say that photos do not do justice to the doomed ceiling. I have rarely felt so small.
It's free to enter and really nice to see inside. Really impressive with the whole in the ceiling and you don't have to keep an eye on what to wear like in the Vatican.

Built between A.D.118 and 125 it is a must see in Rome.  First off, there is no admission to get into the site. It is the best preserved building from ancient Rome. Once you enter and get into the dome, wow is all I have to say.  The Oculus with the sun shining in is a sight to see.  When in Rome...
Beautiful site to visit, a must see.  We loved sitting inside and admiring the beauty of the building around us.  The piazza outside is also quite fun!
Remarkable piece of architecture built with the engineering concept way before their time.  Simply amazing!

Be prepared to be astounded by this truly amazing super structure. Didn't realise the opening at the dome wasn't covered.  Heaps of visitors but the queues move very quickly.  No fee to see this magnificent piece of history!
The Pantheon is an amazing building that it is hard to believe is really as old as it is. It's open long hours and free to enter, so well worth spending a few minutes having a look.
Rome is different but the Pantheon is a wow factor. Complete inside it outshines all the other attractions in a simple majestical way.

This is a must see. Enjoy the beauty but keep in mind this was built in 125 AD. The technology uses was genius. It remained the largest free standing dome structure for over 1300 years when the Duomo of Florence was built. It is magnificent and gives me goose bumps each time I see it. I never grow tired of visiting the Pantheon.

The city tour bus was out transportation for  three days just hopping on and off or just going round to save our poor ,tired feet . Vatican was a bit of a slog to do in one day but so worth it Took day trips to Cine Citta , Pompeii and Florence .
Very impressive the inside of this monument in the center of historic Rome! And free entrance with no waiting time!
If you are in Rome you should visit Pantheon beacause of uniqueness of this place. You can get inside for free and it is really worth spending some time there.

We had just been to Trevi Fountain and due to the heat were wandering, enjoying an ice-cream. Just looked up and saw the Pantheon in front of us! I thought it was just a building but inside it was beautiful, statues, paintings etc. Well worth half hour or so of your time.
You don't need long to visit the pantheon, you can literally just pop on. We did an audioguide tour and that was very informative. It is one of the best places we visited while we were in Rome.
This is an incredible building. You could stay here for ages just marveling at the construction. And it's free to visit!

Women have to cover their shoulders. That’s the only hold up for entering this beautiful structure. The oculus in the ceiling is magnificent and the roof is so cool. Wasn’t very interested in the various alters within the building. It’s free and worth seeing!

We visited the Pantheon on our first day of our holiday to Rome. I would highly recommend this attraction because it is a wonderful, historic and iconic building in the heart of the city, and with free entry to all visitors, you really cannot go wrong! Only gripe with this place is that it does get fairly busy, which makes moving around the Pantheon quite difficult.In spite of this, I would still refer to this as a must-see if sightseeing in Rome, albeit it's not something you would need any more than 30 minutes to fully see.
The Pantheon is so well preserved and the history behind it is fascinating, as are most of Rome. The artwork is breathtaking and the open dome makes you feel like it's a shortcut to heaven!

Amazing structure and one of the oldest original buildings in Rome. Definitely worth a visit. Numerous cafes and ice cream parlours dotted around or you could just sit on the steps and take it all in 😁👍

I thoroughly enjoyed our visit in the late morning to the Pantheon. It is a free, Roman temple not to be missed. The line looked long, but moved quickly to enter. It is the site of Rafael’s burial place and an active temple with services occurring. The dome is stunning. It is a place where decorum, (i.e. silence and appropriate dress & behavior) is expected. Recommended.

I wasn't expecting to be so overwhelmed by the sheer size of the dome's interior.  Free admission makes it very accessible for anyone visiting, unlike most other historical sites in Rome.  We must have been lucky, because the line was not bad.  They were obviously controlling the size of the crowd inside, only letting in people as others left.  This is an active religious site, so remember to check dress code and behavior expectations prior to your visit.  It's a must-see for anyone traveling to Rome.
No one should miss this free to view wonder in Rome! The engineering involved all those years ago is a credit to the Roman civilisation.
A wonderful experience there's just something about this place. It doesn't matter how many people are in there it's still peaceful. Another must visit place in Rome.

Free entry was nice. Line moves quite quick as you just go in, move around the circle and get out. Nice ceiling, make not of where the light comes in. At certain hours it’s right in the middle of the floor
Wowsers! This place,  the architecture,  the ancient energy flowing,  the crowds,  the atmosphere of awe was just something to behold and we feel so grateful!
You must go and at least take photos of this amazing structure but be ready to fight the crowds.  I give it only 3 stars as we saw other churches that awed us much more than this

You may have seen this place in history books and in a Netflix documentary but do yourself a favor and put it on your bucket list as this should be on the list of wonders of the world. You cannot believe this place is 2000 years old it is perfect in every way. If in Rome do yourself a favor and go. The whole place does not have a single nail to hold the structure up yet it has stood the resto of time.
It’s free and very busy. Security was nice and efficient. Dress appropriately. A small gift shop sells literature and postcards. Very close to many other attractions and shops.

I have no idea why I loved the inside of the Pantheon but I did. We sat for ages just looking at the beauty of it and enjoying the peaceful calming feel (although it was quite busy)Hard to explain, but give it a go and hopefully feel it for yourself!

A great attraction. Really easy to find and relatively short queue to get inside. The audio tour booth is inside on the right hand side, again just a couple of people in the queue at 12pm. Z really nice attraction that is a must visit & made even better thanks to the highlights from the audio guide.

Walked in and the place was bustling.  It was like I was at a market or something.  My wife, daughter and myself then went to kneel and say a few hail marys' or something.  At that point in a couple of languages they told people they were in a church not a public meeting center.  told them to be quiet while we were praying...it calmed down and then picked up again and someone shushed the crowd over the PA systemAs man attraction its is top off the line an do not miss stop.  free easy access and a lot to see.  I took some photos which i will post. the last photo shows  the hole in the ceiling.  There is a whole  routine for up keep because of the hole.

I have tried so hard for the past times to get in to this Beauty but its too packed - THe whole idea is to just go in lei surly and be amazed by the architecture and the beauty of this magnificent building but I have officially given up! The lines and the crowds out on the Piazza is overwhelming to say the leastt. I'll will take my espresso on the nearby cafes, yes, I know it sounds touristy and perhaps it is BUT just to see the building is enough for me but cannot and will not deal with enormous lines and being hit by backpacks... If I can figure out what time of the day its best to go and be somewhat alone, I will let you know...

There are so many things to do when In Rome but this is a must do top 10.It's free so always a plus.The decor is amazing and always good if you watched Angels and Demons before leaving the UK lol.Loved it.

This is a wonderful and interesting building all by itself.  The see it and then to see other buildings copying its style is just impressive.  The security lines moved very quickly and there was no need to wear long pants, etc... It was nice to just wander about the building inside and just marvel at the construction.  Of course the dome is the highlight and signature mark of this building.  However, the outside of the building is equally impressive.  Definitely a sight to behold.
date: CK2000New York City, New York310 contributions0

text: Brilliant piece of history and something you must see when visiting Rome. Its free to go inside and you can pay for an audio guide should you wish (I didn't opt for this) It was nice to take my time to look around and not be rushed around. Makes a great photo outside too!

Pantheon was built as Roman temple for Roman gods and godddes. After the rise of Christianity, it was converted into Christian Church, therefore, Pantheon become one of the oldest church in Rome after more than 20 centuries later. The interior design is very simple with big dome in the middle of the church. Inside interior is still original and well maintained. One of Roman buildings is still intact until.now. We highly recommend to visit this place for atl least half an hour to admire the marvel architecture of Roman time.
I personally didn't think it was worth the visit, but I suppose it was good to see. It is just one room after all with not a lot really going on. Five minutes in there and you are done

It's amazing to see 2000 years old building completely preserved.Great example of ancient Roman architecture. During the centuries Catholic church added some Christian items inside, which mixed with ancient Roman art are witnesses of 20 centuries of turbulent history...Try to go there early in the morning to avoid crowd.Good thing is that Pantheon is located close to many other famous sites and interesting streets and old buildings

Rick Steves has a fun walking tour for the Pantheon. It was nice to go inside and listen to the history of this building. We went about 30 minutes before it closed so the crowds were quite sparse. And we were the last ones to leave. It was really a good time to visit. Definitely need to go visit this while you're in Rome.
When in Rome this should be on your bucket list of things to see. Note. Don't just 'see' it. Take it all in. Observe it. Enjoy the inside as well as the outside. You won't regret it.

I give this an excellent rating simply because it's an amazing piece of architecture and it's as old (if not older) than the Colosseum. It's the world's largest un-reinforced concrete dome - simply incredible! Granted there's not much to see inside, it's the fact that you're standing in such an ancient architectural wonder.The line (queue) outside may be rather long, but it moves quickly. You aren't allowed access when a church service is happening, unless of course you're there to attend the service...
We were shocked at the interior of the pantheon. Simply stunning and awe inspiring. No need for a guided tour, just head on in.

The pantheon is one of those places where you visit and only appreciate once you have left. There is a fantastic porthole in the roof which lends great light to the chamber. The decoration and Renaissance artwork is to die for and apart from the occasional rude tourist, it's a must see destination!

Go early. No queues. Peace & quite to stare in wonder at such a perfect structure from antiquity.How it's survived in such splendor  through the various political & religious upheavals of the past 2000 years or so makes it so special - it shouldn't really have - as the Colosseum, Forum & Palatine palace all bear testament. So stand under the oculus, look up, and wonder at which famous historical figures may have stood in exactly the same spot looking up at some with the same awe all those years ago.....

Just the sheer size overwhelms you - pillars, doors etc. no wonder the ordinary man bowed in submission when you entered this spiritual place.Once in, you are stunned by the physical presence - a huge dome, arches, windows and murals.Only in Rome can you see this relic of Roman glory.

You get an audio guide of the things you can visit without an audio guide for free. You don't go to a different basilica or something like that. You have an audio guide for the sculptures/paintings that you already have on display for free. You can read the cards for every sculpture/painting for free without this audio guide.
We visited the Pantheon which was really crowded.  Compare to some of the other monuments in Rome, the inside is fairly basic.

So full of history, paid approx £5 each for audio tour, absolutely terrible if it had cost any more I would have complained, the atmosphere of the place itself though is phenomenal a must see for anyone visiting Rome
Very good. Do not miss a visit to the Pantheon of Rome. It's a pity there's so much people for the visit. It's worth having the guide

By far and away the best place we saw on our trip - absolutely stunningly beautiful - the building, the design, the light, everything - just absolutely epic, so glad I got to see it in such a great setup.

We were just walking around Rome and we came across this amazing building. We had plans to visit it during our stay, so we decided to go in at that time. This has to be one of the most beautiful places I have ever walked into. All the details in the sculptures and the beauty of the paintings is just amazing.  A must if you go to Rome!
This is worth a pit stop to see the marvelous architecture. It’s also one of the free places to see in Rome.
Dont forget when visiting there is a dress code of covering up and not wearing a vest. Beautiful building

We were told the holes in the front facade were caused by a hand granade during the Second World war. I explained from an old 1944 photo that it must have happened before they expected the French in town. The building is amazing and still holds on to the mystique from when it was first built.
Great tour with knowledgeable guide, we had time to explore on the outside and extra time on the inside, truly amazing site and want to go back to see it again.
Grand temple, glad we were able to view. Around the Pantheon is a busy piazza and cafe's. A must see when in Rome.
A wonderful beautiful place that deserves a visit while in Rome. No lines or entry fee. Amazing art and a peaceful place to be still.

We took our tour in the morning and so glad we did! I wouldn’t think about a tour if it’s been raining as the marble steps would become treacherous! We were there first thing in the morning and it was magical.
It closes very early in the evening. If you want to see it from inside, don't wait till late in the evening. Lots of activities keep happening outside.
Words can’t describe this gem from 2nd century AD. If you can only see one building in Rome, this should be it.

The Pantheon by itself is a beautiful structure, but it is located in one of the best neighborhoods in Rome, near the Piazza Navona, Fontana di Trevi and coffee shop Tazza d'Oro (the granita there is a must). There was a rumor that they would start ticketing the Pantheon, but that is false, as we were just there.  To marvel at the occulus, the tombs of Raphael and Italy's historic figures, is a magical experience.
When you are in Rome, you can expect crowds but do not miss a chance to tour the Pantheon. A true marvel.

A visit that at first we did not consider and in the end it turned out to be a past. Super well preserved and cared for immerses you in history. It is a quick visit that you can make at any time, plus with that audio guide is great and very simple without depending on groups to be waiting, however, shoulders and knees depends on the vigilante, covered.

By far, the best cathedral of Rome with a long history and unique structure. Place in front of the cathedral is good for quite few photo ops and the gold plated collection of bust figures of globe, bird, animal, man and woman are worth watching. Dome is open to the sky and it rains inside for a specific radius which gets drained through 24 small holes.
I was surprised with everything I saw in it. History and culture side by side. It wasn’t full and the little line was very fast.
Just like all ancient structures, you will marvel in its beauty and intricacy. Not overly crowded like all the other must see places.
This is another must visit in Rome. Great for photography. Packed in the morning, but it relaxes later in the day when is perhaps best to visit. A monumental building, old and imposing.
It was amazing to see it but it’s kind of not kid friendly, so we had to see it fast and go outside because of our little daughter 😁
we really happy to we can saw this place. i loved italy to visit when its too people because with a people you can shared your joy and near in rome we love to viist everything.

Amazing to see a piece of history in Rome. We went in August around the 15th, which is the holidays in Rome... so it wasn't very crowded. Going inside was a breeze, and inside was equally gorgeous. Lots of gelato places near the area. Hope to visit again!
Incredible building preserved over the years with modern development all around. Walking distance from the steps & Trevi.
Why pay for it? Entry is free. The Audio-Guide Service is not found and unknown to staff. Extremely disappointed. But the place is beautiful, has many explanations for the visit.

It is not so crowded as the colosseum or the Fontana di Trevi, which makes it more enjoyable. Not so hot inside, so the visit is nice and peaceful. No dress restrictions as long as you are not excessively exposed.
The construction of the building is fantastic. Admiration for the Roman engineers is unbounded but cannot comment on the religious aspect of the Pantheon.

Was gorgeous to view from the outside however I forgot to bring suitable covering to allow myself to go inside. If you find yourself in a similar situation there were plenty of men outside selling scarves.

If you enjoy history and architecture, the Pantheon is a must-see! The architectural feat that is the Pantheon (and its dome) is pretty amazing. Personally, I find its pagan history more interesting than its more recent Christian history, but it is all part of the evolution. Plus, it is free, does not not take a lot of time out of the day, and is close to other sites.
Right by the hotel I stayed so there goes my first spot in Rome. Pretty amazing building. Different vibe day to night :)
Complete and intact roman building. It’s like you have been transported back in time. Wonderful spectacle.
If you like history you need to come to this place, and of course this is must see in Rome. There's a lot about the Roman history in this place

A true marvel of the past and a monument to the glory of the city of Rome, the Pantheon is truly magnificent. Its domed roof is one of a kind and the interior decorations are breathtaking.If you come to Rome, never ever miss visiting Pantheon!!

Went there early Sunday am. No waiting time. Ceiling is high and nice! I did not do the audio visit and was out of there after 15-20 minutes. Good to go see but plan the visit with a combined visit to Trevi fountain
There is so much history at Pantheon and surprisingly it is free. Try Rick Steve's Audio and you'll have great time at Pantheon. Must visit!!

This really is a must to visit when in Rome.  And no queues to get in!!We were fortunate to visit in the afternoon when it was sunny so got to see the sunlight coming through the Oculus.The inside of the Pantheon is beautiful.  You can hire audio guides but we chose not to - make sure you have your own wire earphones with you!

This is one of the best preserved Roman temples. Formerly a temple, it’s been used as a church since the 7th century. To think of all the history associated with this Roman temple is unfathomable. This historic place of worship has stood the test of time. The large columns and abundance of marble add to the beautiful of the temple. The detail in the design of this structure is awe-inspiring. Make sure to glance up at the dome top ceiling. It’s nice that admission was free.

Beautiful piece of history! Crowded, but no lines and free admission. Outside is impressive and the inside - beautiful. There is some signage around to explain the history. No food, no restrooms. The piazza where the Pantheon is located has restaurants; the surrounding area has a grocery store, gift shops, and places to grab a bite.

We've traveled to Rome a few times over the past few years.  We love the city, and it will always be dear to me.  The Italian government does a good job of trying to maintain their historical monuments and buildings, so that said, this was actually the first time we saw the Pantheon without any scaffolding in front!  Ladies, if you plan to go inside, please remember to cover shoulders and knees...it is considered a church.
You've seen it in history books, and you need to see it in person, but there is really not a lot to it.  Make sure that ladies cover shoulders and knees to enter. No admission fee.
I love that it is free and easy to get to. Stay for a little to know about it's architecture ingenuity.
Such a fascinating experience learning about the historic Pantheon. It is also a Catholic church with Mass on Sundays. Nearby is Piazza Navona.
really nice building but go early in morning when nobody is there! Hole in the roof is pretty cool also as rain gets in.

The Pantheon shows its age and none the less it is still a sight to behold. located close to food and the Spanish step and Gelato that you can't get enough of. It was one of my favorite places to visit in our trip.
Great for a visit and take some pictures. Pleasant for the eyes. Recommended to visit if you are in rome

We did the Official Audio your via the iOS app... a warning though, it doesn’t support AirPods so we had to hold the iPhones like we were on calls!The Pantheon is an amazing building steeped in history and the Audio app does a brilliant job of giving information in a clear manner.We visited mid afternoon on a Sunday and it was quite busy but in the middle of August I would imagine it is always busy. It was free to enter when we visited, the App was free to download via the App Store and the Audio tour was £1.99 to purchase, so a very cheap tour indeed!

Like the title says. It’s really a must see and awe inspiring.  To think of the time in history when it was built and seeing how beautiful it remains. Busy, but superbly managed.  Hit up Piazza Novona before or after. Catch the sun popping through the Coppola.

We were literally here for less than 5 minutes.  The oculus is cool to see but the rest of it I was indifferent to.Ladies, please remember it has been converted to a Catholic church so no exposed knees, shoulders or cleavage.
The Pantheon is awe inspiring. The guide was not. I couldn't hear him nor could he wait for everyone to catch up before carrying talking.
A must see in Rome for anyone! The age and architecture of the structure will leave you awestruck. Easy and no cost entrance.
Probably the most complete Roman building in the city, just in case for some reason you are tempted to give this one a miss, don't!
A beautiful church, formerly a pagan temple. Located in a hopping square with a fountain, an obelisk, and many eateries nearby. Pretty at night.
Definitely my favorite landmark in Rome.  the genius of the architecture, the coolness of the place in the eat of august, the beautiful art. enchanting
Imagine a Massive 2000 year old building.  The dome is so impressive. You are walking on a floor that is that old. And it has lasted in tack!! WOW
Absolutely wonderful. Amazing and breathtaking hard to believe the temple is so old. Wonderful Piazza with nice places to eat/drink and very friendly locals. Well worth the visit.

The outside of the Pantheon doesn't give the inside justice. Beautiful place of worship with the mist impressive sculptures and paintings. This was the last place to visit on our list but was a complete delight as the finale to our visit to Rome. The place is free to visit and we went in the last hour of opening when all the crowds had almost disappeared.

How such an old and huge structure survived for thousands of years is truly awe-inspiring! It is one of the oldest religious buildings constructed by the Romans which was later converted into a church. When you stand in the middle of the interior looking up at the ceiling, the sight is unforgettable.
One of the oldest sites I have ever visited, and awe inspiring, well worth the trip and free to enter. Time your visit to avoid the crowds (go early) as it can get busy.

Worth a visit,although always busy is free.The evening we visited was rainy so the queues to enter where even longer thatn usual but if you are in the area worhts.The ladies must be dressed appropriately and the most impressive in the interior is the openn roof and the way the sunlight spreads throughout.
Small compared to other churches in Rome, but not lacking in architecture, artwork, or sculptures.  Everything about the Pantheon was truly amazing.
Visited in August so a lot of people, but got straight inside. It doesn't cost anything if you don't took any guided tour.We didn't take a tour because we just wanted to see the sealing and Oculus.
It was very cool to walk around in this ancient building that is still intact. The ceiling and oculus were amazing.
Lovely place to visit Clean Weather was scorching Really busy easy to get to We used the tram and buses Recommend you have comfy Shoes .did a lot of walking xx
We were staying right around the corner and every time we walked past, I was amazed at just how spectacular it is. Visit it

The place where you fall in live with amazing architecture and history.Something you can't miss if you are visiting charming and amazing city of Rome. There is no words to describe feeling when you see for the first time this monumental building
What an impressive structure! Absolutely must see and if possible, with a knowledgeable guide!  Architectural marvel and  stunning design - a standing proof of how advanced the ancient Romans were!
Do not miss the opportunity to see the Pantheon!  Incredible.  The preservation is amazing and such detail!

This building is awe-inspiring. Read up on its history to appreciate the building techniques and dimensions. The oculus (skylight) gives incredible light and considering this building is 2000 years old, it's downright genius. Free entry and many interesting frescos and details makes this a must-see on your Rome bucket list. Appreciate its surroundings and check out the back where you can see ancient column remnants
Visit if you have time. 10 to 15 minutes only. Take a public bus cheaper and goes closer than hop on hop off buses

The Pantheon is free.  Depending upon the time of your arrival, you may have to stand in line to get admittance.  It is well worth the wait.  Note that the Pantheon is a place of worship.  Shoulders and knees MUST be covered.  (There are street vendors standing at the entrance selling shawls to women who are being turned away with bare shoulders.)  Once inside, it is important to remember that the Pantheon is a place of worship and to be quiet.  As well, no flash photography or cell phone usage is permitted.The architecture, the paintings, the entire surroundings are awe inspiring.  The feeling of standing in the middle and absorbing the surroundings is beyond description.  You need to go there and feel it for yourself.  There is no excuse for not going as it is in town, free and, if you select your time right, there is no line up.
One of the most famous monuments in Rome. So majestic it looks like the pictures you take are photoshoped! Haha. Free entrance.

We loved this place. We walked to it after visiting the Trevi Fountain on a very hot afternoon. We didn't have a guide however you can google all the info and a lot of it is provided inside. Amazing historic building definitely worth seeing. The dome is unbelievable. It is free to enter and wasn't overcrowded, definitely worth a visit.
What a gorgeous building this is - free to enter and not too crowded. really worth a visit while you are in Rome.

This is a free, must-see attraction in Rome.  It's mind-blowing how beautifully designed and constructed it is, particularly for a 2000 year-old structure.  It's been maintained the entire time, so unlike most of the Roman structures this one is fully intact, though it has changed from a Roman temple to a church.  It does not take too long to walk through, and though crowded on a summer afternoon it was not so crazy you could not move around.  Must see!
A masterpiece of Roman architecture perfectly in line with classical principles. Even more interesting is that it is still one of the largest domes in the world.
The Pantheon may not have the outward grandeur as the Colosseum but it doesn’t take away what an architectural phenomenon this building is. It’s a must-see!!
Great to see the Pantheon for the second time, seeing this great building never gets old. Quick  and easy to get in with no long queues ....
Guide was passionate and very knowledgeable. It was such a pleasing 2 hour tour - I learned so much and really enjoyed it.

We went in July and got there before 10 am which is the best time to get there before the crowds and lines start.  Also since there is no air conditioning, it was cooler then. There is a guard checking as you enter and some people were stopped for having bare shoulders.  Shorts weren't a problem and not all people were stopped, but to be safe don't wear tank tops.  It is good to have the background of the pantheon so that you know the significance of what you are looking at.  It is an impressive structure.

The Pantheon 🏛 was such a beautiful building. Whilst from the outside, it doesn’t look overly impressive, it’s surrounded by lots of restaurants and a nice square. Inside the Pantheon 🏛 , which does have free entry, it’s just stunning. My brother, who’s 16 and finds a negative 👎🏻 thing about everything, didn’t have a bad thing to say about the Pantheon 🏛, making it the best thing we saw when in Rome. You are meant to be silent whilst inside, something many people weren’t doing, so a staff member actually shushed them through a microphone 🎤 which I thought was quite funny 😂 as it did make those who weren’t being silent finally be respectful.

Inspiring architecture and a remarkable structure made all the more impressive considering how long ago it was constructed.Nice few bars you can sit and have a drink with a view of the Pantheon, however it is a very busy tourist attraction - but well worth the visit.
One of the oldest  remaining in tack buildings of ancient Rome. Beautiful architecture perfectly preserved. Free to enter is a bonus.

Amazing experience, very interesting history & well-preserved alcoves of detail. Incredible that it's just there amongst all the other "regular" buildings... Definitely go first thing in the morning though to avoid crowds!
Next to the Vatican, our favorite part of visit. Beautiful to look at and an architectural wonder. We could have spent all day at the Pantheon.

An amazing historical site. You can go in without paying and just enjoy the beauty inside. Unfortunately, it has become a large tourist attraction, so some people to forget that there are people who are sitting and praying. Please be mindful. It would be nice if there was a bit of security inside. But it is a sight to see.
Amazing 2,000-year-old building, showcasing in a way few others manage the layers of religion and history.  An absolute marvel of engineering.
This site leaves you speechless. It's not really for kids but for adults interested in history it is stoning.
Right in the old town; can be packed with crowds but is free of charge and easily accessible. Plus a nice square it is located at.
A huge building, an amazing one. Full of history. I recommend read a little on the pantheon’s history before coming. Best to visit in the morning.
Turn the corner and enjoy the wow factor. So clever, so old, so beautiful and still relevant. Grab a gelato and soak up the atmosphere.
If you can only see one ancient site in Rome, this is the one you should go to.  It is simply staggeringly beautiful.  The coffered ceiling is amazing and the dome is the largest in Rome.
This place get a lot of traffic.  It's nice and you can get through very quickly if you go early in the day.  If not, you risk very long lines and much harder to see with so many in there.
I founf the Pantheon to be an amazing construction. It is definetly worth seeing with your won eyes. Somehow you feel so small and young as you see how massive and old this building is.

Free to enter, but cover your shoulders and knees( it is a church).Spectacular marble and statues but don’t forget to look up at the dome roof that is open to the elements at the top. Spectacularly huge both inside and out.
It’s free, super easy to get in. Make sure you cover your shoulders. It’s beautiful inside and worth just to have a look.

Really worth checking out. We ate outside of the Pantheon one night and it was such a treat! There are numerous entertainers that come out to entertain the guests at night. We were situated right in the center of the Piazza and a Clown had our whole restaurant laughing over his antics. People often tell tourists to stay away from these places but we found them to be where we enjoyed our time the most.

The Pantheon is such a powerful looking building,it doesn’t have all the prancing horses and sculptures that many later buildings have but it’s shape and age are just as impressive.many people queuing but managed to get in and out quickly.very calming once inside.

Just like everything else In Rome, one minute you’re waking down the street and the next you’re faced with something historical and breathtaking. The outside looks like it would have done when the Romans were walking about in their togas but the inside looks like you might find a Borgia or a Medici sitting at one of the pews praying. As our visit is August and we went in the middle of the day it was super busy. The pantheon is free to get in to. You can’t be on your phones or have any hats on as you walk in. It was impressive and I’m glad I got to go .

This is a must see place if you are into architecture and history, as it is the oldest domed structure that is in use and still in tact.  There was no charge for entry, no hats can be worn inside.it is not as strict as the Vatican.  It is simple and beautiful. The dome inside is lit by natural light and it is amazing.  The piazza outside had a band performing when we went.  You are not allowed to sit on steps of fountain outside.  A simple structure with great significance!
Incredible to visit especially considering it's age! It is amazin how well the Romans built their structures.
Fabulous historic site. Prepare for lots of uneven walking. Wonderful history lesson. Children and teens seem easily bored.
Ended up being quite a walk from our rental but worth every step. Just amazing in every way when you think about the detail, craftsmanship, etc. It was busy the day we went but manageable.

Although the building itself is Rome's best preserved edifice of the Roman Empire, and whose cupola is an engineering triumph, it is wrecked by its subsequent Christianisation: substandard works of pious art destroy what should be a great experience.

We attended Saturday 5pm Mass in the Pantheon,. It was a lovely experience, with a visiting Canadian choir taking part. During the mass,it rained, and the rain fell in through the Oculus in the roof....amazing!   This building is 2000 years old, still the widest dome in the world, and virtually the entire structure, doors included, is original! Almost as a Roman might have seen it inside! For me, the Pantheon is the number one sight of Rome, ahead of the Colosseum or St Peters. And the part of town where it stands is one of the most characteristic and fun areas of the city, being on ly 5 minutes from lovely Piazza Navona.
A must see for any visit to Rome. It was great to look around and not that busy. The architecture is stung to look at both from the outside and the inside.
This is a must see building in Rome.  It was busy but not excessively so - we went twice and would definitely return.

Visited this church/monument many many years ago and it instantly struck a massive chord when I walked back through the doors. The architecture and peacefulness, despite a few hundred people inside was breathtaking.

This is one of the many places I visited during my stay in Rome. It was built in around 1000 AD. The architecture is stunning.  It currently appears to be a Catholic Church, but according to one of the guides in the Pantheon, it has been served several functions and been several things over the centuries.  The exterior is beautiful, but the interior is absolutely stunning.  Don't miss this gem.

What it must have taken to construct this building is not imaginable. Tremendous artwork and marble floor. Such an enormous dome with central hole for the sun to shine through and move writhe walls illuminating sections as it moves around the perimeter.
no ticket or line! loved this place. they are not strict for women on attire, but men just need to remove their hats. super easy historical site

You can seen the dome of the Pantheon from many vantage spots. However, when you walk into the piazza and see it for the first time it is amazing. It was free to visit on the day, inside the different altars are really interesting. But the wonder of the open part of the ceiling is breathtaking. A must visit site.

The Pantheon was more impressive and beautiful than I expected. The streets leading to it are full of character and charm. The building is on a gorgeous piazza that offers good places to take in the size and perfect dimensions of this monument. Inside, without doubt the most harmonious space I have been to. The dome is incredible in its height and elegance and the various tombs, Raphael, Vittorio Emmanuele, etc are interesting touch points of the history of this land. I spent an hour walking around and then sitting on one of the pews (this is still a church, Hadrian turning in his grave) and thus it was shocking to see how most tourist behaved. Thankfully, there were cops keeping an eye on visitors and calling out their rude behavior. No wonder Roman authorities are ramping up fines for rude behavior. Despite this, my visit to the Pantheon was amazing and this building is a true treasure in Rome.
I have often visualized the Pantheon when meditating, so it was very moving to see it “in reality.  And I was surprised there was no entrance ticket.

We went in summer thus very warm weather. So we wore comfortable attire. Girls should cover their shoulders and knees while guys should remove their caps. This piece of structure is already 1900 years old and it still standing today!! Amazing that you get to enter to an old building and how ancient romans design this to withstand the test of time.
Another place to come early to avoid the crowds. For women, bring a scarf if you plan to wear sleeveless as there are guards who check.

My husband and I walked inside and were amazed at what we saw! The artwork inside was breath taking and we'll preserved. The only slight annoyance was people talking next to the 'Be Silent' signs but luckily the art work made up for this! Make sure shoulders are covered as they were quite strict about who could and couldn't enter.
We accidentally came across this as we wasn’t expecting to visit here I’m glad we did we sat in the square before and chilled looking at it then inside is amazing a must to visit

This monument is easy to access and is a must-see while in Rome. Free to enter it is awe-inspiring to see the columns at the entrance and the oculus is a marvel when the light shines through. There are several famous tombs there to see and areas for prayer. It is a holy sight so treat it as such. There are areas to get audio information (like a phone booth) for some coins, but if you have your phone you can download an audio tour from your phone (Rick Steves?) and do your own tour for free!

I had heard so much about Pantheon and it met all my expectations when it comes to the building itself. Personally I find the graves of the former Italian kings too dominating, they could be somewhere else. Raphael, who also has his grave here, which is more moderate, has meant so much more to the country than the kings ever did.
Such a reverent place, the atmosphere is just so peaceful. Ancient art and religious artefacts are on display for all to see.
It’s one of Rome’s oldest buildings but most impressive. Check out the oculus during daylight. The tombs of Raphael was cool.

Pantheon is spectacular for so many reasons. The fact that it is an intact structure that survived for the last 2000+ years alone makes it remarkable. Its architecture is stunning, he engineering involved in its construction is mind boggling, the play of light created by he oculus is amazing and its free to enter for all. Awesome!

As in all attractions in Rome, the Pantheon is very crowded, but it should not be missed.  The complexity of the construction is incredible. The dome construction is impressive for the century it was built. The inside is beautiful. It is worth every minute you spend there.

As a home builder I'm blown away overtime I see the Pantheon. The engineering that went into the construction not to mention the fact it has been in continuous use since it was built almost 1900 years ago.
that was good and intersting , i enjoy very much , every place has its own story...it was amazing experience
Free to visit which in a bonus in Rome. Beautiful decorated,worth pay a visit. You need to follow dressing code:no short trousers and no sleeveless.

The Pantheon is the place for all gods of the Roman times. The art of construction in bricks and stones. a very impressive DOMe and a façade one will have as a print for roman buildings for the rest of one`s life. Avoid Summer visits, since it is too hot and the downtown pollution is not a good thing.
We loved that in Rome, you can be among trendy shops and then suddenly - The Pantheon! The combination of ancient and modern is fascinating. Short wait to get in. Absolutely beautiful.
if you like history and architecture wonders this is a must see. the pictures does not do justice. you will be left in awwww

A premier site. The building is amazingly preserved, the marble floors are beautiful, and the dome is breathtaking from the inside. Around the interior are several tombs, including Italy's forefathers and Rafael the painter. The altar up front and some side altars are pretty, and it is of course a functioning church, but in my opinion the architecture is the reason to go - the building holds a lot of people with no interior support columns, which is just incredible. There can be a lot of people in there, but they filter in and out quickly.
Awesome. The building looks like it was built yesterday and there you have it with its 2 millennia behind it. A wonder, a trip to the past.

Always suggestive to enter this temple chapel church built on a temple of more than 2000 years ago 'transformed but always a magical place. Do not miss behind it stone square where with LASER lights evoke moments of life 2000 years ago with music and sounds. Beautiful initiative of the City of Rome
Tempted to skip with limited time in Rome but took the walk in late afternoon before closing. Amazing and indescribable, photograph would not do it justice.
Just finishing a 2 week trip through Italy and the Pantheon is a must. We had a lot of paid guides but this one we skipped.  They have a great audio tour for a few euros that was fine.
Great to walk around and check out all the beautiful works of art. Aside from the beautiful art, we loved the sun streaming through the hole in the ceiling.

This is probably the best preserved of Rome's ancient buildings because in one form or another it's been in continuous use since it was first built around the year 126AD. Obviously first built as a Roman temple it later was converted into a Christian Church. It's s must see for all visitors to Rome but especially those who love Rome's ancient architecture and history.

I did not participate in the Audio tour, I'm certain there's a huge benefit to that. In the exterior courtyard stands an obilesque with an egyptian inscriptions, but a crucifix mounted atop. The Pantheon building, like most of these Roman treasures is a sight to behold both inside and out. You (ladies) are asked to 'cover up' should you expose too much shoulder or back. (Even more strict at the Vatican!). The 'art' that are the statues, alter, painting, etc., is in the same caliber to that of the Vatican, but on a smaller scale.
Don't just look at the Pantheon from the outside. Do yourself a favor and go inside. Most amazing structure I've seen.
Beautiful roman-style dome that currently serves as a place of worship. It is meticulously decorated!

Expect crowds, but the incredible preservation of a Roman, pagan space by the Catholic church is well worth seeing, when much of the colosseum and forum was dismantled to create newer sites like Saint Peters. Its a majestic space that deserves to be seen.
A beautiful huge building and of the most important monuments in Rome.The entrance is free and it was not very crowded compared to other buildings.It was a great place to visit

What a brilliant building and so well preserved. Apart from its history it is an engineering enigma as the concrete roof defies  engineering logic as to how it has stayed up. Whilst its quite easy to visit this site your self doing it as part of a tour will provide you with many interesting facts.
Visit to be repeated several times, both for the beauty of the place, for the preservation, and finally, for the history that it embodies '.The audio guide is exhaustive and well told.

We stayed near the Pantheon which was nice because we got to see it in a variety of settings (rain, sun, overcast). Very cool to see in person and especially in the rain because it comes down in through the hole in the roof. Worth seeing regardless of weather.
Amazing and don't forget coffee from that famous coffee place round the corner!!!  I don't remember the name but it's literally round the corner!

The Pantheon is a mystical building. Still now the largest dome with no iron support inside the concrete. The concrete is a special one. It is also a kind of sundial and at noon the circle of the sun, created by the hole in the center of the dome, falls by the entrance: above it in winter  solstice, on the floor under  the door in summer solstice and right in the middle of it in the equinoxes.It contains almuall the geometrical shapes: a cylindrical base, topped by a half circle dome (the hight and the width of the dome are the same) so a perfect sphere, the front “pronaos” colonnade is a rectangle coveruby a triangle and the dept of the colonnade is like the height, so we also have the squareIt was a temple dedicated to all the gods (of ancient Roman time), turned into a Christian church, which is still now.Great place to visit

hold your breath while you are entering the gate which has been prepared for gods to enter. you will see a high roof with gigantic walls and this architecture is breathtaking. Just enter and enjoy this place.
Amazing structure. Huge dome. You feel small inside. Plus knowing how old it is. People were inside 2000 years ago. Must see.
Beautiful building with beautiful inside , nice that there was no queuing as with a lot of things in Rome. Again ladies shoulders need to be covered .
great location, lovely piazza with lots of good eateries, casual, great shopping in little laneways to be explored
The Pantheon is definitely one of the best monument that you’ll find in Rome. It’s a must visit. It’s absolutely magical.
They built concrete palaces when the rest of the world was living in mud huts. In a sense, the world was catching up until 1880 or so...

If you read Da Vinci Code, you will dream away for a moment. If you did not, then I hope it will get you to read it because it is like steeping in ancient times of Rome. I went there several times since I went t Rome that many times but it has that magnet attraction for me. I don't stay there very long but I still believe it is worth seeing!

There wasn't a long wait at all to get into the Pantheon (maybe 15 minutes?) even though other places in Rome we'd been that day were overwhelmed with people.  No idea why as this was a highlight for us.  It's an icon of Rome & absolutely a must visit.  The kids were totally intrigued about the hole in the roof - yes, it's not glass, it's a hole!! We'd been 20 years earlier & I'd forgotten how beautiful it is inside.  Furthermore, it had a lot of artwork on display which made it even more interesting.  Totally worth a visit.  Free, quick & enjoyable - what's not to love?

Pictures do not do it justice you must go see it.  We were awe struck by this amazingly beautiful place.  Very clean inside and you must be dressed appropriately it is a place of worship, please be respectful when you go, no hats, no shorts, and cover your shoulders  ;-)

the possibility of meeting today in the past, with this architectural jewel that continues to amaze visitors today who, knowing the building secrets of El Pantheón, continue to be amazed at its history and its secrets
Go to the Pantheon first thing in the morning or late evening.  It gets very busy around lunch time.The history is amazing,  Its good to sit and take in the amazing structure.   You dont have to pay.
Now a church, this iconic piece of architecture is a must on any itinerary.Go early or late to avoid the crowds of tourists.
We went but couldn’t go in bc our shoulders weren’t covered. I think I’m the height of tourist season they are more strict. I regret not going in—I hear it’s worth it.
After you grab a sip from the fountain and enjoy the structure, spend a few minutes inside the museum. Lot's to do before you get inundated by the souvenir vendors.
This is a must see when visiting Rome. I am so glad we visited and went inside. Although we didn't do a guided tour and simply walked around, it was an incredible thing to see!
This historical building is a must see in Rome. There is a good flow of visitors and no entrance fee required.

The Pantheon is a must when visiting Rome.  It's such a surprise to see this beautiful building when while walking down the street.  There is no entrance fee. Rick Steves offers a good free walking audio tour that was great! Enjoy

This is one of the most impressive tourist sites in Rome and it is free.The queues move very quickly when there is one so don’t be put off. Whilst there is a definite line going in you can branch out and fully explore. Lots of tour groups but they don’t tend to stay too long.
this is free and breath taking - It is somewhat crowded, however it does not take a super long time to visit.
A must see for those who go to Rome. The temple does not disappoint and is one of the best Roman temples to visit. The dome is spectacular as is the splendid interior. And it’s free?
Religious history everywhere in here.   Short trip in and out is enough.   could use a tour guides but easily could read the stuff on the wall yourself
Obviously, there are so many things in Rome to see, but this one doesn't take long to see, so it's a no-brainer to step inside and look around. It's a historical icon and well worth your time.

The Pantheon was built in 125 AD as a monument to the Roman gods. Once the Catholics established themselves in Rome it became a basilica with a number of memorials to various people, including Queen Margherita and her husband King Umberto and the famous artist Raphael.
We took part in a free tour which was great but went back on our own too which I think is a prefect combination.  This place and its history is outstanding really

A must see in Rome, we visited around lunchtime, virtually no queue. The architecture is impressive, a feat of engineering. The inside is beautiful and it feels very peaceful to wander around. There are several tombs inside which are impressive. And don't forget the dome, just amazing and much bigger than I expected.

The Pantheon is a must see when in Rome.  It is unbelievably well preserved and an amazing building.  It was not too busy in the early evening so we were able to admire the dome as well as the engineering ingenuity in the floors.  As in the majority of churches in Rome, make sure your shoulders are covered.
A very interesting place. Looks so big and imposing from the outside yet so unassuming and compact in its inside. Visit is for free so make sure you do stop there.

This is an amazing ancient site and well worth your time as it's free to enter. The dome is very impressive and appears much bigger than in photos. It also contains the tomb of Raphael. Despite how busy the Pantheon is you can still have a very enjoyable visit. The staff were also very helpful. This is essential for any visit to Rome.

THis was an unexpected thing to see on an old historic building. A hole on the ceiling where rains inside and yet, the marble and inside of this building are in a great form. Right in town, well located and its free! I would say a must if you are in the area.
Didn't get a chance to go inside!  Missed due to lack of time. Saw it from outside last evening. Do plan time for each spot before starting the day !
It has to be seen when you're in Rome and the Pantheon is a stunning sight. Make sure you address appropriately as this is a holy place but the mausoleum is an incredible place.

The Pantheon never ceases to amaze me -- it's my favourite building in Rome. The experience is somewhat off putting during peak season when hordes of tourist are visiting but it's still one of the must see places in Rome.
No bear shoulders, so bring a shawl.  Come during the day to appreciate the light coming in.  Then head over to the rooftop bar at the Hotel Minerva for a spritz and some spectacular views.

What an impressive building. Immense, old, sacred, everything.It’s allways crowded but pls don’t watch the people. Look up and study the walls, the hole and remember: people built this over 2000 years ago.

We visited the Pantheon after a whole day of walking around Rome and we were not disappointed... Although it was very busy, we were able to get inside and have a good look around with ease. The queue looked quite long but moved quickly and we was in within minutes. There is a fountain outside with drinking water which is cold and refreshing... This place is a must when visiting Rome.
Free to enter.  There maybe a queue but it is brief to get in.  depending on your purpose  expect to spend 5-20 min.  when we went it was late afternoon so the sun cast on the dome ceiling.

Many thanks Pete, it was a great walking tour including the Spanish Steps, the Trevi Fountain, the Pantheon, ( where we met friends who had just arrived in Rome much to our surprise. Thanks Tatiana). Pete then continued the tour to Piazza Navona, the way he spoke about Rome showed how much he loved the city, & I think he made us fall further in love as well. Thank you Pete, it was a great afternoon and night
It's one of the antiquity wonder still standing for your admiration. Perfect shaped, with a huge dome built of Roman bricks, what masters masons worked there?
It's the best preserved site from ancient Rome, the architecture, the scale, the preservation is amazing. And it's free.
Nice square in front of the pantheon, but if you plan to visit the pantheon from inside and a have a tour, I strongly recommend to plan it in advance and the line can be very long.
It is a marvel to remember that this building is a thousand years plus old.  It is amazing to behold.  As with everything in Rome, spoilt by the crowds.
Not what I thought it would be at all, the whole place is huge and on a massive scale.  Seeing Raphael's tomb gave me goose bumps and just another stunning and amazing example of genius engineering!

This has to be in the ekist for any visit to Rome. Be amazed by 1,900 years of history in one building and such a fantastic feat of engineering to produce the dome which for centuries was the largest in the world, until people worked out how to copy it.
A MUST see in Rome - the specimen that is the Pantheon - an engineering feat for all time - besides the beauty  - a true marvel of the time and a highlight not to miss

Small wait outside the building, however the line moves quickly. Tucked in the Piazza della Rotunda, the architecture of this building is awesome - both inside an out. You enter though 40' tall x 15' wide red and white granite columns and large bronze doors, then the magnificent dome appears! The dome is perfectly symmetrical 142' from floor to roof and 142' from side to side (how did they do it!? Have a seat an just look up and around for a little while. The Pantheon also contains the tombs of several famous people such as Raphael, King's Victor and Emmanuel II and Umberto I.I used Rick Steves Audio Europe app and listened to his tour of The Pantheon - very informative. Oh, and btw did I say the app and the entry into this building are free? Enjoy!
Loved the ceiling and the quietness once inside. Sat on the benches inside and just had a quiet moment. Beautiful.
How often do you get to walk into a building this old and witness history? Pretty cool. A free must do in Rome.

Lovely place. I could spend hours staring at the walls and taking in the sunlight. There's not usually a line for entrance, but make sure your bottoms are at least knee-length and that you're covering your shoulders (which is pretty much standard for all Italian churches).
The light shining in through the ceiling is phenomenal. Even our young kids enjoyed the Pantheon and seeing Raphaels tomb as well.

Since admission is free we were going to check this out ourselves. James with “Explore Italy Tours,” was in front of the Pantheon offering guided tours. We used of their guides, (a wonderful woman from Finland originally) and it was money well worth spending. It’s something to just look at a beautiful building but quite another to be told where the granite came from and stories about the people buried in the church. We also learned about the building and areas importance around the Pantheon.
You should see this with a guideSo much to see and learnThe place is magical in the evening tooVery touristic Look for holes in the floor:)
Long line, but worth the wait! The building also offers a hood amount of shade for during the hot summer days. Bonus, look close to the roof! There's still bullet holes in the building !

Considering how busy the Pantheon can get, it was surprisingly peaceful and a lovely place to sit and admire the architectural features.We were impressed that it is free entry, though we would have liked to have learned more about the history of the structure when we were there... then again having lots of sign boards around could have taken away from the sheer experience of being in such a lovely building.

My daughter was stopped on entry to the pantheon, strappy top and short shorts. Had to buy a wrap for 5euros to cover shoulders (rightly so). Kept walking when they said shorts unacceptable as other visitors had much skimpier ones!  Daughter was blown away by hole in roof, shame it was not raining as supposed to be spectacular.  Well worth visit
The dome and the building itself are amazing.  Beautiful inside and stunning.  Lines move quickly so don't be intimidated.   Free access so why would you not!!

Nearly 2000 years old, this Roman building still stands the test of time in the heart of Rome. Perfectly designed you stand in awe as you walk around the circular building and stare up at the oculus. Don't forget to visit the tomb of Raphael!

There are few structures that remain virtually wholly intact from the age of the Emperors and the Pantheon is one of them.  Stepping inside is like stepping back in time particularly as you gaze upwards.  The sheer size of the dome overwhelms you (following its construction in 118AD it remained the largest dome for more than 1,700 years!) and the oculus at the peak is a very unique feature (opens up the heavens).  The Pantheon was saved from oblivion when it was converted to a Christian church in the 600s and several memorials have been built into the side including two former kings and the artist Raphael.  Be sure to walk around the exterior to see the amazing Roman brickwork.
The Pantheon is a Roman hallmark! You can't miss this historic monument and Roman Temple converted into a Catholic Church. Proper dress code is mandatory.

We visited the Pantheon in July 2019 as this was on our list of "things to see in Rome" It is just an amazing architectural wonder..fascinating and very interesting indeed. It is free to enter the Pantheon which is an added bonus. We will certainly return..

This is for sure one of the fastest and cheapest things you can visit in Rome. Wait  until 16:00 and there will be nearly no people even on a busy day. The building is very impressive and don’ t forget to look up!
The good thing it‘s free and when you go in the evening there are not many people.It‘s worth to pay it a visit
Wery beautiful historical monument. In center of coupola there is a whole, an interseting is that rain is not faling inside!

Having heard there were queues were were pleasantly surprised that there weren't any (at around 12.30). The building (both inside and outside) is amazing and a quick read up on a website or two helps to give the history. Women are asked to cover up a little but the security were fairly relaxed so most people were fine.

While a good example of the re-purposing of a pagan site into a Catholic Church and a great example of Roman engineering and building skills, there wasn’t a lot to see inside.  We were more impressed with Vittoriano than the Pantheon.
Go early to avoid the crowds. It is free to visit. Be sure to check out the sides where the tomb of Raphael is and other beautiful mosaics.
While traveling to Rome you must visit this historical site.  Seeing is believing.  Be sure to book a live guide in order to  thoroughly get the full essence of this  monumental building.
You must make sure you take the time to stop and see this.  The crowds were not bad and it's very intriguing.

This is such a lovely building and it's open to anyone to check out. It's also where some famous things are. Raphael is buried here as is the first king of Italy. There is lovely artwork through the Pantheon, from paintings to sculptures. It is a very pretty church.The Pantheon was commissioned by Marcus Agrippa in the first century and the current building was completed in the 100s CE. It was originally a Roman temple. Now it's a church. It is in amazing shape, you'd have no idea how old it is because it has been so well maintained. Outside the Pantheon is a large square with shops and restaurants (and a great place to get some coffee called Caffè Tazza d'Oro), including a cool large obelisk worth taking a look at. Be sure to visit the Pantheon if you visit Rome!

So beautiful and amazing but always crowded. It is free to visit but I encourage you to read a little about it before you go. We did this as part of a one mile walk outlined by Rick Steves. He gives you the route and some of the history and you can walk it easily on your own for free.
Had no idea that this is still a religious site where mass is said.Also did not know that the tomb of Raphael is here.
The Pantheon is a famous building and so many other buildings in the world have based their domes and design on it.  Amazing that it is in such good condition and is in the middle of a busy city.
Everything I expected and more. The line to enter was free flowing and took no time at all. Once inside, it literally brought tears to my eyes. Amazing!

We arrived in the evening. There was a line to get in but it moved quickly. Audio tours were not available while we were there. Although there were signs to be silent, there were too many people and it was not respected. It was free to get in, backpacks and photos were allowed. Our guide from the Colosseum stated that this was the Roman’s finest achievement. It just didn’t have the wow factor that the Colosseum and the Vatican had. But I’m still so very glad that I visited this important spot!

A marvellous old building lit only by the light coming in from an opening in the roof. This Roman temple was originally a place to worship their gods and goddesses but it is now a Christian church and a great piece of architecture.
Amazing architecture. Could have spent an entire day inside this amazing building. Well worth the line-up.

We stayed near the Spanish Steps, and the Pantheon was an easy 15 minute walk with my two kids (11/13) and mother (71) in tow. We visited before 11am and it was not crowded.  As we were leaving there was a small que beginning to form. Beautiful and amazing.  We did not get an audio tour.  We just looked up about the history while standing there.   They have an app apparently, but it wouldn’t download while I was there.Good quick outing
It's worth to visit the mighty Pantheon. It's also close to other "tourist attraction" in the area. Wow!

Beautiful building - unique that the only light comes from the only opening in the whole building in the roof! It really is a marvel to see.Well worth a visit - just be prepared to queue a little to get in and remember in summer months especially it will be absolutely packed to capacity but that wouldn’t stop me from visiting it - the beautiful sights are the same inside.

Everyone is trying to sell tickets and tours outside. We just walked in so we're pretty sure it's free and you're only really paying for the tour. I can see how the tour would be useful, as without one you don't really get the history. We didn't have a tour but could still admire the beauty and age of the building.
Very good for tourists great old structure to sit n just look at its architecture. Grab a gelato,  enjoy the atmosphere and take in an amazing building.

The Pantheon was one of my favorite sites in Rome and it is free!  It is one of the few historical sites in Rome that remains quite intact.  It is beautiful and has so much history to offer.  Take your time there.  The piazza surrounding it is very nice as well to take in the exterior.

The queue to get in was very short and wow this was so worth it. We listened to an audio presentation while we were in the pantheon.It cost 2 euro but both me and my husband could listen at the asme time and we learned such a lot from it and it helped us understand what we were looking at. Top tip - just sit and enjoy the amazing architecture and immerse yourself in the atmosphere.
One of the many places that will be on every tourist agenda when visiting Rome. The place and surrounding area are good and it is free entry inside.

This place was stunning both inside and out and showed so much history! The only part I didn't like was how overcrowded it was. At times it felt like we were being herded in like cattle, this did however have its benefits as it meant we avoided the dress code restrictions (I had bare shoulders and a short dress on due to the heat which is not permitted but most people got away with the same attire - I think it just depends on how busy it is). Even though this place was overcrowded I do think it's worth visiting even if you just look at it from the outside it's a stunning piece of Roman architecture!
Very nice monument and a very busy location. Must visit outside peak hours to enjoy the site from inside. Great ceiling.

This ancient architectural wonder, now a church, is in the heart of Rome. We were surprised to find it free of charge. The occulous (opening in the ceiling) is quite the wonder as is how the grand pillars were brought from Egypt to Rome. Doesn’t take long to explore so do try to fit it in.
