Enormous & amazing! This thing is old and still standing - Wow!  4 stars because it was super hot 🥵🥵🥵. Next time will be in the Fall or Spring.
One of the favorite parts of our trip. We had a tour guide that told us so much about this place. Incredible
The Pantheon should be on everyone's list of landmarks to visit.  First it's truly beautiful and it's free to get in.
If your into history this is definitely a must see, more tours are available in the area , a very crowded district.

This is a must see during your visit to Rome. This is the world's largest unreinforced concrete dome, with an amazing hole in the middle of the dome. It is like a giant eye looking down on you! There is an energy at this place and it seems as though you can find a crowd at any hour of the day or night. The building is amazingly maintained and when you consider it has been in continuous use since around 126 AD it is even more remarkable..

Overall the views were an amazing architectural experience. In this day and age it’s incredible to be able to see something like this knowing how old it is. If you are able to get there a little early you can avoid some of the crowds.

I love this place and especially Roman wall and columns. Unfortunately it was turned into a church and a majority of antic features were just removed. There are graves of two Italian kings there. It is free to visit and it may get pretty congested.

We stumbled on the Pantheon while getting lost on our way to Trevi. The giant colonnades revealed themselves as we emerged from a narrow alleyway, it was an awe inspiring sight, and just as magnificent inside under the dome.
You'll go in and out in 10 minutes. It's beautifull and has great history. You look at it few minutes from outside and inside, make pic and go to gelato. :)
This is worth the visit.  Please go inside,  I'm so pleased I did.  Outside is a little square of cafes and small shops.

I have been at the Pantheon many times and as always I'm startled by the Roman Architecture and the magnitude of the cupola. Even though Manu things were stolen from the building throughout the centuries its still manifests the ability of the Roman Empire!

Thinking about what it took to construct the Pantheon challenges the mind. How it has survived intact for nearly 2 millennia is a miracle. Don’t forget to walk around to the rear of the structure...you’ll find some great little shops and restaurants.

This is a must do. SURREAL exterior and even more awesome inside. Arrive EARLY to avoid the lines in hi season. It's free! And even if it cost 25 euro.. it would be worth every cent. Soak this treasure up.. top 5 places we have visited throughout Europe
Out of all the sites I saw in Rome I was truly amazed by this one.   It has been well preserved and is a site to behold in person.  Make sure to check this out when in Rome.
For starters, we were amazed by its enormity as we approached it. Once inside, we couldn't take our eyes off the ceiling... simply astonishing.
Free entry and no particular intense security to go through to get in. When inside, it blows your mind and is a relief to get out of the hot sun!

Built almost 1900 years ago it still stands strong. A former Roman pagan temple it is now a church. Located in the middle of Rome in the Piazza della Rotunda. It is one of the most preserved buildings of ancient Rome. Very interesting with free admission.
We walked all over Rome and managed to see this on our travels. Beautiful. Lots of things to see and do in the area.
the Pantheon is great - but (perhaps due to the fact that entry is free) it is necessary to plan ahead as you could be waiting in a queue for a long time

We visited here in the day and of course it was crowded as was only to be expected. There was no actual queue to get in however (I guess it depends which time of day you visit).Free entrance (we did not do any guided tour).Magnificent interior and a great feeling to be inside the most preserved building of ancient Rome. I took the opportunity to make a panoramic photograph of the entire interior.On our way back to the hotel in the early hours we took exterior photos of the Pantheon as the street was completely empty. This again was a great experience. Can recommend same if you want to get a photo at Trevi Fountain without other people in the frame.
One of the most impressive monument of Augustine Rome.  Entry to the venue is free and worth to spend some time view the whole structure during your stay in Rome.

Even though it was crowded it was so worth it.  We did an in person private tour and heard a lot of details we wouldn't have known on our own.  Definitely worth visiting.  It's a top site for a reason.  Beautiful and amazing.

The Pantheon is simply stunning. The square is perfect for having a coffee or gelato and admiring it from the outside. The queue to get inside didn't take long at all (early afternoon) and it's a very humbling experience while inside. The Pantheon is a must see when in Rome.

We were staying not that far from here and it is well worth looking at, both inside and out. The little square it sits in as well is bustling day and night, nice to be around. Overall if you have time try and fit it in, especially at night when lit up.
A must-visit plain Rome. It is free to enter. One of the best and most breathtaking places in Rome, extremely well preserved and maintained.
Most interesting history that goes with this building. What is now a church has a larger history from roman times. It’s worth investigating before visiting it gives the visit a different dimension
Worth a visit. Make sure you read up on the history and then see how many drains you can find in the floor!
The Pantheon is breathtaking. The history of the building is long and varied. Hard to think about walking where people so long ago walked. One of my favorite sights in Rome.

We went there after the whole tiring day to Vatican City. It was nice to sit in the bench inside of Pantheon and find out the history as well as the unique thing of this temple from the internet because we were just accidentally crossed by with no plan before . It worths to visit at night as cool view as well i think.
Amazing ancient building in the heart of Rome. Easy access, beautifully decorated. No tickets required.
We had a lovely tour of the Pantheon. Our tour guide was a very knowledgeable and funny gentleman from the UK.
Free entry with no queue . Inside was enormous and beautiful. As we went later in the evening it wasn't as crowded .

The story and history of this building is beautiful if you pop into the hotel grand de la minereve and sit in There roof terrace for a drink you can also view the beautiful roof structure of the pantheonEnjoyed the story of the holeIn the roof and looking at the architecture

A hot and humid day for our visit. We arrived about midday. We visited this site on our own, without a tour guide/group. There was a transit strike - apparently somewhat common in Italy - but they do announce them ahead of time. We walked from the hotel to the pantheon - about a mile. There is a plaza (piazza) in front with a fountain. It is a religious building so the girls had to put on their shawls to cover their shoulders and I, of course, took my hat off. However, unlike the Sistine chapel we did not have to have something long enough to cover our knees. I saw this building in an architecture class many years ago and had always wanted to see it in person. It was an incredible site. The ability to build such a large, open space in ancient times is very impressive. The opening in the center is an interesting touch that also contributed to reducing structural load. It would be interesting to see it when it is raining. The first king of Italy is entombed there in one of the alcoves. There are other famous dignitaries there as well. The outside of the building may not seem that impressive with its unadorned stone and its squat dome - but remember how long ago it was built and how impressive the space it creates is. The inside has a number of aspects to appreciate ... altars, statues, paintings, columns... marble, stone... candelabras... etc. it would be great to see an actual mass conducted in the building. It was definitely crowded inside but it was not so bad that you couldn’t move or get up close to the different sections. We did not have to wait to get in. There are also a number of wooden pews if you want to sit down and pray, reflect or simply quietly appreciate your surroundings. It is easy to see why this is one of the most popular attractions in Rome.

Beautiful stop on the way to or from something... We did this on the was from Trevi fountain and to the Spanish steps. Technically woman should have their shoulders covered but many of the women went in anyway...

We visited the Pantheon just before closing time, which was a good idea because there was no queue and the site itself was quieter. It is incredible that a place like this is free to enter!The dome is truly impressive - it is an imposing space - quite intimidating in its scale and size. It is a wonder to behold.It would have been nice if there had been more signage available to help us understand the features, frescos and sculptures in this magnificent buildings.
This place from outside doesn't make honor to the great and spiritual place this is.Didn't have to wait too much, and the visit was totally worth it.

The Pantheon was beautiful to see but very full on a HOT July day.  I suggest knowing the history around it before going otherwise you are wandering around aimlessly not understanding the history and the art.

We stumbled upon the Pantheon one evening in Rome without even meaning to. It is a very impressive building from the exterior and you can see that many modern buildings have taken inspiration from it. Inside the building is considered a place of worship which we had not realised, so remember to cover up appropriately before entering. Worth a look both inside and outside.
A must see place in Rome, which was really great. The atmosphere of the place was fantastic with the way that the light entered from its top. Don’t miss it

Blimey-you turn a corner and there it is !!! This wonderful building in the middle of Rome ? But of course it was there before nearly every thing else in RomeBuilt by Hadrian as a Roman Temple, but now a churchFree to get in, and once inside all you can do is admire its marvellousness A cylindrical building with a central dome opening to the sky (called oculus) how they built it is beyond any comprehension-go and see for yourself ! Lots to see inside too 👍

Just a heads up for people wondering, free entry into the pantheon. Thought I would have to pay but you can just walk in. Definitely work a walk though. Neat architecture, and once you’re done it’s a nice easy walk to the fountain!

Make sure you visit the Pantheon before you visit St. Peter’s basilica otherwise you will feel completely underwhelmed by the Pantheon. They were more strict on the dress code here than at the Vatican. No strappy tops, no hats.

Got to see this in the early evening, with a singing group practicing in the portico! THAT was a bonus. It is a working church, and was not open when we went there. Quiet an excellent ancient building.

Visited with my daughter and pleased it was free to enter with no queues either, just a steady stream of people.  Inside is very impressive and with quite a wow factor.  Just wished there were more information signs inside (or I’d taken time to research more about it before going as I’ve since found out so much more), make sure midriffs and shoulders are covered.
Definitely one of the highlights in Rome and an absolutely must see. It's also free to go and walk around.
Not really a lot to see, but it was exciting to know how old it was and see it still standing, and the people taking an interest in it and it’s history.
This destination is free and takes a short amount of time to visit.  We went around 4 and there wasn't a line.

I did my own research and tour of the Pantheon.  Although for other sites in Rome (the Colloseum or the Forum) a guided tour is the way to go, the Pantheon is more accessible, because it is one of the few Roman era structures still intact.  No description is necessary to understand what it looked like when it was built, because the structure is still complete.  It was turned into a church pretty early on, hence its survival for almost 2000 years!! There is the added benefit of it being free, and there are no lines.

The pantheon steeps high in historic significance in that it was one of the few structures built for multiple religion worship. Entry is free. Hence there is always a massive crowd in and around this region. At times its tough to even get in. Best to wait near the entrance for an opportune moment and slip in when the crowd thins. But be prepared to fight off the crowd once inside. The best approach would be to read up on the history of the structure before going so that when you are there you know exactly what you are looking at and won't wonder around in the crowd. It is best to go during the opening at 9 am or closing hours around 5 pm when the crowd thins down
Don't miss this magnificent building. It gets crowded for a reason and you'll see why after entering through the monstrous doors.

We arrived shortly before closing, the architecture and artwork inside was truly breathtaking. It wouldn’t take too long to visit it as it is one large (albeit impressive) room, not sure if it was free because we were some of the last visitors before it closed or if it’s free generally.
I love coming to the Pantheon. Its an incredible structure given when it was built. A must see when in Rome.
One of the most impressive sites in Rome. I used the cellphone for my commentary, which was very insightful
We had been to the Pantheon before and went inside. It's beautiful. But this time we only took pictures outside because it was closed to the public for some reason.
Amazing place to be visited. The queues are not big and it is free. Inside there is a christian church.

It’s a most beautiful expierence, a visit into the Pantheon. Unfortunately it’s overcrowded. My female companions had to put on more covered clothes, despite the 40+ degrees centigrade...nevertheless a visit to Rome without visiting the Pantheon is not recommended.
Visit late in the day.  Fewer people and zero queues.  This is a working church as well as a view into the past

The oldest building in continuous use is not to be missed. You can visit on our own but you will learn much more about the Pantheon if you have a guide or are partaking in a tour. I used Driver Guide Services for all my Rome tours and found them to have exceptional knowledge of not only Rome but Italy as a whole.

This structure is surprising. It is in the middle of several other buildings with an open area in front of it. Several artists played their instruments out front and were spectacular. The Pantheon itself is another of Rome's jewels. It contains treasures of the history of the city which should be viewed.

Seriously bring a shawl to visit the religious sites in Rome despite the July heat.  My daughter was in a lovely sundress, but with her shoulders exposed, she was denied entrance and she didn't want to purchase a shawl from the street vendors on principle.  The rest of the family found this site to be one of our Rome highlights -an architectural wonder!  So amazing that this was built and has lasted through the ages.

We were walking around outside taking pictures. I really didn't think it was worth our time to go inside. We did though, and it was stunning. How they engineered this structure so long ago is astounding. Definitely worth the time.
Don't forget there is a dress code. Get there early and have a breakfast by one of the cafes out from then walk straight in. So original full of history. Loved it.

A must visit destination  for followers of Roman antiquity and followers of important Christian sites. Built by Marcus Agrippa, whom Augustus intended to be the next emperor, the oculus (hole at the pinnacle) is stunning. I am pretty sure what Agrippa would think about its repurposing from its original intention, from classical to Christian, but the sands of time are on display in a magnificent example.

We walked to the Pantheon and there was a line for security (no tickets are required). It was Sunday afternoon and very hot. However, the security line moved swiftly. A group in front of me was told to get out of line because of what a woman was wearing. I purposely wore a dress with my shoulders covered because we knew we would be visiting church buildings like the Pantheon.
Wow what an amazing piece of ancient architecture. The science behind the dome itself is amazing and to think it was built all those years ago. Stunning. No charge and worth the visit.
We were in awe of this ancient temple- we went toward the evening so it was not too crowded.  Just an enjoyable experience.

My husband and I were walking through Rome checking out the sights and were literally shocked when we came upon the Pantheon. The size of it is overwhelming. This enormous temple is beautiful and the inside has been turned into a gorgeous basilica.
This is the most moving building that I have ever been to! It's just amazing--take some time to just stand in one place and take it all in!

Being one of the oldest buildings still in use in Rome it is a must see. The line can be long to get in but it's relatively fast moving. Allow about half an hour inside - that should be plenty of time.
Must see.. but do note, entrance is free.. and you can buy audio guide. it wasn't clear on the internet, so just to let you know.,

There is no other historic structure, in Rome or elsewhere, that has the cultural, historic, and architectural significance that the Pantheon has. It has been the model for numerous churches, palaces and government structures throughout the world and it is astounding that given it's ancient history, that it is still standing and looking as good as it does. It is a must-to-see on every visitors list, for its historical significance and its beauty (and it's free!)
Come early to avoid waiting in a line of crowd. Then go to Tazza d'oro Caffé to get a 0.90 espresso. Wow. You can take a lot of photos without photobombers too!

We visited the Pantheon on a very hot Saturday morning in mid July. The Pantheon is located on a piazza (can't remember the name) and looked unremarkable as we approached from the rear but the front was impressive and the interior - wow. I wasn't sure what to expect as I hadn't done my homework for the Pantheon but it is so impressive inside and provides a decent amount of information too. It's hard to believe that such a building was constructed in the 1st century AD. Be warned that as its a church you do need to cover your shoulders to enter and be appropriately dressed although there are sellers outside who sell scarves. The Pantheon is free of charge to enter and there was no queue or security checks. It's a must see when in Rome.
The Pantheon was a beautiful building which is free to get into and near other attractions. If you are doing a tour all around the city, it is worth a stop.

It is amazing that this is a perfect sphere, created so long ago, with none of our current technology.  The only source of  light is through the very large oculus in the centre of the ceiling.  Beautiful.
Unforgettable building, one of the most impressive architectural sites ever.  Unfortunatelly most visitors do not  respect request to be silent. Please, be quiet, it's a sacred place!

Okay if you are a first time to Rome this SHOULD be on your list of things to do/places to see. The entire inside is just gorgeous, words cannot express how beautiful it is. Take a look, walk inside, see the amazing hole in the ceiling, do it on a rainy day why not?

When visiting, you should remember this is a standing ancient Roman temple turned into a basilica. Whether you're a history buff, a fan of architecture, a religious pilgrim, or just inquisitive, this is a site in Rome you must see.  While it may not seem impressive to the modern eye, this dome has been standing for nearly 2000 years and probably inspired the Duomo in Florence, St. Peter's Basilica, and probably every other dome that came after it.  Please note that even though this was the Pantheon of ancient Rome--a temple to all the gods--today, it is a functioning Catholic church.  You should check their website for dress codes that include covering of the shoulders and knees.

Walking through the Place Saint Genevieve past the shrine of the saint Genevieve and church of Saint-Étienne-du-Mont, we approached The Panthéon, deep in the heart of Paris on top of the Montagne Saint-Genevieve, the historic hill of the Latin Quarter.  The Pantheon was originally built as a church to hold the remains of 5th century Saint Genevieve, it is now a burial place for famous French heroes. Designed by Jacques-Germain Soufflot and Jean-Baptiste Rondelet, it combines the look of the Pantheon's portico with 24 large Corinthian columns and the small commemorative tomb, Tempietto (Italian: 'small temple') by Donato Bramante, in Rome.    Construction started in 1758 and finished during the French Revolution in 1790. Its base design was that of a Greek cross with massive portico of Corinthian columns. The dome features three superimposed shells, similar to the St. Paul’s Cathedral in London. Above the entrance in Latin it says "To great men, the grateful homeland” Artist Puvis de Chavannes did many of the interior paintings and mosaics. The crypt covers most of the building underground area, and holds the tombs of some of Frances greats men, like Soufflot, its architect, Voltaire, Jean Moulin, Louis Braille, Victor Hugo, and Émile Zola. In 2002 Alexander Dumas remains were interred here.  There is a 220 feet pendulum beneath the central dome, which is a copy of physicist Léon Foucault original iron sphere, that is now on display in the Musée des Arts et Métiers.  TIP: Go early before the crowds and tour groups.

My daughter and I kept scarves to cover up with as needed. My 16 year old was a bit resistive until she was given an “ugly” covering at another site and after that always used her scarf.

I love the pantheon. I've been to Rome twice and it remains in my top 3 must sees. when you first turn the corner to face it, the presence of the building is beautiful !!and interesting things inside....beware of the Roman guards outside they may look cool, but they want cash for a photo taken with then... apparently 5euro a photo is a going rate!!!

The Pantheon is one of the most amazing buildings in existence today. However, the incredible story behind this building can easily be missed if you have not investigated the building beforehand or have a knowledgeable guide to talk you through it. I did the tour with a local tour guide, David Battaglino from City Walker Tours (search Trip Advisor for more info). If you really want to experience something special, read up or book the tour on the Pantheon.

I had visited the Pantheon before and I don't remeber having any trouble. However, this time, there was a woman and a man standing outside deciding who they thought weren't dressed appropriately. I would have understood if they picked out every person who was wearing shorts and a top not covering the shoulders, however the woman only decided to pick on certain people and allow others in (she was particularly lenient towards those in groups) which I thought was extremely unfair. I waited outside whilst my family, who hadn't visited before, went in. There was nowhere to sit so I sat on the edge of the Pantheon steps. Within 30 seconds of having sat down (considering it was 34 degree heat) I had a man yelling at me to stand up. My parents came out extremely angry as there were people inside wearing less than me. Very disappointed.
This building was very old and has so much history.  It doesn't cost to see this, and it is worth the time.
Free entry - no lines even during busy season - the piazza is great people watching.  Go in the late afternoon - great lighting and lighter crowds.
You have to have your shoulder cover to go in but there people going in with nothing on the shoulder and not wearing very must. Fell very picked on going in here. But it is free
The Pantheon is one of the first sites we saw in Rome. It's not only a great place for a photo op, it's also a nice place to duck out of the sun, but people also watch or enjoy the fountain.
This building is a must see. It is 2000 years old and has an immense amount of history you can lear about it.

This Use To Be A Pagan Temple But Is Now A Catholic Church Basilica. It Is Open Daily And Is Free To Get In. When Mass Is Taking Place On Saturday Late Afternoon And On Sunday Morning Only Those Attending Mass Are Allowed To Get Inside. Everyone Else Has To Wait Outside For Mass To End And Then Are Allowed Back In. Silence Is The Order Of The Day But Know It Is Very Noisy. Request For Silence Is Made Frequently But People Tend To Ignore The Request. It Is Very Large Area And The Alter And Pews Do Not Take Up A Lot Of The Space.

At we struggled to find the pantheon thinking lots of other church were it and then suddenly we turned a corner and found it, basking in its glory! It’s free to go in but there was a queue so we didn’t.

As an Australian you will never experience a building of this age, it is amazing. Please take the time to soak in the atmosphere & history. This is a free site but I recommend arriving there prior to 10 am to avoid queues & be able to enjoy the ambiance. This & the Palentine Hill are my highlights of Rome.

We went one afternoon about two hours before close and there was a huge line around the square and down the block! We decided to return the next morning a little after it opened and there was no line at all! It is very cool to see, and much better without a huge crowd inside. However, the visit won't take long. It is good to see it early in the morning and then head out to other activities that may take longer.
Great experience to see.  We were there on a weekday (Wed)  and weekend.  Less crowded during the week.

Experienced at sunrise and again later in the day.  An ancient imposing giant that continues to persevere 2000 years later.  The dome is a marvel of technology.  The oculus a site to behold.  I enjoyed it more at sunrise with less crowds but to go inside to see that beam of light piercing through the oculus is transformative. Whether it is the first time witnessing or the 100th.  Just remember that this is a church and visit with respect and introspection. This is not another Instagram location to check off one’s bucket list.
I loved this place, it was obviously much older than the surrounding buildings, it is oozing history. The famous artist Raphael rests here

Because this is a church it is open and free to go in and look around.  Nothing special per se if you have seen other old structures with awesome paintings and art but there is that big hole in the middle of the ceiling.  Worth a stop if you are anywhere close.  We stopped by on our way back from our Colosseum tour.
Excellently preserved, highly recommend it. The que moves fast and there is no entrance fee. Maybe the most beautiful Roman building.
Such an interesting site.  Go with a guide who can you about what you are seeing. The building is 2000 years old!  The whole thing.  It is not a ruin but in wonderful condition.

Great to see such an iconic building, Entrance is for free ,as it is a church. Do yourself and do a walking tour that includes this so that you can learn more about the history, while staring at the beautiful interiors

It was amazing to see this building.  It is the worlds largest unreinforced concrete dome!  It was used as a church so be mindful of your attire.  It is also free to enter! Go when the sun can shine through the opening tmso mid morning or evening.

An incredible feat of ancient architecture and a great metaphor for religion in general. This Christian Church was built before the spread of Christianity. The great dome took almost two millennia before it was usurped in size. It is a must see for anyone’s visit to Rome.
This is a truly sacred space that's worth seeing for the architecture and art.  It's glorious by day, but at night, it becomes truly captivating. Your pictures will show the difference!
A must see!  Much more impressive inside than outside.  A great place to visit during a hot summer day to cool off and see an impressive site.
If you go on your own to the building you might think it a bit dull since it is really a big empty hall.  But with a guide to explain all the history it was really interesting.
Busy,But worth it.Great feeling inside, sunlight coming through the roof.Casting its rays over the walls.
It is amazing that this is still a beautiful structure after nearly 2 centuries.  The history of this building is worth researching first.
For me personally this is not somewhere I took a tour.  I did a quick walk around the outside, then enjoyed a drink and pasta for prime people watching.
We stayed a couple of nights very close to the Pantheon. It is a beautiful area. We spent hours inside watching the light rain come in through the oculus. Fascinating.

We only had 1.5 days in Rome, so we weren't sure if we would be able to fit in the Pantheon, but we managed too.  Even though it was busy, the line went quick.  Loved that the security made people take off their hats and show respect.  Must do when in Rome.
It's free.  It's historic.  It's impressive what 40,000 slaves could build without any power tools.   Impressive standing example of ancient Rome.
To find this gem in the middle of the city is beyond amazing.  The artwork inside takes your breath away... This is a must see while in Rome..
free entry but security is tight. it's a wow place so get your cameras ready. sometimes there is a service so they request tourists to keep the volumes down.
Very nice. Got to see it during our electric bike tour. Did not take a lot of time to see and worth the visit.
The architecture blows my mind. It was interesting to imagine religious ceremonies taking place here. The tomb of Raphael was very interesting also.

Absolutely gorgeous religious scenes displayed inside the pantheon and the ceiling is incredible.They don't mind if you wear shorts which is also a plus as some churches do have that rule. They don't mind what you're wearing just come in and pull up a pue.

We expected a long line to enter - but it is easy to come and go.  No lines (2pm), free entrance.  Beautiful interior.  Like so much of Rome the history is amazing and the engineering and design of the building is incredible.  A quick stop - 20/30 min, easy entry and beautiful attraction....one of the best sights in Rome!
A small version but possibly better than the Sistine chapel hard to find down a little alley but worth a look

Very crowded but don't be deterred as the line does move quickly and you will be glad you did.  This structure is incredible as the dome is just as wide as it is tall with a very large opening at the top as the only source of light.  It was good thing that it did not rain that day.FYI: Men are asked to remove their hats when entering but women can keep them on.It wasn't an issue for the men in our group.

You know the Romans gave us a lot and this is another example of a wonderful building and a tourists hub. The building is magnificent but it’s extremely busy. And for a real change a well signposted monument because getting to see these buildings in rome, the streets are not set out normally and the signposting is shocking. But take the time to find it, you won’t be disappointed. And there are some lovely, inexpensive bars to have a drink in aswell
One of Rome's landmark.A ancient Greek style Pantheon, well preserved.The site is crowded with sightseers
This is a very impressive structure, and it's unique that it has an open ceiling.  It is a feat of engineering and remarkable Roman architecture!
The inside design of the Pantheon is definitely worth seeing!  Found the tomb of Raffaello Sanzio!  First painter (amazing painter!)to ask and be granted burial here!
Oldest Church in Rome - been used for all sorts of Worship over the years.  Also amazing engineering.  Hire a local tour guide and you will learn a ton about the Pantheon.

It’s free to enter and they ask you to be respectful. No hats which is understandable  and I was carrying a closed water bottle which they didn’t seem to like. The building is very beautiful though go early in the day to get the best out of it.
What can I say but wow absolutely amazing stunning architecture beautiful statues just awesome make sure you wear comfortable shoes as there is a lot of walking involved but the sights are phenomenal
This is a definite place to visit when in Rome.  It is very interesting and amazing site to see.  It was well worth the read prior to visiting it.
We were part of a tour that ventured to the Pantheon. It was beautiful. I kept thinking The De Vinci Code
very beautiful place to visit, if you are in Rome  if you are in Rome I highly recommend to visit Pantheon

the pantheon is one of the oldest building in Rome  used to be a Temple  to honor the gods and now is a Cristian church honoring  saint Mary and martyrs   .. famous because the first king of Italy and his son are buried in the pantheon  among the great painters Raffaello e Caracci  composer , architects etc. However  is most famous because has an  nine meters opening on the center of the dome .called oculus ... legend says that the oculus was build in such way  for the rain never to get in .... I actually went to visit in a rainy day and is not true does rain inside and the marble floor gets very very slippery .one last think near the Pantheon there is my favorite Ice cream  parlor Gelateria  Pasticceria " Giolitti"  that offers more then 100  flavors of gelato ..... opened  129 years ago
The Pantheon is a, massive and impressive structure. Do get there early to avoid queues. Entrance Is free

It was ok.  There was not much going on when I went there twice, it wasn't even nicely lit at night when I was hoping it would be.  I hope to see more events or at least some cool lighting for this place.
This wasn't on my to do list but I ended up going as I passed by it!! It was beautiful and full of history and information
It's the Pantheon! Incredible ancient architecture in perfect condition. Be prepared to take no-flash pictures. No entrance fee, take the time to touch the walls, pillars, and massive doors.

Don't go when it's crowded. Go when you can just sit inside and suck in the amazing architectural feat and thousands of years of history of this building. Sit there and realize that you are sitting in one of the most important buildings in the world.
Pantheon and it's environs are must visit on your Roman Holiday. Can do without a guide also. Amazing architecture & enduring beauty.

Entering the Colosseum the way the gladiators did is an amazing feeling.  A guided understanding of what they did and saw from the ground floor was worth it.  Afterward you can still go up to the upper ring and see all the displays of marble and how it would have looked in peak form.
On entering the building you think you are on a movie set. No pictures can make you appreciate the atmosphere of the place. A must see when visiting Rome and its free entrance.
Absolutely breath taking and great to see some old guards taking care of kids running amok and teaching them to show some respect
Mesmerizing... completely worth it to queue to get inside and watch the dome. It doesn't look that spectacular from outside but it is

We went here as a guided tour with princess land tour. The building is amazing such history and pleasant place to walk around. Was not very busy when we visited so lucky as it can get very busy. Plenty of camera shots as well.best ice to go early in the morning before it gets crazy busy.

I visited the Colosseum, Trevi fountain, Spanish steps, Vatican City to name a few and this was my personal favorite!  Being there by yourself takes you back in time! It was so peaceful to marvel the massive columns and walk underneath and towards the door. It is truly a massive building. Since I arrived early, they were still setting up the lines. And I was able to peak inside for a few minutes before security came over.  It’s free to go inside. The Pantheon is just a few minutes from Trevi Fountain, so visit Trevi first and then head over to the Pantheon. I got there around 7:30am on a Thursday.
You're lucky if you visit the place in a quiter time of the day. Otherwise it may be a little bit tiring
The proportions of this gorgeous building make it a geometric marvel.It is lit naturally by an apex skylight. That this is a BC building is even more marvelous
The Pantheon is one of the attractions that worth a visit in Paris if you go for more than 3 days. Do not forget to check the catacombs.
Beautiful surrounding, however, as everywhere in Rome it is a very crowded place with lots of tourists.
As usual lots of people and groups of tourists. The architecture is absolutely amazing. The entry is completely free

one of the first known environmental connected design in architecture, Pantheon is an open access place to visit and also a church. many buildings copied in history time(s) its interior design shape, with different integration as volumes, and scales of interpretation.#agrippa#apollodorus of damascus

Walking in and looking up you cannot help but appreciate the details of this building.  The fact that it was made with no modern tools, or conveniences is astonishing.  So very cool to learn about ancient history, renaissance history and current history all in on location.  Kudos to the Roman's for knowing how to keep these locations protected for us to visit up close and personal.

The Pantheon is a must see!  It is one of the oldest buildings in Rome as it is still in use today as a church.  The opening at the top of the  Pantheon is amazing and entry is free.  One can still view the paintings around the inside.  Don’t miss this opportunity!
Free admission and cool retreat during hot Rome afternoons. Amazing to step into a building that old and marvel at the architecture and accomplishment built so long ago.
I was able to walk to the Panteon from my flat.  All my friends that visited loved the history, archetecture, resturanats in the area.  Lots of fun, lots of people, highly recommend.
date: i lived in Rome for a year in 2015

text: I've been here before on a past visit and I still can't figure out how they build that dome all them years ago.  A simple, stunning place with fantastic decorative walls etc that has the bonus of being free to enter!

It's for sure one of the most amazing places to visit in Rome. The size, architecture, and its history worth a visit.Tip: Be sure to arrive by Via del Pantheon, because you will face the building and it's stunning!
Fantastic architecture but due to the queue being huge & 37 degrees heat we were not standing in it!

Thousand of tourist visit this place every day.... so you don't forget to do so if you're in Rome. The Pantheon with its open sky roof its unique.Many attractions also around this monument will be found.It's time for an Italian Ice Cream come to get one in Trastevere an visit the Pantheon
Tourism in Rome has increased over the years and at the end of June it is too busy everywhere including the Pantheon.  It is often too hot to see the sights as well.  Going in November next time
Maybe the word Pantheon means "all Gods" in Greek, but this place is more than that. You will be amazed with the simplicity -yet cleverness- of the dome architecture from inside.
Despite it being very crowded, I enjoyed the Pantheon. Such a rich historical monument.We did not have a guide, so I am sure we missed many details.
Did this one on our own, don't really need a tour or a guide unless you want to get the history and stories behind the building itslef , which you to be a pagan temple before christianity took over.
It’s one of my life dreams to visit that place it is beautiful to see how much the past civilization were inspired to build that magnificent masterpiece it is truly breathtaking

My husband and I were booked on a 3 hour tour of the Pantheon.At the pick up point, no body showed up. We asked all of the tour guides that were already there where our tour guide was, nobody knew. We even asked the people on the door, same response. Because of this we didn’t have a tour and I am utterly disappointed! I hope we get our money back!
How anyone can give this amazing place less than excellent is beyond me! It’s truly spectacular and awe inspiring. A must see when in Rome.
Visited this historic building as part of a walking tour of Rome,  lovely to see the interior.  It was free to enter but we had to wait in line but it did not take too long.

Stopped here on Christmas eve to see Raphael's tomb. While the building is impressive and the interior quite interesting (still functions as a church) the people outside were harassing the tourists for money.
The Pantheon is just as epic as you'd expect it to be. Definitely worth heading there and bearing with the masses which you won't be able to avoid in Rome anyways.
Once indeed the pantheon of the gods, Catholic Church turned this “pagan” temple into a church, replacing Roman god with christian statuary. The dome is breathtaking.
A must everyone should go and tourit was amazing how this was built and stands the test of time I highly recommend it

We visited Roma in 2009 and again this June 2019 and this was on top of our list based on the experience we had the first time.  The building and its history are very impressive and taking it all in from the outside and the inside is a must for anyone visiting Roma.  If you get a tour, that is probably best, but even if you are tight on time, go by and see it for yourself first hand.  On both of our visits, we stayed to have drinks / food at the cafes nearby the square.  It's a great people watching area and one of the best stories about our visit in 2009 was from this place.  We were having a drink and looking at all the passersby taking photos and what not and I spotted a college buddy walking in the area.  Imagine that, someone I had not seen in years from 10,000 miles away in the Pantheon!  Amazing.  Anyway, it is a special place for many and certainly for us.  I hope you go see it and have a similar experience.

It’s an ‘unmissable’!But I reckon you have to read about it before going through the ancient doors, info is fairly limited and if it’s busy , it’s loud, we were there moments ago , sat up the front and it’s truly marvelous, and quite funny with the recorded message for silence echoing around the place, that no one pays attention. It truly is a celebration of architecture, history, and tourism triumphs . What’s great, and well done to whoever, for making it free. It’s truly a world treasure. Easy to find, take your hat off and enjoy. Oh and read the wondrous words on Raphael’s tomb. Special. Happy days

There was a long line to get in, however it moved really quickly... walking pace... and you essentially just walked in and were able to see the inside.  Very beautiful, but it would have been nicer with a few more info displays to explain what each of the smaller side chapels were about and who made them.  It is definitely a "must see" when in Rome and is a lot easier than some of the other sites which require advance tickets and even guides.
It is a very interesting place to visit. Breathtaking!  Bear in mind that the entrance is free!! This is the place to visit when visiting Rome

We visited the Pantheon in the afternoon and while the line went across the square it moved very quickly. Unless you like to gaze intently at art and architecture for long periods of time, this landmark will be a nice and relatively short stop. I would definitely go again!

There are a few places on Earth that you really must visit. The Taj Mahal. The Forbidden City. The Great Wall. Paris. The list goes on. I would add the Pantheon to this exalted list. While certainly crowded in late June, it absolutely lived up to its reputation as an architectural wonder. If you had any doubts about ancient Roman building talents, this place will forever lay these to rest. The dome is spectacular. Yes, and it’s free.

It was a record heat wave in Rome when we visited the Pantheon and the line to get in (free entrance) was long. There was a church service in progress, so it was closed until around 1100 hrs. Once the mass was over, the Pantheon was opened up and they kept the line moving...once inside, take some time to look around. Highly recommend purchasing the audio guide as most of the signs provide limited information.
Breathtaking!  This is a must see to get inside and be amazed. To stand in this temple where so many in the past have stood before.  Be prepared to see armed guards with rifles nearby.

Don’t buy tickets - it’s free to enter!It’s a relatively quick stop on any walking tour of Rome and it’s still an architectural marvel. Who’d a thunk putting a hole in the roof would have such a transformative effect. Go.

So looking forward to visiting this and not disappointed. First things first, it is a church so a shame that there were so many people who were not respectful of that. Just don’t need gobby tour guides and know it alls spewing out a bunch of facts.  Appreciate the space, the art, the design and just take it all in. A truly awesome building. No queuing to speak of.  The oculus is huge, quite a surprise.
The pantheon is very beautiful inside. The line outside was long when we arrived but it moved pretty fast and was worth the wait.

While the front facade was impressive, the interior of the pantheon does not live up to the cultural legacy of Rome, at least in my opinion. The skylight is somewhat impressive, but the artwork was underwhelming. Furthurmore, the mood was ruined by repeat announcements in different languages asking for quiet. However it was free entry and the queue moved quite fast so going was not too much of a loss.

Just standing inside the building looking up on the  impressive is fantastic. No need to walk around to get the feeling of awesomeness.  Just turn around yourself and you will fill your senses.Absolutely worth a visit.

The pantheon is simply a must see if you are in Rome.  The dome is the largest cement dome in the world and a real stunner.  The open top adds charm.  There is some beautiful art, and the graves of the great Italian artist, Rafael.  Really worth the walk.  No wait and, even though it was busy, the place is big enough to handle the crowds.

We visited this early morning 8.45am ish, they were no crowds and was easy to look around The inside of this building is pretty impressive and it's free to visit.Take an audio guide if required or simply view..

For all the history buffs this is one of the most interesting sites!  If you can put yourself back in time and think of all that took place there, you have to be moved...  A must see if you are visiting Italy!

Watch out for what you are wearing, again you need knees and shoulders covered. Inside and out it's a very unique building and really beautiful inside. Spent way to long just looking around just because it's design and architecture was so different to anything else.

A must see if you are in Rome. Not sure if agrippa really build this one or if it was actually rebuild by a later emperor this structure is still almost 2000 years old. Unfortunately people have ruined it with Graves and stuff from 15th century kings and onwards, this is a must do anyway. Nice bonus is that it's free of charge and the line is really quick, don't be afraid if it's long, it moves at a fast pace and you will be inside in minutes anyway!

This is an absolute must when you come to Rome. Totally breath-taking, the open dome with the light streaming through will leave you speechless. We visited about  1pm and there was no queue to get in.

Excellent building largely intact. This is a free place to visit. There can be long queues in the afternoon. We walked straight in about 10:00am. It is closed for some periods when services are being held. The dome with hole open to the sky is great to see as well as the floor design and monuments inside. It also looks great at night.

The pantheon is one of my favourite buildings in Rome. Originally created to be a temple for all the gods, its dome is a wonder of roman engineering and very beautiful. On a sunny day the sun streams in through the central portal and illuminates the incense smoke giving you a chance to get some amazing pictures!While the line is usually very long it is worth joining as once the doors are opened it moves at a fast speed so you will be inside in no time. There is an audio guide usually for those who want the full details, but I was happy just to wander around inside.
The history of this place makes it a must visit. The only issue are the crowds, queues and pick pockets.
Our walking tour group stopped in here today.  Huge crowd there.  We waited in a very long, but moving line to see inside.  The police did a great job in keeping people from lingering ner the exit.
Much larger and impressive than I imagined. Beautiful structure that has survived the ages. Easy to get to and free to get in. Only took about 10 minutes to soak it in.
The most fascinating building in Rome IMO. Visit v v early before the crowds(before 9 am)  for a much better experience.
Well what can I say when you stand in front of this beautiful building you can’t comprehend how it was built it’s immense and the feel you get is amazing it’s easily sign posted to get to.

This awesome building has to be seen each time you are in Rome.  It is incredible. To think it was built 2000 years ago and still a functional building in mind boggling. Strongly recommend it to be seen.
Beautiful building. Found it fascinating. Entrance is free, and it's a great place to sit and take a little rest.
The Pantheon is grand in its architecture, but also provides a window into a time past when Rome had no moral compass.

The queue outside is massive but the flow of people is quite rapid so don't be put off. It is essentially a large church on the inside with the occular ceiling that's worth seeing.The pillars on the outside give the building a truly majestic feel to it all. Worth a short visit - it is free afterall :)

As with the colosseum, it is difficult to see how such a immense structure was constructed 2000 years ago. the dome inside is huge but the intriguing part is the hole in the centre. although it did not rain while we were there, we kept wondering how it would be. the cllection of art inside is great as well.

Wonderful Exhibit, family friendly. We saw tons of families.  Guards, guides and locals very friendly. It is close to cafes and lots of place to take good pictures. I noticed they also have events nearby the exhibits.
Went to the Pantheon first thing in the morning, not very crowded at all and we used Rick Steve audio download as we toured, it was very helpful and all free. Must stop and get Pantheon coffee!

The Pantheon is a must visit in Rome. It's a free attraction and has so much history and what a magical presence when you walk inside. There's a nice fountain out front to refill water bottles or just cool off. Note: shorts are okay but you need to cover your shoulders prior to entry.
Trying to take in all the history, the engineering of the building, and the sheer size of the space was an incredible experience. Take your time.
The Pantheon is a must see! Very beautiful especially the hole in the ceiling and all the art!! Great structure and a very nice alter!!
This is truly a wonder of mankind showing not only architectural marvels but artistic greatness. It's worth the visit to all of this in one place.

What I love in Rome is that one can walk easily from one place of interest to the other... this is one of them that is a must to include in the walking tour. Amazing experience and story.  A must visit
despite the crowds who seem to forget this is a church one can't help but be impressed by the inside of this building and it's history.
Partly because it’s something to correlate all of the thoughts running about in our minds, many similarities occurring now across the world compliment the sense of wonder of this site!!!
A must-see for seekers of landmarks on a budget as it is free to enter! Not too crowded either, which gave a nice contrast to the Colosseum I visited previously. Lovely art o the inside.

Free entry, not overly crowded even in prime time. You can take pictures all you want. An engineering marvel considering when it was built. Even by today's standards, it looks perfectly constructed. Incredible...
Worth the visit, nice wall and dome art, next time we'll read some more on the history first and go when less busy. Very close to Piazza Navona for refrewshments.

It is free and easy to visit. It is amazing to see such incredible construction!! Understand how the dome is almost perfect. This is a building that I have dreamed of seeing and it did not disappoint. It was built as a temple to all gods but then turned into a Christian church which is why it still stands. It was not destroyed by looting or resourcing for other buildings like many other structures.

This was a little out of the way of the other sights we saw, but so worth the walk! We went into the plaza, stood by the fountain, looked in, and saw this amazing shaft of light coming through the oculus! So we went inside-just breathtaking! Inspirational!
Beautiful original Roman Architecture!  Absolutely amazing what was built so many years ago!  It’s a must see in Rome.
It looks very fantastic from outside. MAKE SURE YOU LADIES WEAR CLOTHES THAT COVER YOUR KNEES! I was not let inside because I was in shorts... :(

Stopped in half an hour before closing and walked right in (no line). Probably should have done more research before going in (our guide gave some basic details outside and let us explore on our own) to get the most out of the visit, but still impressive both inside and out. Another Rome special where you're walking along congested, cobblestone alleys, and then...BAM!
We have visited the Pantheon may times and never tire of it.  Beautiful building inside and out.  Be sure to spend some time on the inside!  It's free.

Really interesting place. Free to walk in, no photos but people are taking them. It’s another religious spot in Rome with an open ceiling and lovely statues. It’s close to other sites and worth walking in.
Free to visit and a real gem, not the ornate and elaborate decor of most, but had a real impact, best time appears to be round lunch when the walking tours take a break
Beautiful building well worth a visit as its also free. Good insight into history of Rome. Nice and cool on a hot day
When in Rome, I always return to visit it. Amzing building, such old, but it feels perfect attraction for modern people..
Really magical historical place - must to see.. Really nice that it is possible to get in. Sometimes even national musicians playing in front :)
Enjoyed seeing this beautiful building. Easy walk to and free admission. Make sure to walk far enough away from it to see the domed top.

What an awesome ancient structure!! We did not expect how large it was inside. The largest free standing dome in the world. We had a local guide from "get your guide" with us. Absolutely worth it to have a guide who explains all the details of the builfing and its history. The eye the eye!! What a feature. The only source of light for the panthoen. The architectural design made us just speechless. Yes a must visit when in Rome!!!

We visited the glorious Pantheon during our recent stay in Rome. During the morning we explored the Baths of Caracalla, and after a languid lunch we walked across central Rome until we came to the Pantheon. We'd booked an audio guide beforehand, which is well worthwhile - Even though the place was crowded, we still found seating inside, and once settled, we could look around, follow the audio guide, and take in the surrounding splendours. Neither of us had visited the Pantheon before, so we were particularly awestruck by the sight. From outside, the Marcus Agrippa inscribed portico, the 16 columns, and the dome itself, look enormously impressive. From inside, however, the dome, oculus, and all the immediate surroundings just take the breath away. The level of preservation in a nearly 2,000 year old  building, is simply stunning - not that much has changed since the Emperor Hadrian opened the temple in AD125 (or thereabouts). We left the Pantheon with stiff necks, as a result of too much looking upwards at the perfectly positioned oculus at the centre of the dome. A wonderfully enlightening experience, and a great highlight of our time in Rome!
This site is amazing from the outside, but follow the line to get inside--the oculus in the dome is just astonishing, as is the art. Absolute overwhelming.
I really expected something more spectacular from what I had read about it. Again, the area around it was dirty and strewn with garbage.

This place is gorgeous and there may be a line out the door, but it moves SO quickly and is worth the two mins you end up waiting. The history of the stone work in there, and the gorgeous opening. It's beautiful and so easy to bop into. Definitely swing in!
This truly mesmeric building ouzes history and culture and is simply stunning. It epitomize all that's special about the eternal city. The square outside is a great place to imbibe in the local beer

Visited here both in the day and the night. You really see the building very differently at night and it looks fantastic. This building gave me the biggest wow factor in Rome as you approach by tiny small cobbled streets and then all of a sudden this massive building appears from know where . The dome is made of one piece and i can't image how the actually built it. Well worth a visit.

Great stop in Rome. We were in line maybe 10 minutes total, it moved rather quickly. I would allow maybe 30-45 minutes inside depending how much you want to read up and take pics. The building is outstanding inside and out.

Its fairly simplistic inside, we were lucky to come when there was no queue, i wouldnt like to have queued for 45 minutes like we saw later in the same day. Its an impressive monument and intriguing because of its age, but dont expect to have your mind blown.

I've been there before. It's crowded and yes it's beautiful inside but the most stunning part is the architecture and the hole in the dome, where light comes flooding down.  You can walk in and maybe look up online what's in there. I don't think you really need a guide for this.
Pantheon used to be a Roman Temple of all God and now a church. The dome is the world largest unreinforced dome. Spectacular architectural dome and it is free entry for all visitors.
One of the best examples of Italian architecture ever.The dome is one of the most spectacular sights in Rome.
Was quite busy even in January but definitely worth a visit. Definitely get a guide book to read up on each of the painting and alcoves
Like many things in Rome free to enter and awe inspiring. Our hotel was close so we visited twice as first visit was very crowded mid day.

This structure does not look like its anything great from the outside but once you are inside, its spectacular. This is the oldest roman church which has been preserved without much rework done. And the best thing is there are no entra charges
Really recommend going to the pantheon on your trip to Rome. On a scorching day it was cool inside. Lots of information and things to see inside. An incredible sight.
Pantheon, the oldest, continuous use facility in the west and likely the world.  Really cool, and just hits you when you walk into the square.  Well worth the walk and look inside!
While the crowds were boisterous in the Sistine Chapel, they were not here.  It was quiet enough to contemplate the history and religious significance of the Pantheon.
Easy to get in, just make the line and wait.  Beautiful pictures to be taken on the inside.  A must see in Rome!
Queue can be long, so we went back as they opened it back up after it closed for a couple for hours for Sunday service. Ended up walking straight in.

The Pantheon is free, if there are free places in Rome ... but you have to arrive early if you want to enjoy it and take the best photos, then it is impossible .... open at 8.30am and close at 7.30 ...  I visited it 2 times at different times and it was best to visit it earlier.
The line to enter the Pantheon moves quickly (~1-2 minutes while we were there in December) and for time to tour the interior I’d recommend 15+ minutes depending on your interests. Plus it’s free!
Went around 10pm cooler but only outside pantheon truly huge grand roman building still like built decades ago

Totally blown away by the art and architecture of the building.  The crowds were massive but moved quite easily throughout the floor.  We self navigated and enjoyed setting our own pace.  Afterwards had a great pizza just outside the Pantheon.  Great people watching!

This was fantastic. We went in the evening, so the line to get in was super short. There are a ton of restaurants right on the square - I don't recommend eating in them (tourist traps, the food we tried at one of them was not the best), but it's worth sitting down for a drink while you enjoy this amazing view.
Loved it! it was so tranquil, an amazing experience and so pleased we decided to take a look inside, would love to visit again!
Beautiful architecture. We were brought here on a free walking tour. The line up was insane but it wasn’t too bad. Worth check out.
Line was very quick. Don't be put off by this. It was not our first visit but you learn new things all the time. We waited in what seemed to be a long line for maybe 5 minutes. A must see!!
There were long lines to get in. If you don't want to wait spend some time walking around outside. There is  beautiful fountain across from it.

Don't just hang and look at the high Roman Corinthian colonnade and porch at the front of the approx 2,000 year old Pantheon - walk around it's rustic curved sides and rear, sit on a side wall in the shade with a gelato, go inside to see the dome's amazing oculus with it's almost 9 metre wide opening to the sky! Maybe attend a mass there if you can even if you are not religious - you may be surprised with a full choir and music.

This is a must see when visiting Rome.  It is truly an amazing place.  I would suggest you pay the extra for the head set so that you can hear about the amazing site you are seeing.  Note....I do not think you need to pay the extra for a guided tour.  The groups I saw were quite large and with the surroundings, it is very difficult to stay together in a large group and hear the tour guide.  This site is easily explored on your own especially if you rent the headset.  It allows you to explore at your own pace and hear about the site.
If you like history, this is a great place to go. It's a huge place so be sure you are well rested. not good to go without being in a tour as tits a huge place.

Unless you are Tom Hanks, you just can't run up to a tour guide you did not hire, and ask a question.  My most sincere advice is to know the history (at least the text book summary) of the Pantheon BEFORE your visit, even if you do visit with a tour guide. Knowing the Pantheon history makes the visit much more enjoyable as you can take in the sights (which are stunning) and still appreciate the specifics of what to concentrate on while on your visit.  The oculus is amazing and knowing its place in history makes it look more amazing.  Being prepared allows you to enjoy the Pantheon in a shorter period of time, which is helpful  if it is crowded and/or if Rome is hot (like in July) and you would rather not linger.  Keep in mind that the Pantheon is till an active Church and part of the Pantheon still serves that purpose (be respectful).
Very busy place, too many people wandering not really taking in the occulas.  I have been in here five times before and this wasn’t the greatest visit

This is huge and so beautiful. You can go through it for free or get a guide. It is so beautiful inside and out. Very interesting history behind it. We went with our young kiddos and they enjoyed it as well.

I went twice to the Pantheon. It can be crowded depending on when you go. But it is also an easy visit. Make sure you stop and read some of the information inside at various sites. Or get a self-guided tour.
My wife and I was on our honeymoon in Rome, we stumbled onto this and can I just say wow! Peaceful, beautiful and still had a touch of magic left inside!

We saw this on a free walking tour with Rome’s Ultimate, so that made it much more meaningful and impressive. We stopped by a second time when it was on our way to somewhere else, since it’s a very quick line to enter and cool inside compared to the summer heat. We sat in the pews toward the front the second time, and I’d recommend enjoying it from there to take it in. The people seated aren’t usually taking any photos, so it feels calmer and easier to take in from there. It’s neat to see the light pouring in from the open ceiling!
Amazing architecture and history.   Check it out early morning before the crowd to see inside.  Saw it at night as well and just as stunning.   Great coffee shop that has local coffee nearby.
For those who would like to have a feeling of what refined Roman architecture was like - the Pantheon offers a glimpse into Roman architectural sophistication.
Very few things in life are free, especially in Italy,  but this was one of them!  Absolutely beautiful inside.

Completely agree, get here early (especially in summer) before the crowds and you can enter and be inspired for free. Even kids ages 5,6,8 yrs old were in awe. The lane ways are full of cute little trattorias and gelaterias. You literally round a corner and this incredible building looms large.

Who doesn’t love free right? It is very beautiful inside and I love the history of it. We went when it wasn’t very busy inside, so we were lucky. You should also stop by in the evening when it is closed. It is very pretty in the evening all lit up. We stayed at a hotel right across the street so it was very convenient.
Just want to say...go there, first thing on the morning and at midnight, on both times you will be overwhelmed by this grandious monument
We popped in quick during our full day tour to snap some pics. We didn't have a lot time to linger. But it is definitely one of the must sees in Rome.
Do you have this much history in one place is amazing. This is a beautiful structure and must to see if you are in Rome.It is not as crowded as the Colosseum.
Always one of my favorite places to see, I come every time I am in Rome, built starting 14bc. The first domed concrete building

This was our first day.  We had been in airports and planes for 21 hours.  We got to the Hotel Villa Pinciana around 2 am.  I got up early and went for a walk.  It is amazing what is all around in Rome.  The wall that surrounded is still standing. Lynn and I spent the day just walking around Rome and seeing what was close to our hotel, which was everything.  We saw the fountain of Trevi and from there walked to the Pantheon.  The Pantheon is a church but started out as a temple to all the gods.  It was first built around 27 BC.  The structure is amazing.  You can get a tour of it or you can download the facts and go see it yourself.  There was no line.  It is a church so no hats or shorts that expose the knee are allowed.  The dome is the largest unsupported dome in the world.
Enjoyed the calm quiet atmosphere even though it was busy as visitors are encouraged to respect that it is a place of worship and silence is requested
We were amazed by the Pantheon.  The history and the art are wonderful. Truly enjoyed after the short wait to enter. A must see while in Rome.
Wonder in each section, and much to see-- art and history.   The history is explained beside the art  in Italian and English.
amazing!! very crowded you know. you must seen that . i went with my friends. one of the oldiest place in rome.

This was my second visit to the Pantheon which has to be my favourite building in the world (to date).  The majesty, engineering, beauty and longevity are just astounding.  Don’t be put off by the queue outside because it moves very quickly and it is absolutely free to see this incredible treasure.The fact that this building is still so complete and beautiful 2000 years after it was built is a testament to the incredible construction.  And inside it is so beautiful in stark contrast to its fairly formal entrance and the unassuming construction of the exterior walls.  This is absolutely a highlight of Rome.
I was impressed by this building.  We didn't have a guide (it would have helped) but it was beautiful inside.

This is such a wonderful building- so unique with no windows apart from the roof circle.  Great that it is free and spacious but attempts to keep people quiet and respectful of the sanctity of the basilica never work, which is a shame
Unbelievable structure. Free entry. A must visit for Rome. Magnificent building. Very popular and very crowded. But don't be afraid if you see long queues...

We didn’t realize where we were walking until rounding a corner and running into the plaza in front of the Pantheon.  It’s one of the “must see” monuments in Rome, and as with most of them, is just overwhelming.

Breeze to visit, view the beauty and/or sit inside, not peaceful inside with the movement of people, but totally free. Security helps control one line in and one area for out. Another beautiful area to take your family.

The influence of the original in Athens is very evident from the portico.  It is fascinating to think that the whole structure of the dome was constructed as an un-reinforced structure.  Even more, the main structure inside has the same height and diameter, which means a perfect sphere could be fitted in here.The top of  the dome has an opening probably to let in natural light.  However if it rains, the inside gets flooded, even with the drainage system which was designed to take the water out to the river (early rain water harvesting).

The Pantheon, a very ancient Roman temple, is located right in the heart of Rome, sprouting like a mushroom after the rain in between buildings.The temple itself is not the best you will see in Rome, as it lacks beautiful frescoes, many amazing paintings and is not overly adorned, but what is mesmerizing is how people could so many years ago build such a structure without the aid of technology. Really really impressive.Overall a wonderful attraction, better to visit when not too crowded to fully enjoy it.
It’s architecture is special. It was built as a pagan temple to worship many different Roman/Greece gods. Later this was changed to a church.
Free entrance, beautiful inside and outside! We had a private golf cart tour with IterItaly and the guide drove us close to Pantheon. It's a must!

We stumbled on the Pantheon during one of our lost wanderings through Rome.  It was practically deserted inside at night (about 2130) but still an amazing sight. Try Napoletano's in the piazza for great gelato and a perfect view of this beautiful, ancient temple from their outdoor tables.
The Pantheon is probably one of the most attractive churches in Rome and well worth a visit. The architecture is fantastic, the dome is most impressive and the interior is quite beautiful.

Most people planning a trip to Rome will have seen images of the Pantheon, actually experiencing it in person is something that shouldn't be missed. The impressive exterior is actually surpassed once inside. More superbly detailed marble & stonework topped off by the amazing domed ceiling. The Pantheon is nicely situated on a walking route from the Trevi Fountain to Piazza Navona.

To save yourself some hassle, come in the early morning or closer to closing time. There are lines to get in mid-day at some point but in the early morning and end of day, you can freely walk in. Also, if you happen to be in the city during a rain storm, come here during a rain storm to experience the rain falling through the center gap and then make off to one of the many bars and cafes in the area to bide your time until it clears up.
Stunning work of architecture. Originally a temple yo all the Roman pagan gods, then dedicated as a Catholic church.  Drink from the fountain out front, and enjoy the vibe.

As you round the corner and see the Pantheon for the first time, it would be considered impressive it it were built in the last few hundred years. However, consider it's age (like a lot of buildings in Rome) and it's breathtaking. We sat for a drink in one of the cafes in the square and just soaked up the view. Wonderful!!

mesmerizing experience of the awesome structure of the past.stones have life here.just imagining that these pillars were hauled across from so far away itself makes the experience so overwhelming.the eye is huge and makes you stare at it for long.the shadows on the walls the floor..everything is worth wtatching
The pantheon is well worth visiting, the roof has a hole in the centre to allow for rainwater drainage.
We were first in line, actually we were there before the line even started. It was awesome. A super quick stop and worth the extra walk to get there.

This is another Roman architectural marvel. Although free it is very busy and lines can be long. Read about the incredible design with an open dome that allows rain to enter and be drained away. There are multiple tombs of important historical figures.  Highly recommend. Hope this helps. A handicap tag will allow you to enter without waiting in line.
You must stop in here, either on your own or on a tour! So so beautiful, and so interesting! You can take pics inside which is a bonus:)
This is history pure and it's breathtaking. You want to be here once in a lifetime. Magical and Theatrical

The Pantheon is such a wonder in that it is almost as it was in ancient Rome.  It is now used as a church but the building itself remains largely intact.  Visit early in the day or late afternoon as it is a very popular attraction.

No visit to Rome would be complete without a visit to the Pantheon!  Truly a marvel with all the beautiful marble and the oculus.  The tombs of Raphael an several kings are also located in here.  It's free but I would recommend visiting early in the morning or later in the day to avoid the crowds.

I absolutely love the Pantheon.  It’s truly an architectural marvel and not to be missed!The location is very central and really worth your time to visit.  I would imagine it would even be very special in the rain to see it coming through the ceiling.
A truly ancient temple, steeped on history . The interior shows man's artistic yslenys nearly 2000 years ago
Simply a beautiful example of architecture. Amazing to think it has survived from antiquity when so much else has not.

I had only ever looked at this impressive building from the outside, I mean how different could it be inside to all the other buildings, churches, museums etc that one visits in Rome.  But my wife had never been to Rome, so in we went ... and WOW, it is truly spectaular inside, and in my view, a MUST SEE on any itinerary in Rome!

We booked a free tour, it lasted half an hour, 7pm to 7.30pm, I’ve got to say it was fantastic, Brandon our tour guide was informative and pleasant; fortunately for us as it was raining a group of another 4 people did not attend so we had our own personal tour guide. Great to see the rain coming in through the open dome.
We walked here from our AirBnB.  It was worth the walk.  It is so beautiful inside.  It is something you don't want to miss while in Rome.
Neat stop, it was a little overwhelmed by tourists, should have had a tour guide rather than just walk in.

The Pantheon was quite busy inside however, the queue to get in moves really quick. In my opinion I don't think you need to pay for a tour guide to show you around the place. We walked around for about 20 mins, took some pics and walked to Trevi Fountain.
To this magnificent building. No fee to entry, cool inside, a place to sit and contemplate the big and small questions. Plus, that incredible dome!

This is an incredible building. My favourite thing to see in Rome after the Trevi Fountain. It’s so impressive when approaching from outside with its massive columns and great to look inside, there are tombs there of the first two Italian kings and also the tomb of Raphael. It’s free to go inside, we went at around 6:45pm, there was still a line of people but we were in within a few minutes. It is still a practicing church so please remember that when inside. The square outside also has great restaurants and street entertainers in the evening.
Just very beautiful edifice, paintings and church. A must visit by all who visit Rome. Unbelievable pieces of art to look at..
Came upon the Pantheon while walking. No line to get in, although later in the day it was very long. Beautiful church inside.

I have never saw the massive structure like pantheon infront of my eyes before. I 'd only see on the World map and travel book. In this year, i see it infront of my eyes. I have only one day.After that, i need to go back to my country. My holiday vacation only took 2 days only
How many places can you visit for FREE in Rome? This is your place.  Great history, great story. Worthy of your time. I think we spent about 20 mins here.
Interesting place to visit.  There's a great gelato place just steps from here.  We did have some problems with people following our teens and approaching us so just be vigilant.

This is a must visit site. To think it is almost two thousand years old and has the largest dome of concrete in the world still. It’s survived from pre-Christian Times when it was a temple to the Gods to become a church 1300 years ago and has been since.....amazing history.
Very nice workmanship. The memory of a vanished age, the tomb of the great ones. Monumental gate and beautiful interior.
It was really beautiful to look at and a very impressive feat if architecture and ingenering. It definitely gives off a divine vibe, make sure you take your hat off when you enter.

It's a spectacular sight to see the Pantheon from the outside dominating the square. Inside is great- an incredible amount of history (it's where Raphael is buried and the site has been used for worship of various kinds for who knows how long). We were there at 2.00pm on a Monday and were treated to a concert. Brilliant and it's free into the bargain.
Loved it...a massive peice of history sat there. Its amazing..you have to see its scale and the roof.By the way..Free entry

For the architectural significance alone you have to visit the Pantheon.  From the outside it appears pretty stripped of it original elegance.  Walk through the massive front doors and look up!  WOW!  How did they do that!  Now serving as a church, the original simplicity of the structure is somewhat lost but the perfection of the interior space is all that I imagined from my architectural text books.  It remains as a significant structure that must be seen.Hopefully you’ll get a chance to see the dome from a higher panoramic vista too.
