I visited the Pantheon in late April. It was busy but the to get in moved very quickly. The inside was very busy but not to bad. We decided to pay to listen to the audio booth they had it was quite good lots of information
This is amazing site to see. Can be cramped when its crowded but worth seeing anyways need time to view all the things inside.
I can’t explain how marvelous it is to soak in the atmosphere and beautiful of this Roman church to the gods... Raphael is buried here in a crypt for those art aficionados...
Absolutely beautiful. We visited around seven in the evening. It wasn't too crowded but it was closing around 7:30pm. Well worth the walk! Don't miss!
Very grand, worth it to walk in and look up (!) and around. There is usually a line outside to actually walk into the Pantheon. It is free :) And, yes, very crowded.

The pantheon is beautiful. Everyone who comes to Rome should definitely go. Our recommendation: learn to appreciate things fast! Take pictures really fast, enjoy the place as quickly as you can or you'll get runned over by other people.

When I first walked into the plaza I was hit with a wave of wonderment and admiration. I was startled by how large it is.  It is a marvel of Roman engineering and one of the most impressive structures ever built. That said, I was most impressed by the exterior. I admired the dome from the interior too, but seeing it once was enough. A must for first-timers.

Although entrance to the Pantheon is free we decided to book a guide to tell us all about the history of this amazing building.Our guide was Jessica Coppola and she was brilliant, so enthusiastic about her city and particularly the Pantheon which she absolutely loved. She was both interesting and informative We particularly liked the concrete dome and the colonnaded frontage to this building full of history. The Pantheon is well worth a  visit.
Impressive building for being so old and there is something magical about the open roof especially when the sun shines a focused beam through it
It was awesome and very large. It’s just the people that make it less appealing since such a crowd doesn’t make the place inviting or comfortable.

So after visiting the Pantheon originally back in 2014, being back in Rome 5 years later I figured it wouldn't have the same shock and awe factor...I was wrong. The Pantheon really is truly amazing and never fails to leave me speechless, such a commanding building nestled into the centre of Rome. The fact this building is two millennia old is utterly remarkable and the unsupported centre dome is still the largest made.We initially visited at around midday and the whole area was heaving with people. On our second day however we visited at 7:30am and whilst early it was so peaceful and next to no other people around, making getting pictures easy and personal.Be sure to check it out at opening or closing time for the best personable experience and if you do not have a tripod lean on the fountain at night to get it focused!
Breathtaking. One word. Entrance is free. Place is enormously huge. If you saw pictures, you can't imagine how big it is.

It would have been nice...except that nobody apparently speaks English at the Basilica (that is when the guards are not sleeping at the booth early in the morning when we visited). I paid 11 Euro for headphones that I could not locate where to find, nor access during my brief visit here. The electronic ticket emailed to me, apparently grants access to wait in line and get the headphones. What is the point of ordering this online?
Whenever in Rome , I go to see this amazing piece of history. It is ofter crowded-- so try and go at opening or right before closing for your best experience.

Set at one side of a pretty piazza, the Pantheon doesn't look its age. It is almost impossible to believe that the structure (if not the interior décor) is two millennia old. Perhaps most amazing is the unsupported concrete dome, which looks as good as new.Externally, the dome and grand, colonnaded frontage are handsome and impressive. The interior has changed multiple times since the Imperial Roman origins of the building and now very much resembles one of the vast, marbled interiors of some of the city's churches and basilicas. The space is imposing and attractive.I was surprised to find that such a famous and remarkable building is entirely free to visit. All you need to do is to join the shuffling stream of tourists filing in through the colonnade.If there is a drawback to a visit to the Pantheon, it lies in the fact that it and the small square it faces become extremely crowded with visitors. I was there in early May and it was heaving. Goodness knows what July is like!Overall, though, a wonderful site and a must when you visit Rome.

Simply breathtaking. On our first night in Rome, we took in the Trevi Fountain and Pantheon - and the Pantheon was certainly the highlight of our entire week-long trip! It was breathtaking, full of character and history, with gorgeous architecture and we couldn't believe it was free to enter. A real must. There are lovely places to sit and reflect outside, too, should you want to, with plenty of great ice cream/gelato shops nearby.
Nice to see this historic building. Security was slightly firm, but with the amount of people touring through, it was understandable.

You can spend between 30 minutes to an hour in this sacred worship place where you are expected to maintain silence. Another important building in the history of Rome dates back to paegan times and takes you through a time travelling journey together with its brilliant architecture.
if you have the chance go by at night as well as in the day, it's really pretty illuminated and there are no crowds!

We really wanted to see this heritage site but like every place else in Rome, there were tons of tourists.   Its such an amazing, historical place that we decided to return another day to go in.      Entrance is free.  You can self guide but on that first time to the plaza, we encountered a young Irish gentleman  with Vatican Tour Company.  He offered short tours that went inside and outside of the building to explain it.  We liked that.  So when we returned, we went looking for him to take this tour.  he wasn't there, but glad we found his colleague.  Had a super-informative, friendly and sometimes humorous tour with Antony Stuart of Vatican Tours  and a small group of about 6 Brits.   Such a great price for such a great little tour.

Another 'must see' attraction when in Rome is the Pantheon. You happen upon it from narrow streets and it opens up into a charming square with centre fountain, restaurants surrounding it and then the actual Pantheon towering over it all. I wasn't prepared for the scale of it and also the fact you can go inside for free! What a bonus. It made me laugh that they ask for silence inside, yet a tannoy booms out 'Please be Silent' messages regularly at top volume - Roman Irony? Anyway, if you thought outside was impressive then inside is its equal. It truly is a sight to behold. Just one more thing - you will pay top money to eat in the square, but we decided to anyway - how many times in your life can you say you have eaten next to the Pantheon? You will not be disappointed!
Went inside to keep from rain, very nice. Also great that it is free of charge. Must visit if checking out Rome.
A beautiful place, which exudes art and culture and does not pay in. It is well worth the visit. I hope to come back other times.

Nice to know that Romans were actually building memories for civilization to marvel.  If you could actually close your eyes and imagine Rome without selfie-sticks, you would be dazzled.Best seen at night with Rome’s subtle lighting and less tourists.  A real treasure.
This place is free of charge to go visit and is worth going to seeYou spend around less than an hour here Quite impressive architectureThere might be a long line but it goes quite fast
You Will see many sights in Rome but not many that are In their original state. Built by the Romans .tip check out YouTube .
Not much to do here, but this place is extremely beautiful and full of history. This city honestly has so many cool spots that are so significant you run out of adjectives.

First off, this is an active church, therefore, you must remove your hat.  This place is absolutely amazing.  The fact that this dome is is only one of 2 free standing domes in the world is spectacular.  Yes, there is a hole in the ceiling with a drain for water.  However, that is the secret, it is one of 2 in the world where the roof is holding itself up. There are no columns or trusses; the dome itself holds the weight and has held for a thousand years.
Visited this place during evening , very good structure with good view and well maintained property must visit place

A must see structure in Rome.  Both inside and out the structure is stunning. Inside the design of the dome is exquisite!!!It is important to not that the Pantheon is a religious relic, so while visiting please keep that in mind.  The main alter and statuary throughout the structure are breathtaking !!
Well worth visiting here, its Beautiful inside and has an open top , which is suppose to be great to see when its raining , it all drains away so wont flood it
This is one of my favorite spots to go in Rome. It is over 2000 years old and still in use. It looks modest on the outside, but is very impressive on the inside.

Beautiful building - Impressive as this is one of the oldest temples on earth. Amazing architecture and fascinating history. It’s more like enjoyable with the audio guide but how fantastic that entry is free. Highly recommend.
Raphael the painter is buried here. Lovely building both inside and outside. Free entry. Fantastic how they managed to build the domed roof so long ago
It's free to get in and although there was a queue to get in it was very quick and very easy.  It's busy but should be on everyone's bucket list. Great that it's free from touts and blaggars.

The Pantheon (not to be confused with the Parthenon in Athens) is SO COOL. First of all, this huge, beautiful structure was constructed in like, 100 AD, so it is just really cool to walk through. It is also free, but be prepared to stand in a line that wraps around the whole square before you can walk in. It is really pretty inside and it is just nice to sit in there and take it all in.
We toured the Pantheon on our own as it was not part of the tour. So beautiful! Worth the effort we made to see it.

We were there 45 minutes before closing time. Perfect: less people and we strolled around and looked at the interior.  This is a phenomenon of architecture.  Highly recommended.  Please read the study before going to embrace your visit.  We really liked it.

A fantastic start to the four days we had in Rome. It was the first attraction we visited and it didn't disappoint. Due to it being relatively early in the morning it was easy to look around without too many other tourists. Amazing architecture\11

There is nothing I can say about this that you cannot read about.It is awe inspiring to stand in and just take a few moments to contemplate.Do keep in mind it is a church so modest clothing and keeping very quiet is essential.Read up on the history pre visit to get an insight into just why this site is so remarkable and significant.A visit during a rain shower is very much worth it to see the fall come in through the Oculus.
What is amazing is that all these ancient structures are still standing in great shape with modern Rome all around them.

Built originally as a Roman temple, it now serves as a church. The temple dates back to the second century and was built at the site of an earlier temple. The main building is circular and is topped by one of the largest domes of its time. In front of the entrance is a large portico.The dome is coffered on the inside and a large hole at its center lets in natural light. Inside, there are several altars distributed around its circumference. The fact that it is still standing is a testament to Roman engineering.This is one of the better preserved Roman monuments and is definitely worth a visit.
This was such an amazing building. It huge and for being so very old it is in very good shape. Another great historic building
I am sure this visit would have been enhanced with a guide but even without one, it was worth seeing.  It is a marvel of ancient building techniques as well as being very beautiful.

What a fantastic work of art and beauty!! Rain or shine it is a must see. We went on May 1, 2019 which happened to be Labour Day in Italy therefore the crowds were huge due to the main sights being closed that day, Vatican etc. the shopping and restaurants are amazing in the area as well. Also, no need to be worried about being pickpocketed just be smart and aware of what is going on around you when in crowds. You will not regret!
This site is a must see in Rome. It is centrally located and is quite well maintained. The view is worth the experience. Best suited for a leisure walk around lunch time.
Free entry and a must-see when in Rome - the middle of the building with the hole in the ceiling is epic! Great design and plenty to see inside, definitely worth a look.

This is an excellent attraction in Rome and one of the few places that does not charge a fee for entering it. I would recommend going here as early as possible as the queues later on in the day is quite horrendous.
Great place to go and visit would recommend to anyone in Rome it's a must see nice restaurants in area too

I booked a tour of the pantheon at the last minute.  It was a group of 3 of us and our guide took us around and gave us the history of the Pantheon.  It was a really nice tour which I recommend.  It tends to get very busy during the day so be prepared to wait in a line to get in.
A must see and also the resting place of Raphael (Painter, not the Ninja Turtle). Free entry, and when you walk in and look up to see the sky shining above you, it's simply breathtaking.
The history about the italian kings, queens and saint buried there was very interesting. Get a great photo of the dome by laying phone on floor under the oculus

This stunning historic building is beautifully preserved and a must-see in Rome. Take your time to walk around the circumference of the building to read about the history, and see the structure from all angles.

Loved visiting here today. . The architecture both inside and out, the beautiful artworks and sculptures were breathtaking and the whole vibe took you back in time. . The altars were magnificent. . When the announcement for silence came it felt so calm to have no sound and being left to your own thoughts . . Beautiful place and free to the public.
A definite must see.  All columns are original outside with exception to the right side.  The dome is incredible.
One thing after another, the sights, the sounds the smells. Ah, Roma! Truly the eternal city. If you haven't been, you must!
Been to Rome before but with so much to see, this has been bypassed, loved our time here today.  The long queue moves quickly. Men, remove your hat!

It's interesting for those who wonder how they perfected the circular building without modern technology.  The drainage of the water with an open centre of the dome.We counted some 12 types of marble in a small area!!

This structure is absolutely beautiful. We were able to go when the sun was shining bright in the afternoon which provided for a very beautiful experience inside with large open skylight in the center. The tour groups in here were massive but it was very easy to navigate within given how large the building is.
This one of many must see sights in Rome. The history and artisrty on view is mind blowing. Get used to the fact that everything will be busy
Go there. Even if the line is long it moves super fast. This is a must see. Although the roof is impressive, look at the floor as well.
The tomb of Raphael was my aim, but got so caught up in the architecture and history I soon digressed. It is free; that’s right... free. One of the most amazing construction wonders.

Wow, you can't picture just how enormous it is from photos. To think it was built with the available technology 2,000 years ago is staggering.Shame it has been bastardised into a church, so you don't get the feeling of the original inside, but well worth seeing anyway.

Unbelievable Enginering from 2,000 years ago! As you enter portico look up at the massive beams and the ironwork used to integrate the trusses—- masterwork when most of the world was using rocks for tools...
The architecture is amazing, considering the era it was built. Got lucky line was short, an amazing piece of history

The Pantheon is one of the Greatest buildings of history. Understanding it’s significance and history before you visit will make your time in this marvelous structure a highlight of any trip and a learning experience for kids the can refer to later in school and life.
Go early so you can enjoy crowd free. Was free thing to do while in Rome. Pretty neat how it’s dome is open.

Ok another iconic building but by the time U see this I think you may be frescoed and columned out.  Free entry which is worth taking advantage of as it's one of the few free things to see or do.  Again the line for entry is long but does move along well.
Really amazing building with rich history and artwork. Definitely worth spending money on a guide or audio guide to hear everything
This church is very interesting.  The lineup wasn't bad considering how crowded Rome overall seemed to be on the day we visited.  The architecture is pretty cool.

I came on Easter Sunday at 5pm. Free admission. Big crowds. What a beautiful temple. Everything I have learned in art history classes were true about the Pantheon: the Greek columns, portico, niches with statues, coffered ceiling, round ceiling opening, marble floor,... It is an incredible place.
After hearing and reading a great deal about the Pantheon it was in real life a little disappointing. A bit like any Great Cathedral in the UK.
This is a must see!  The architecture is incredible, the art work amazing It is easy to get to and is free to enter. Definitely put this on your list when in Rome.
Interesting building with a hole in the roof. Best of all it's free. One of your must see attractions in Rome.

When visiting Pantheon in the morning there was a exhibition so it was close until 12:30, we descided to go back later but if you are going with a group it could be worth asking them in advance how they do when this happens.When standing in line it went really fast since everyone goes basicly just around and then out again and there is no money beeing paid. Its important that you take off your hat and are silent in the building, if not you will be told.If you wish to get a good photo of the dome you will get this one just as you enter the building and go outside the building so dont hold the line, keep the line flowing. This building is one of the most preserved in rom and was during the acient time a place to worship the goods and later on became a memorytempel for important people in Rome.
It's definitely worth seeing but I wouldn't stand in line for an hour. Go early and beat the crowd. It's a magnificent piece of architecture.
Quite a Grimm looking building really, so much better to see in Rome, but nice bars to sit, but service very slow indeed
Very pretty church with an interesting history.  Different than the typical architecture as well.  Worth taking an hour to explore!
Neat site to see. Only spent about 19 minutes here and saw everything that I wanted to see. Would be neat to see when it is raining since the top appeared to open.

We were surprised there was no admission fee. The interior is amazing. First time we walked by was on Sunday and the line to enter was very long. We came back on Monday and we just walked in. The marble is a must see.
We actually did not go inside however we sat across the way and had pizza. The area is very busy and is very close to the Trevi fountain; another popular attraction
Crowded place but definitelly worth a visit. You do not need much time for visit of this plače, nice building, recommended
The engineering feat of this structure is amazing. Let alone the how long ago it was built. A must see in Rome.
Despite the floods of tourists, the Pantheon is a "do not miss" site in Rome.  If you are an early riser, go in the morning...

The Panteon isn almost 2.000 years old construction, still holding the record of the largest concrete dome (with no supporting pillars) in the world.  It is magnificent, an example of the roman engineering 2 thousand years ago.  It is now a catholic church and contains the remains of the worldwide famous painter Raphael.  Must see place.

If there wasn’t a Colosseum this would be the most famous building from Roman Empire times in Rome. You can’t miss this amazing building and read up on the architecture, engineering and historic value. Go early, go at night and go back again throughout your stay in Rome. It’s magical at night when no one is around, see the moon above the Pantheon and think, this building has been here and nearly unchanged for for almost 2,000 years.
Love this and it was FREE.  You really got a sense of the history of Rome in this spot.  I will want to visit this again on my next trip.  Highly recommend.

Although it was free to enter, they only allow a certain number in at once so never felt crowded.  Could take pictures and stand and stare without feeling rushed.  Organ playing inside which added to the atmosphere.Amazing building, the size of it was unbelievable.  Definitely not to be missed.Nice Plaza nearby for drinks and people watching.
The marble work alone is worth the effort.  Soak in a little history and see why Hadrian was one of the best emperors.

Went to the Pantheon during a morning walk while seeing other historical sites in the area. Arrived on a Sunday at around 10 AM and there was virtually no line for entry - based on other reviews, this is not the case in afternoons and/or evenings depending on the time of year. Lots of cafes nearby if you want to grab a quick breakfast as well.
The first dome in architecture, a tour de force in Hadrian's time and still wowing visitors. The bronze doors are original--that is amazing--1800 years old and still swinging.
I'll skip all the readily available information and just say if you can get to Rome in your lifetime this is one of the places you should not miss.

This is the second time I have seen the Pantheon. Why is it so special? It's still standing. It's 2,000 years old and it is still being used and as far as I can see, short of war, terrorism or an earthquake, it will still be standing in 2,000 years. The reason it did not turn into rubble is that though it was born as a Roman temple, Christians saw a good thing and decided to turn into a church. It is a marvelous piece of architecture and is beautiful inside with incredibly tasteful marble. I find the oculus, the hole in the ceiling, the most impressive achievement. It not only lets light into the building, keeps the heat from building up, but with superior engineering knowledge it does not allow water to pour in when it rains. And it's not a small opening, either. And there are some pretty statues, too. After you see this and the Colosseum, you realize more than any book can convey how remarkable Roman civilization was. It's fair to say that it was the most successful civilization in the history of man and laid the groundwork for the advancement of all mankind from then on.I typically spend 10-15 minutes here, but some day I will stay longer. It deserves it.
Great plaza and beautiful architecture and history-- if you're in Rome, then this is a must stop for you to see.
I will admit this wasn’t at the top of our list but The Pantheon blew us away. The magnificent entrance gives way to an even more magnificent interiorMake it the top of your list
It is of course a must see for everyone who is visiting Rome. I would recommend to go either early morning or later on in the evening as it is a lot more peaceful at those times

What a fabulous building!Queue looked long but was only a few mins to get in. No free information inside so a good bit of knowledge before you go would make the experience very rewarding. This is free to enter.Highly recommended from us.

Our tour group went inside briefly and I was unable to hear some of the history, however to understand the Pantheon's antiquity and change from Pagan Roman God worship to a Christian faith center is marvelous to consider and behold.  Do your research beforehand to understand the construction and Hadrian's reign.  Must see!

The Pantheon is definately my favourite place in Rome. The building is such a beautiful I could stand there and watch it all day long. Great and majestic. Worth to visit and discover it from the inside.

The Pantheon is an ancient temple now used as a church.  It was right beside our hotel and would have been easy to access with a wheelchair, if not for cobbled streets, which are, sadly, ubiquitous in Rome.  Once inside, it is very impressive.
Amazing history and remarkable engineering feat. A must see.  Better with a guide as is the case with visiting most sites in Rome. Guides bring Italy to life.

I been there at least 10 times before and still am fascinated by the place. the only issue is that the line to get in may be long and getting hold of one of the narrating machines in your language may be a challenge. Will recommend a guided tour.

I’m not sure I knew what to expect here. We were wandering around town and decided to stop in. Before going inside, we grabbed gelato and just enjoyed the views and architecture. It was kind of weird....here’s this ancient structure surrounded by homes, shops, and restaurants. Maybe I thought it would be a bit more stand-alone?Anyway. When you go in, look up. Before you cross the doors. Seriously. It is pretty amazing use of the beams and how they reinforced everything. Inside, be quiet. It is still a holy place, and you can sit to reflect/pray. Check out the middle of the floor. When it rains, there are 22 drains in the middle of the floor. Spend some time looking at things. There are some translators available, but if you download google translate, you can read what everything says.

When the internet claims the Pantheon is "the most complete Roman structure on earth, having survived 20 centuries of plunder, pillage, and invasion," you know you have to find this treasure of Rome. An easy walk from other nearby sites, this huge building emerges from the surrounding neighborhood in all of its glory. Step inside to the repurposed Christian church (the secret to its preservation). Look up to the ceiling that has allowed sunlight and rain to enter for centuries. Not to be missed!
We had an impromptu tour from an English guide and had a fabulous time, highly recoomended.You would not imagine the history and the charm of this building, not to be missed.
Rome is an amazing city to visit. There is history on every street and the Pantheon is incredible architecture. The piazza is great to sit and watch the world go by!

the Pantheon, no matter how many times I visit it (4 times in this week long stay, alone, and this isn't my first trip to Rome!) is simply awe inspiring.  Its age and significance is beyond compare.  I love watching the light from the oculus move around the dome as the day progresses and the Pantheon at night is another experience altogether.  Make time to see this wonder more than once if you can.
Outstanding architecture, very quiet and peaceful although very busy with tourists. A must see attraction!
This is a site to be seen, outside and inside. Just amazing. And the square it’s in is such a lovely place to eat.
The Pantheon is well worth visiting, both for the architecture and the history.  If possible, go during the rain, so you can see the rain come in through the center of the ceiling.
Compared to others touristic places,the Pantheon visit is very fluid.Line from outside is manageable.

The Pantheon of Rome, the giant temple built from the orders of the Emperor Hadrian and finished in 126 BC, was a symbol of Emperor's empire and force .The Romans built the Pantheon nearly 2,000 years ago, so that the sun's rays illuminate the entrance exactly when the emperor passed the temple during the day that marks the echinoctium in March and September, and on April 21 the day when the Romanians celebrated the day the city, the date when Rome was founded. The edifice is loaded with mysteries. The legend says that it was built in such a way that when it rains or snows, water does not enter through the opening of the ceiling. It's a magnificent building !
We were approached by James to take a Tour, it was worth every penny, to learn the fascinating history of this iconic building

I have been to Rome many times, both on trips and for university. In addition to the trevi fountain, this is also one of the mandatory ones. The structure is majestic and beautiful, plus with the guide you can learn about the history from the basics to the present day. AMAZING!! To see , highly recommended the guide

Good to visit, if you are in the locality. I went in the afternoon, by then, it was soooo crowded, I couldn't go around much. There are other incredible churches in Rome, so you can skip this one, if you are time constrained.

I always make it a point to stop by the Pantheon every time I’m in Rome.  It’s just amazing that this building has been around since Jesus walked the streets of Rome.  How fitting that a once pagan temple has been converted to and used as a church.  It’s a definite stop for any Rome visit.  Spend time when you’re here to take in some Amazing History!!

a major site in Rome that you should see, and is made more interesting and impressive if you have some interpretation of it....otherwise it just looks like a big old building.  Download the 15 minute Rick Steves audio guide and you will get why it is so special.  You can start by just checking out the 16 columns outside that are made of a single piece of stone...and brought from Egypt.  Pretty amazing.
Great place to visit, unfortunately too crowded. Amazing feet of construction considering its age. You could see it many times and discover new sites.
The rotundra is closed at night and whilst the restaurants are a little expensive the stetting provides a wonderful setting.

Visit My City Tour, Rome and get David to take you on one of his special tours around this incredible, magical, magnificent & mysterious building.  You need somebody like him to explain it properly, and to show you the tiny details which make it so amazing!

This free event is top notch. The building is amazing and is rich in history. Make sure you either get a guided tour or audio tour. Considered downloading Rick Steves free audio your if you can get it. No reservations are required.

It's so much fun threading thru the streets looking for the Pantheon then all of sudden you walk into a plaza and there it is.....There was a huge line and we almost took a pass on going inside but soon realized that even though the line was long it moves fast so don't let the crowds keep you from missing out on an wonderful building.
The architecture and history of the ages just vibrates with energy in all of Rome. Th sites all around the city are beautiful.
Really amazing architecture. There is a possibility to take an audioguide. You can take photos there. It’s great that the entrance is free.
Great during the day but one of the best lighting of all Roman buildings at night. A nice place to spend time
Just like most tourist sites in Rome, get there early to beat the crowds.  Plus, be wary of any strangers approaching with "gifts".
One of the historical sights in Rome that cannot be missed. Can do by self. Genreally crowded during peak season.
It great building but once again you should know about its religious and historical facts otherwise your visit will be just following the tourists !
Medieval Building with classic antiques. Must have grand tour of the city. Very rich Comunity in the world.
I really had only seen images of the outside of the Pantheon so didn't realize how amazing it was on the inside.  I'm so glad we took the time to visit.

We visited the Pantheon on a Sunday morning knowing that there was a 10:30 Mass. What we didn't realize was that before Mass began they asked anyone not participating in Mass to leave and closed the Pantheon to visitors for about 75 minutes. They were very strict about tourists trying to sneak in for pictures etc. during this time. So if you are not interested in participating in the Mass, don't visit at this time. If you do, this is absolutely the best time to experience the grandeur of the Pantheon. Following Mass, as they put away the Blessed Sacrament and sacred vessels, you are free to wander and take pictures. We had an incredible experience there so if you are Catholic and visiting Rome, I would highly recommend it!

What can you say, when you stand in the middle of history and contemplate all the people who have come before, and will come after.  What a place to think about history, and how we got to where we are today.
Not crowded,  really nice visit, beautiful Basilica.  I got in trouble for leaning on the marble exterior of the building.... so don't do that!
Amazing building in the middle of shops bars etc, Roof and wall paintings wonderful. Well worth a visit

Not only is the building spectacular but the piazza makes it a destination for lunch or dinner. So don't just pass through and check off the building on your list, stay a bit and enjoy the experience.
One of the true ancient building in Italy.. Amazing architecture as well as, historic artifacts contained inside... a must see on any trip to Rome
Can get very busy, and some visitors want to take photos at the most awkward moments.  Important contribution to the understanding of Roman culture, the art work inside is stunning.
One the must see attractions in Rome. Free entry and not too busy. The architecture is superb.  The open roof is fascinating.
Our recent visit was on a Sunday -- I do not recommend visiting on Sunday.It is beautiful. Do a few minutes research before you arrive so you know what you're looking at.Don't miss this site.

Am in love with Rome & all it delivers ...it’s a tad dirty in places, lots of loose garbage doesn’t seem to have bigger litter bins....all that being said Roma worms it’s way into your heart...I tigress..The Panthanon..wowsers! Unbelievable mathematical built structure..go see for yourself...they would not be able to achieve this today....

and still faring very well.  The light source at the top is beautiful.  Download an audio tour to your phone prior to going and you'll get all kinds of juicy tidbits about this place.  For instance, Margherita pizza is named for Queen Margherita who is entombed here.

Pantheon is one of the most beautiful places of the world. The atmosphere of the building is legendary: grandiose and modest in ont time. It is worth to see but very crowded.

This was my daughter’s and son-in-law’s favorite site in Rome. The domed roof is an architectural wonder considering the time-frame of its construction. The massive columns in the front of the building were originally brought from Egypt in one piece each weighing over 55 tons. The inside is massive with varying shades of marble covering walls and floors as well as lots of gilt to adorn the altar and other relics. A beautifully crafted building. There are massive crowds and queues but the carabinieri keep the lines moving.

The Pantheon is an amazing feat of engineering. To see the way it is built and how it is still in such great condition was breathtaking. Since it is a church, be ready to be very quiet while observing all aspects of the inside. The hole in the roof and the floors drains were so unique! We loved the architecture and actually standing in it.

Super-crowded on a Sunday.  Really just one room to view.  Exterior views are more exciting than the interior.  Make sure you walk behind Pantheon for some neat views and camera angles.  We didn't visit at night but wish we had.
Really loved this building , its fabulous architecture and interior and one of the few places you can visit and actually enter free of charge .

Enjoy the silence this building gives you when you are inside. Forget the tour guides, audio guides and other noice, this building gives you tranquility if you allow yourself simply to enjoy the building and hear how it speaks to you. Must see in Roma
Well worth a visit, magnificent inside with loads of history. One of the free places to visit. Queue's, but they move reasonably fadt
Have been to Romeseveral times but have always missed the opportunity to visit it the   PantheonInteresting building and well worth a look see.

The Pantheon is a beautiful monumental building. Once again we admired the height and beauty of this building. Pantheon (pan - all + theos - divine) "all divine '. The Pantheon was originally built as a temple dedicated to all the gods of Old Rome. Like everything in Rome and this building has a rich history, and it's nice to know it. The temle was built by Marko Vipsani Agrippa on the 27th and 25th centuries, before. Cr. Around 126 years, the reign of Hadrian, the temple was rebuilt. Pope Boniface VIII in the year 608 is transformed the Pantheon into a church with the name of St. Mary of the Martyrs. The Pantheon is a circular shape. At the entrance there are three rows of huge Corinthian columns. The columns along with the beautiful entrance doors give the monumental value to this temple. A concrete dome with a beautiful design takes breath away and creates a beautiful atmosphere within the temple. It has an opening on top, an ocular, or a big eye through which light comes in and serves as a ventilation. Since the time of the Renaissance this Pantheon is used as a tomb. In the Pantheon are buried the painters Raphael and Annibale Carracci, the composer Arcangelo Corelli, and the architect Baldassare Peruzzi and two kings, Vittorio Emanuele II and Umberto I. Admission is free and it is certainly worth a visit.

It is one of the ancient landmarks which you just have to visit while spending time in rome. The uniqe arichtecture, with the dome, history and the fact that is build almost 2000 years ago is unbeliveble. I would recommend that you read a little about the history and architectural design before you visitt. Take your time while visiting. Recommended for families,  couples and friends.
Absolutely mind blowing. Set in beautiful piazza. Take time to enjoy the building from the outside before going in. Look up!
We visited the Pantheon as part of our walking tour, our guide was good and explained the Pantheon's significance. Not to be missed on your visit to Rome.
All I can say is Wow!! The artwork in here is amazing. I was completely overwhelmed and blown away by it all.

it is always nice to see the place, its fascinating, definitely recommended.  you must wear comfortable shoes, and bring (the guide person has sunscreen and offered free which was very nice) sunscreen. it will require long hour (2-3) walking on paved  and gravel road.  the guide woman was professional in history and had very extensive knowledge about the site, very enjoyed overall experience

This is one of my favourite places in Roma. It is hard to believe that it was build over 2000 years ago. Beautiful and amazing! Inside the Pantheon, there is Raffaello's grave.If you read  ' demons and angels'  before visiting Roma, this place must be much more interesting!  You must visit Pantheon  once in a lifetime.

I love how these gorgeous pieces of history have been preserved, cared for and are available to us to enjoy its beauty. The Pantheon's dome is still the world's largest reinforced concrete dome inspite of it being nearly two thousand years old. One of the things that i wanted to see & wasn´t disappointed. Go in daytime to see inside, as it closes in the evening.

It rained the entire time that we were in Rome and specifically while we were in the pantheon. Saw the drainage system for the water falling through a giant opening in the copular type roof. The Pantheon is massive with simplistic decor. It has been stripped off it’s Pagan decorations years ago and presently serves as a simple church. It currently contains a picture of the Virgin Mary and Child that was supposedly smuggled out of Turkey during the period of Christian persecutions. Francesco, our guide was wonderful. He showed us the architectural genius that was utilized in constructing the Pantheon. Leo was our driver who drove us safely through the crowded streets and made many stops for us to take pictures. There is one simple rule that Leo has and that is that you cannot eat in his car. As a result his car is clean and free from stale food odors. Franchesco (guide) and Leo (driver) were both referred  to us by the Limosine service of Naples- Alex.

This is a beautiful building.  The amazing fact is that most of the building, including the floor is original construction going back almost 2000 years. The piazza also has an interesting feature with a fountain and obelisk, which themselves date back to the 16th and 18th century.  You will also find the horse and carriage operators here in the piazza and there was a quiet relaxed feel to the piazza and inside the Pantheon.

Agrippa was one of the great generals of the Roman Empire. A loyal soldier and right hand of Augustus for whom he built it, though completed at the time of Hadrian. A great Roman architecture and engineering. But its purpose and beauty now corrupted by catholic rituals. Best to see it very early in the morning before the hoard of tourists.

Visited here on 26th April 2019, luckily for us our hotel was next to it, literally 50ft away.As soon as the doors opened we were in. What surprises me most is on the outside the entrance is dramatic with its imposing pillars etc. but once inside the marble floor and decoration around the building are stunning. It did take me by surprise how nice the building is inside compared to the exterior.Entry is free and the doors open at 08:30 you can stay inside as long as you like but my wife and I were only in for about 40mins.I saw later that same day the queue stretched around the square and the wait was quite long, but you can get there early or book a tour online etc.I am no history buff or lover of architecture but I am glad I went inside this building, its worth an half hour of anyone's time

We visited Pantheon last week. It is free to get in but there is a line which moves fairly quickly. Once inside I went to the information desk to rent the audio guide. You need to give them your ID as a security deposit and get it back when you return the device. I used my passport card because no way I leave my passport at those places. I am not sure  if they accept driver license but won't hurt to try. The place is very beautiful and you don't feel crowded as at other attractions in Definitely a must see place in Rome.
The Patheon only takes an hour to visit... so it's a must.  It's the largest solid cement dome ceiling in the world.  Also still used as a church.

this building is over 1900 years old. Original built by Hadrian but he dedicated to Marcus Agrippa who built the first one and died approx 100 years before. building is beautiful and the dome and oculus (opening) amzing.

Amazing architectural feat on a very nice square in central Rome. The most greek looking building you’ll find in Rome. This building is not as richly decorated as some other churches (no elaborate frescoes) but it has a pared down beauty all of its own. The cuppola is amazingly open so you look straight at the sky. Plenty of nice restaurants, cafes and gelaterias nearby.
Great place to visit. Had a queue but the queue moves very quickly, very interesting. Well worth a visit.

The Pantheon is situated right in the middle of the old city. Squeezed between tight streets is a church different from the others in the Gothic style. Imposing and beautiful, I would say. It's easy to get there... Just a walk from de Fontana Di Trevi.
This is a very beautiful place in Rome.  We went in the evening and it was very crowded so maybe try going earlier in the day.
